title: B PHARMACY
duration: Course Duration of Bachelor of Pharmacy [B.Pharm] is 4 Years.
eligibility: WANT TO TAKE ADMISSION IN THIS COURSE OF THE BEST COLLEGE BUT HOW?
details:
B- PHARMACY



WHAT IS B.COM PHARMACY??



Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharmacy) is a four-year undergraduate programme that focuses on drug testing and research for a variety of diseases and disorders. 

Pharmacist, Pharmacy Technician, Retail Pharmacist, Pharmacy Manager, and Health Inspector are just a few of the employment available to B.Pharmacy graduates.







The course covers all aspects of medical care, including biochemistry, drug readiness, and the quantity of drugs that should be recommended to patients for proper treatment.





OVERVIEW OF THE COURSE







Degree

Bachelors

Full Form

Bachelor of Pharmacy

Duration

Course Duration of Bachelor of Pharmacy [B.Pharm] is 4 Years.

Age

Minimum age limit is 17 years

Minimum Percentage

50% in 10+2 from a recognised board

Subjects Required

Should have studies Physics, Chemistry and Biology in 10+2

Average Fees Incurred

INR 4 - 10 LPA

Average Salary Offered

INR 2 - 5 LPA







WHY SHOULD YOU OPT FOR B PHARMACY

 

There are numerous compelling reasons why you should seek a bachelor's degree in pharmacy. Apart from the fact that B Pharmacy will prepare you for a profession as a Medical Representative, Scientific Writing, Medical Scripting, Clinical Research, and so on, we will go over the various benefits of doing this degree in depth.



Government Jobs:You might work for a range of government organisations and hospitals after receiving your bachelor's degree in pharmacy.

Working with Patients: You will play a significant part in the health-care system by providing asthma care, cholesterol testing, diabetic disease management, and bone density testing, among other services.

Various Career Options: Working at a retail pharmacy, whether in a healthcare facility or in the community, is the most apparent option for pharmacists. The job include offering people over-the-counter and prescription drugs, as well as counselling.



FIELDS UNDER THIS COURSE



There are various fields in B Pharmacy. Some of them are as follows

Pharmaceutical Chemistry

Pharmaceutical Technology

Pharmaceutical Analysis and Quality Assurance

Clinical Pharmacy

Pharmaceutics

Pharmacy Practice

Pharmacognosy

Pharmacology

Pharmaceutical Marketing Management

Quality Assurance

Phytopharmaceuticals and Natural products

Drug Discovery and Development

Medicinal Chemistry

Bulk Drugs

Drug Regulatory Affairs

Biopharmaceutics

Industrial Pharmacy



CAREER OPPORTUNITIES AFTER GRADUATING B-PHARMACY:-



Job Profiles

Job Description

medical writer

The main responsibility of a medical writer is to interact with doctors and keep track of all medical results, product usage, and medical data.

Clinical Researcher

A clinical researcher collects and analyses information gathered via thorough testing and research.

Drug Inspector

A Drugs Inspector is in charge of overseeing the quality of a drug and issuing licences to other pharmacists.

Medical Representative

They are in charge of marketing numerous drugs for the firm for whom they work. Their work is goal-oriented.

Pharmacy Business

After passing through all of the ethical and legal requirements set out by the monitoring agencies, the main employment responsibility will be to distribute and dispense medications in either retail or wholesale, regionally or across India, while ensuring that safe and proper medicines are delivered to the public.

Hospital Pharmacist

A hospital pharmacist is in charge of medicine dispensing and pharmacy maintenance. Hospital pharmacists are in charge of inventory management and medicine delivery in compliance with physician orders.







WHAT WE CAN DO IN FURTHER/HIGHER AFTER STUDYING THIS COURSE:-

M Pharm Quality Assurance

Pharm.D (Post Baccalaureate)

Master of Pharmacy [M.Pharm] (Pharmaceutics)

Master of Pharmacy [M.Pharm] (Pharmacology)

Ph.D. (Pharmacology)

Master of Business Administration [MBA] (Pharmaceutical Management)

Master of Pharmacy [M.Pharm]

Master of Science [M.Sc] (Pharmaceutical Chemistry)

M.Phil [Pharmaceutics]















WHAT ARE THE ENTRY-LEVEL SALARY PACKAGES & AVERAGE SALARY PACKAGES FOR B PHARMACY?





Job Profiles

Average Salary (INR)

Medical Writer

4 - 6 LPA

Clinical Researcher

5 LPA

Drug Inspector

8 - 14 LPA

Medical Representative

3 - 6 LPA

Pharmacy Business

7 - 10 LPA













WANT TO TAKE ADMISSION IN THIS COURSE OF THE BEST COLLEGE BUT HOW?





TOP ENTRANCE EXAM FOR B-PHARMACY ARE-

Entrance Exam

Application Dates

About

NEET UG

July 2022

NEET is also the sole test that is accepted for admission to institutes like JIPMER, AIIMS, PGIMER, and others.

PUCET

July 2022

TOP ENTRANCE EXAM FOR B-PHARMACY ARE-

Entrance Exam

Application Dates

About

NEET UG

July 2022

NEET stands for National Eligibility and Entrance Test, which is an offline test that is used to provide admission to MBBS, BDS, AYUSH, Nursing, and other programmes. Under the 15 percent AIQ and 85 percent State level quota seats, all government and private medical institutions will grant admission. NEET is also the sole test that is accepted for admission to institutes like JIPMER, AIIMS, PGIMER, and others.

PUCET

July 2022

Punjab University Common Entrance Test (PU CET) is a state-level entrance test held by Punjab University, Chandigarh for students seeking admission to various undergraduate and graduate programmes. Undergraduate courses are tested in PU CET UG, whereas postgraduate courses are tested in PU CET PG.

MHT-CET

February 10, 2022 – March 31, 2022

The Maharashtra Common Entrance Test is a state-wide test for admission to B.E., B.Tech, and B.Pharm degrees given by government and private universities throughout the state. MHT CET is held once a year, and applicants from different Indian states are welcome to take part.

BITSAT



BITS Pilani administers the Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test, a national-level admission exam. Every year, BITSAT is held to give admission to B.E., B.Pharm, M.Sc., and other courses at BITS campuses. Every year, around 3 lakh applicants take the BITSAT.

title: BSc General . docx
description: The full form of BSc is Bachelor of Science. A bachelor's degree in science (BSc) is a three-year undergraduate programme that covers courses such as physics, chemistry, mathematics, biology, and computer science.
details:
BACHELOR OF SCIENCE

What is this course?

The full form of BSc is Bachelor of Science. A bachelor's degree in science (BSc) is a three-year undergraduate programme that covers courses such as physics, chemistry, mathematics, biology, and computer science.



The BSc programme is regarded as a credible option among the degree options available following 12th-grade science. 

It provides a diverse range of disciplines and speciality courses that provide a strong foundation in science and hence open up a variety of alternatives for students who desire to continue a professional job or post-graduate studies after completing their BSc.







Overview of BSc Course-

BSc Full Form

Bachelor of Science

BSc Duration

3 years

BSc Eligibility

Class 12th in Science stream with 50% marks

BSc Admission

Through Merit Lists and Entrance Exams

BSc Fees

INR 20,000-2,00,000

BSc Colleges



Delhi University, Madras Christian College, Christ University, Stella Maris College, Presidency University, etc.



BSc Entrance Exams

IISER, UPSEE, KCET, etc.



Difference between  B.Sc vs B.Sc Hons.-



Parameters of comparison

BSc (General)

BSc (Hons.)

Definition

A degree that focuses on developing a greater understanding of Science rather than focusing on a single area.

A degree that focuses solely on one specific field of science and specializes in that paper.

Demand

Is less in demand in the academic sphere.

Is in higher demand in the academic sphere.



Scope in future

Has a narrower scope than a B.Sc. Honors degree.

Has a broader range than a B.Sc (general).

Subjects of Focus

All of the disciplines in the degree are equally important.

One specific subject (i.e. math, bio, botany, zoology, chemistry, physics, or economics) is of primary focus.



Value

The concentration is on a single subject (mathematics, biology, botany, zoology, chemistry, physics, or economics).

Traditionally, the degree has a lower worth than a B.Sc Honors degree.

The degree is more valuable than a B.Sc (general).



Fields of BSc-



Here is the list of some popular fields of BSc courses-



Chemistry

Genetics

Biochemistry

Geology

Biotechnology

Physical therapy

Microbiology

Horticulture

Zoology

Bioinformatics

Computer Science

Anthropology

Botany

Mechanics

Environmental 

science

Molecular biology

Forensic science

Numerical analysis

Bachelor of Science in Agriculture

Radiology

Forestry

Aviation

Psychology

Optics

Occupational therapy

Pathology

Physiotherapy

Speech Therapy

Nursing

Health Science and Nutrition

Forensic Science

Sport Science

Agriculture

Audiology



Why should you study BSc-

Scholarships that are appealing-



Bright students interested in BSc studies can apply for government-funded scholarships. 

The advantages of these scholarships include attractive offers such as paying all of the student's study expenditures while completing the degree. 

Some of these scholarships also cover MSc expenditures if the student chooses to pursue it as a form of higher education.



Possibilities for Employment in the Research and Development Fields-



The finest aspect of earning a BSc is the numerous job options in the field of research and development. 

One of the key motivations for the government's generous scholarship programme for BSc graduates is to strengthen India's R&D sector. What better profession for a science student to pursue than that of a scientist?



Education after BSc-



Most B.Sc. students want to continue their education to pursue a job in science. You can pursue a master's degree or even a PhD after earning a B.Sc. 

As in science, the more you learn, the more specialized your education route gets, allowing you to concentrate on a particular area of competence. 

Most B.Sc. students would rather study more and pursue a specialized career that would provide them with a good salary, success, and job satisfaction.

Master of Comput

title: BSC MICROBIOLOGY
description: BSc Microbiology courses are in high demand due to the increasing complexity of diseases caused by microbes in recent years, with the global pandemic being one of the most recent examples.
details:
BSC MICROBIOLOGY





What is this course?



BSc Microbiology courses are in high demand due to the increasing complexity of diseases caused by microbes in recent years, with the global pandemic being one of the most recent examples. 

The core BSc Microbiology course covers topics like mycology, industrial microbiology, and biostatistics, among others, preparing students with the abilities needed to work in the business.



Highlights of the course-



Full Form

Bachelor of Science in Microbiology

Duration 

3 years

Eligibility

Class 12th or equivalent with at least 55-60% marks/entrance tests with minimum needed scores



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Research Assistant

Food, Industrial or Environmental Microbiologists

Quality Assurance Technologists

Sales or Technical Representative

Clinical and Veterinary Microbiologists

Medical Technologists

Biomedical Scientist

Clinical Research Associate





Why should you study this Course?



The advantages that the programme provides, BSc Microbiology is in high demand. Candidates can work in a variety of research labs, pharmaceutical businesses, food processing industries, and other settings after completing the course.



The following are some of the advantages having a BSc in Microbiology:

A graduate degree in microbiology can lead to professional opportunities for candidates in a variety of fields, including bacteriologists, virologists, cell biologists, mycologists, and others.

Candidates can pursue higher education by enrolling in MSc programmes such as MSc Microbiology, MSc in Applied Microbiology, MSc Medical Microbiology, and MSc Microbial Genetics and Bioinformatics after completing the course. Candidates with a master's degree have a better chance of landing a job.

Opportunities for research: The majority of microorganisms rapidly change their genomic architecture. This opens up new research possibilities. Microbiologists are constantly studying microbes in order to better understand the next wave of infectious diseases, the influence of bacteria on the environment, the impact of microbes on food products, and many other topics.

A microbiologist in India gets roughly INR 310,000 per year, which is a competitive salary. A microbiologist with ten years of experience can earn up to INR 600,000 per year.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Position

Job Profile

Bacteriologist

A Bacteriologist's work is to research various types of bacteria that are continually changing and evolving. Their primary responsibility is to conduct laboratory research for the government or pharmaceutical businesses.

Virologist

A virologist is a scientist who researches the growth, development, structure, and features of viruses. His primary responsibility is to isolate and culture important viruses in a standard inhibitory medium while controlling environmental conditions such as aeration, moisture, temperature, and nutrition.

Mycologist

A mycologist is a scientist who studies fungi, which are organisms that are separate from animals, algae, plants, and bacteria. Mycologists look for organisms that are useful in medicine by studying the organism-based processes of poisonous, edible, and parasitic fungus.

Cell biologist

Cell scientists used to investigate and analyze the properties, relationships, and structure of a cell's molecules in order to derive basic principles or to learn about the impact of cell behavior on a specific organism.

Biochemist

Biochemists study the chemical processes involved in all of life's basic functions and work with the chemical composition of living organisms. These professionals will be able to supply us with much of the basic knowledge on themes like genetics and heredity through research, experimentation, and study.







What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



After earning a B.Sc. you'll have a plethora of work opportunities and professional possibilities.Microbiology programme. Students can pick from a variety of careers based on their talents and interests. Following are some of the most popular career options after completing a B.Sc. Microbiology programme:



M.Sc. Microbiology: Most students pursue M.Sc. Microbiology after completing their B.Sc. Microbiology. M.Sc. Microbiology with a specialization of your choice is an option. It is always suggested to continue your education after completing this programme, as it will not be as beneficial otherwise. The typical cost of this training is between INR 50,000 and INR 3 lakhs.

M.Sc. in Applied Microbiology: Candidates who have completed a B.Sc. in Microbiology can enroll in this programme, which is a speciality of microbiology.The typical cost of an MSc in Applied Microbiology programme is between INR 2 and 5 lakhs.

course graduates?



Job Position

Average Annual Salary

Bacteriologist

INR 2,91,000

Virologist

INR 10,00,000

Mycologist

INR 6,00,

title: BSC ZOOLOGY
description: Zoology, or Animal Biology, is a discipline of biology that studies living and extinct animals, as well as their interactions with their environments.
details:
BSC ZOOLOGY





What is this course?



Zoology, or Animal Biology, is a discipline of biology that studies living and extinct animals, as well as their interactions with their environments.

It is well-structured, with theory-based papers and practical sessions to provide students a hands-on experience and make scientific concepts easier to understand.

Animal Physiology, Molecular Biology, Biochemistry, Developmental Biology, Biotechnology, Functional Histology, Ecology, Immunology, Genetics & Genomics, Bioinformatics, Environmental Management, Biodiversity: Non-Chordata, Biodiversity: Chordata, Evolutionary Biology, and Cell Biology are among the topics covered in the course.

Animal Biotechnology, Biodiversity Studies, Environmental Management, Ecosystem Monitoring, and Animal Bioinformatics are just a few of the growing fields of biology that this course prepares students to explore. It also emphasizes the development of students' practical scientific knowledge.

The course's curriculum is tailored to meet the contemporary needs of B.Sc (Hons.) Zoology students. The contents were created to acquaint pupils with the expanding horizons of biological science.



Highlights of the course-



Course level

Undergraduate

Full Form

Bachelors of Science in Zoology

Duration of the Course

3 years

Eligibility

A candidate must have received at least a 50 percent aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology in Class XII.



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Environmental Consultant

Research Scientist

Marine Biologist

Lecturer/Professor

Conservation Officer

Animal Behaviorist.

Zoo Officer

Ecologist







Why should you study this Course?

In recent years, BSc Zoology has become one of India's most sought-after degrees in terms of personal development and job advancement chances. There are numerous advantages to earning a BSc in Zoology. The following are a few of the:



The course offers a variety of career advancement prospects and encourages the expansion of existing expertise.

Animal Physiology, Biochemistry, Genetics & Genomics, Biodiversity, and other elements of the subject are covered in depth in this course.

The course pays handsomely, with salaries ranging from 4 lakhs to 7 lakhs per annum.

The course provides students with a variety of professional options. Students can either work in the field or pursue higher education in the field.



Career opportunities after this Course-





Job Profile

Job Description

Zookeeper

The animals in the zoo are cared after by a zookeeper. He ensures that all of the rules are adhered to by the visitors. He is also in charge of feeding the animals at the zoo.

Researcher

A researcher performs significant research in areas such as animal genetics, animal reproduction, and animal behavior, among others.

Lab technician

A lab technician works in labs that are well-kept and well-equipped. He aids researchers and scientists in their scientific endeavors.

Teacher

A teacher's job is to provide students with accurate and useful information. He/she gives lectures as a kind of instruction. In order to effectively assess students, a teacher also gives regular tests and assignments.

Nature conservation officer

A nature conservation officer is in charge of protecting endangered plant and animal species as well as their ecosystems.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



The degree of BSc Zoology has grown in popularity among students in recent years. Students are eager to learn about various parts of biology. The course's ever-expanding breadth can be attributed to this alone. Students may have the following options in addition to looking for jobs:





Master of Science (MSc): Students who want to expand their skill set can pursue more study in the discipline. Students can pursue a Master of Science in Zoology, Applied Zoology, Life Science, and other related fields.



Students who desire to specialize in a discipline can pursue a postgraduate diploma in genetics, biomedical science, marine biology, ecology and evolutionary biology, animal behavior and neurobiology, and so on.



Students interested in business can get a Master of Business Administration in Biotechnology, Pharma Management, Dairy Management, Clinical Research, or Hospital Administration. This not only broadens their skill set but also allows them to pursue a variety of high-paying work opportunities.



Students can also pursue a master's degree in communication to further their education and develop a career in the media sector. Zoologists are always in demand for research and documentaries. They can train for careers on the National Geographic Channel, Animal Planet, Discovery Channel, or other similar channels.

title: BSc Computer Science
details:
BSc COMPUTER SCIENCE 

What is BSc Computer Science?

B.Sc. Computer Science is an abbreviation for Bachelor of Science in Computer Science (B.Sc. CS). A bachelor's degree in computer science takes three years to complete. 

Theoretical computer science, programming languages, database systems, networking, software engineering, and artificial intelligence are all topics covered in computer science.

 Students who get a B.Sc. in Computer Science will have a variety of job options, including being an IT project management, DTP operator, hardware, software, or network engineer. It is concerned with the study of computers and their applications in a systematic and logical manner.

 Graduates of the B.Sc. CS degree can select between core occupations (programming, testing) and non-core professions (BPO, non-technical).

 

Overview/Highlights of this Course-

 

Key Highlights of BSc Computer Science

BSc Computer Science Full-Form

Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

BSc Computer Science Duration

3 Years

BSc Computer Science Eligibility

50% or above in the science stream

BSc Computer Science Admission Mode

Merit-Based/Entrance Based (In some cases)

BSc Computer Science Average Fee

INR 3-7 Lakhs 

BSc Computer Science Syllabus

Introduction to Data structures, system programming, introduction to web technology, mobile applications development, python programming, and many more.

BSc Computer Science Job Profiles

Software Engineer, Software Developer, Systems Architect, Web developer, Mobile App developer, Website Designing, Network Engineer, Data Analyst, etc.



 

Streams/Fields of the BSc Computer Science-

Software Engineer

Network Engineer

Information Management and Data Analytics

Bioinformatics

Mobile and Web Computing

Computer and Network Security

Artificial Intelligence



Why should you study BSc Computer Science?

The Indian IT sector is predicted to develop rapidly in the future years, contributing ten percent of the country's GDP. As a result, the spectrum of career prospects in the IT sector expands.

Computer science graduates can work for a variety of firms, including computer hardware companies, computer networking companies, software development companies, academic institutions, and more.

Graduates can also help to make the world a safer place by working as security engineers, who safeguard users from hackers and other bad software.

Because the topic of study is always changing, the applicants would also be open to research.

Career opportunities after graduating BSc Computer Science:-

Game Developer: A game developer's job is creating video games for computers and mobile devices. Game developers build code and put the game's features into action. They're also in charge of the graphic material for the games. Zebu Games and 99Games are two game development businesses.



IT Consultant: IT Consultants assist businesses in meeting their objectives by analysing their IT systems, such as IT infrastructure and technological solutions.



Web Designer: A Web Designer's job is to use coding and programming to create a website's layout. The web designer also handles technical and graphic design tasks.



System Analyst: A system analyst is a person who works with computers to solve problems. They also set up software and hardware, as well as adding applications to improve the system's efficiency.



Database Administrator: Database Administrators are in charge of database maintenance, performance monitoring, data recovery, data storage, and data organisation.



Lecturer: A Computer Science lecturer's job is to offer students information on hardware, software, and networking. Students can work as teachers at colleges, universities, and institutions.



Network Engineer: The network engineer's job is to design and maintain the organisation's network infrastructure. They also examine the setups of routers and switches to see if they are working properly. Network engineers are needed by companies such as Ericsson, Huawei, and Airtel.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course:-

MS/MSc Computer Science

MBA

MCA

Gaming Courses

Web Designing Courses

title: Bsc Nursing
description: The Bachelor of Science in Nursing is a four-year undergraduate programme that focuses on providing medical students with extensive nursing expertise. The BSc Nursing Eligibility criteria are met by students who have finished class 12 with Biology as a subject or who have earned a Diploma in Nursing. NEET is the admissions process for BSc Nursing. The MNS Nursing test is used to give admission into the Indian Army for Bsc Nursing.
eligibility: Admission to the BSc Nursing programme is based on both merit and admission tests.
details:
BSc Nursing



WHAT IS Bsc NURSING?



The Bachelor of Science in Nursing is a four-year undergraduate programme that focuses on providing medical students with extensive nursing expertise. The BSc Nursing Eligibility criteria are met by students who have finished class 12 with Biology as a subject or who have earned a Diploma in Nursing. NEET is the admissions process for BSc Nursing. The MNS Nursing test is used to give admission into the Indian Army for Bsc Nursing.



The average BSc Nursing income ranges from INR 2.5 to 8 LPA after completion of the degree. A registered nurse with less than one year of experience may expect to earn INR 2,33,151 per year at the start of their career. Nurses earn an average pay of INR 2,49,994 after four years of service, while nurses with five or more years of experience earn INR 4,96,269 each year.



Candidates must have passed their 10+2 examinations from a recognised board and be from the Science stream, with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology as obligatory courses.







OVERVIEW OF THE COURSE 



Nursing BSc Depending on the institution, fees range from INR 2,000 to 2 LPA.

Admission to the BSc Nursing programme is based on both merit and admission tests.

AIIMS Bsc Nursing Admission Exam, MNS Entrance Exam, JIPMER, BHU UET, and others are some of the most prominent BSc Nursing entrance examinations.

Salary following a BSc in Nursing degree might range from INR 2.4 to INR 7.5 LPA, depending on the candidate's academic achievement.

Candidates can also enrol in a BSc Nursing Abroad programme to advance their careers and gain additional practical experience.





Course Level

Graduation

Duration

4 years

Examination Type

Semester System

Eligibility

10+2 in Physics, Chemistry, Biology

Admission 

Based on entrance exam

Course Fee

INR 8000 to 30,000

Average Starting Salary

INR 3 to 6 Lacs

Top Recruiting Organisations

 Manipal Hospital, Apollo Hospitals Enterprises, Medanta Medicity, Columbia Asia Hospital, Wockhardt Hospitals, Global Hospitals, Max Hospital,Fortis Healthcare,

Top Recruiting Areas

Defence Services, Colleges, Nursing Schools, Hospitals, Health Departments, etc. 

Top Job Profiles

Case Manager, Certified Nurse Midwife, Clinical Nurse Specialist, Manager/Administrator, Nurse Anaesthetist, Nurse Educator, Nurse Practitioner, Staff Nurse





WHY SHOULD YOU OPT THIS COURSE?



There are several benefits to studying BSc Nursing. The Bachelor of Science in Nursing is one of India's most popular nursing programmes, preparing students to work as nurses in a variety of settings. Some of the leading nursing colleges in India offer the programme. The following benefits are available to students who enrol in the course:

It creates several career possibilities in the healthcare industry.

It is a four-year programme recognised by the Indian Nursing Council that aids in obtaining government positions.

Students might find well-paying work in a variety of hospitals.

Candidates can also continue advanced courses in MSc Nursing and specialise in a field that interests them.

If students do not intend to pursue a Master's degree, they can enrol in a postgraduate programme in public health management.



FIELDS IN THIS COURSE



B.Sc. Nursing is a bachelor's degree programme with no specialty. Candidates can choose from the following specialisations in nursing after completing their B.Sc Nursing:



Industrial Nursing

Public Health Nursing

Military Nursing

Orthopaedic Nursing.

Hospital Nursing

Paediatric Nursing.

Home Health Nursing

Psychiatric Nursing



Career opportunities after graduating Bsc Nursing

Candidates with a BSc in nursing have a wide range of options following graduation. Candidates might choose to work or further their education in the same sector to improve their profile. 

Nursing Advisors to the Indian Government, Nursing Assistants/Supervisors, Teachers, and Nursing Superintendents are some of the occupations available. MSc Nursing, MSc Neuroscience, MSc Medical Microbiology, MSc Biotechnology, and MSc Biochemistry are available to those interested in continuing their education. 

In addition to MSc in renal sciences and dialysis technology, B.Sc Nursing graduates can enrol in a postgraduate degree in Public Health administration.The following are some of the job opportunities available to candidates:



In government or private hospitals, clinics, and other health agencies, clinical nurses are employed.



Nurse educators work at nursing schools, training institutions, and other settings.



Nurse researchers work at hospitals and research centres.



Hospital administrators or nurse managers.



HIGHER STUDIES AFTER COMPLETING THIS COURSE



Students wishing to pursue higher studies can opt for the following fields 

 MSc Neuroscience, MSc Biotechnology, MSc Nursing, MSc Medical Microbiology and MSc Biochemistry



What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for Bsc Nursing Graduates?





Job Profiles

Nursing Advisors to the Indian Government, Nursing Assistants/Supervisors, Teachers, and Nursing Superintendents are some of the occupations available. MSc Nursing, MSc Neuroscience, MSc Medical Microbiology, MSc Biotechnology, and MSc Biochemistry are available to those interested in continuing their education. 

In addition to MSc in renal sciences and dialysis technology, B.Sc Nursing graduates can enrol in a postgraduate degree in Public Health administration.The following are some of the job opportunities available to candidates:



In government or private hospitals, clinics, and other health agencies, clinical nurses are employed.



Nurse educators work at nursing schools, training institutions, and other settings.



Nurse researchers work at hospitals and research centres.



Hospital administrators or nurse managers.



HIGHER STUDIES AFTER COMPLETING THIS COURSE



Students wishing to pursue higher studies can opt for the following fields 

 MSc Neuroscience, MSc Biotechnology, MSc Nursing, MSc Medical Microbiology and MSc Biochemistry

Under the 15 percent AIQ and 85 percent State level quota seats, all government and private medical institutions will grant admission. NEET is also the sole test that is accepted for admission to institutes like JIPMER, AIIMS, PGIMER, and others.



CENTAC- The Centralised Admission Committee (CENTAC), Government of Puducherry, conducts a state-level admissions procedure for admissions into different Engineering, Pharmacy, and Medical Programs at various government and private institutions across the state.



SAAT- The Siksha 'O' Anusandhan University Admission Test (SAAT) is a national level test held by Siksha 'O' Anusandhan (SOA) University for admission to undergraduate (UG) and postgraduate (PG) programmes.



ITM NEST- TM NEST 2022 is a qualifying common entrance examination for admission to several programmes at ITM University Gwalior. title: BAMS Bachelor of Ayurveda, Medicine and Surgery
description: BACHELOR OF AYURVEDIC MEDICINE AND SURGERY (BAMS)
details:
BACHELOR OF AYURVEDIC MEDICINE AND SURGERY (BAMS)

ABOUT

Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery (BAMS) is an acronym for Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery. BAMS is a bachelor's degree programme designed to familiarise students with Ayurvedic concepts and prepare them to apply them to patient care. BAMS mixes Ayurveda with modern medical principles, and students are taught a combination of the two as part of the course.

Ayurveda, which dates back to the Vedic period, is one of the oldest medicinal systems. Its treatments are known for using natural materials and are based on the therapeutic properties of herbs.







The Ayurveda medical system, which is regarded as an alternative therapeutic strategy, not only heals and prevents diseases, but it also reduces the number of diseases that enter the human body. It makes use of the self-healing mechanism.

People  around the globe are beginning to adopt this alternative therapy method. Even the World Healing Organization has established a global forum to promote traditional health methods such as Ayurveda. As a result, students enrolled in the Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery (BAMS) programme have a variety of possibilities.



OVERVIEW

Degree Type

Undergraduate

Course Duration

5 Years

Minimum Academic Requirement

Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry and Biology (Medical Stream)

Subject Requirement

Physics, Chemistry and Biology

Minimum Aggregate Score Requirement

50 per cent or More

Frequency of Exam

Annual

Admission/ Selection Process

Entrance Exam Based

Exams Accepted

NEET, OJEE, KEAM, etc

Average Course Fee

Rs 20,000 - Rs 3,00,000 per year



SPECIALISATIONS UNDER BAMS 

Padartha Vigyan

Sharir Rachana

Sharir Kriya

Swasthavritta

Rasa Shastra

Agad Tantra

Rog & Vikriti Vigyan

Charak Samhita

Prasuti and Stri Roga

KaumaraBhritya

Kayachikitsa

Shalya Tantra

Shalakya Tantra

Charak Samhita



WHY SHOULD YOU OPT BAMS?



BAMS is a globally recognised programme. Doctors and scientists are interested in discovering alternative and conventional medications to treat patients' illnesses and disorders.

Ayurveda is an old and traditional therapeutic method. As a result, this sector has a variety of applications.

Students who want to practise allopathy medicine following BAMS can take a bridging course.



BAMS graduates are considered to be on par with MBBS graduates.



Various state governments have established health facilities for the practise of Ayurveda.



For a better understanding of the subjects, various MD and MS after BAMS are available.

Job opportunities after BAMS with their salary packages-





Ayurvedic pharmacist: Candidates may pursue a profession in Ayurvedic pharmacy. After finishing the BAMS programme, he or she can work as a pharmacist. A pharmacist creates ayurvedic medications with precise ingredients.



Teacher: A BAMS graduate candidate may pursue a teaching profession. He or she can enrol in a postgraduate programme, and after successfully completing it, hopeful candidates can enrol in a Ph.D. programme. He or she might choose to work as a professor at any Ayurveda institution after completing a Ph.D. degree.



Ayurveda Counselor: BAMS graduates have the option of working as an Ayurveda Counselor. Through traditional Ayurvedic medical knowledge, he or she advocates preventative healthcare approaches.



Dietitian or dietician: A dietitian or dietician is a dietetics professional. Dietetics is the science of human nutrition and eating habits. A dietician adjusts the nutrition of his or her patients based on their medical conditions and needs. He or she is responsible for diagnosing, assessing, and treating nutritional issues.



A Panchkarma Practitioner- is a person who gives panchakarma treatment and cures for a variety of human ailments, problems, and illnesses. Panchkarma refers to five different treatments. It entails using ayurveda cleansing treatments to relieve stress and prepare the human body tissues to receive the advantages of diet, nutrition, and exercise.



HIGHER COURSE AFTER BAMS

phd in ayurveda

md in ayurveda-panchakarma

ms ayurveda

b pharm ayurveda

title: BDS
details:
BDS- Bachelor of Dental Surgery

About this course-

BDS, also known as Bachelor of Dental Surgery, is a professional degree programme that provides individuals with a variety of work prospects. 

They can work as a dentist in a government or private hospital after completing their degree. They might also work in the pharmaceutical or medical device manufacturing businesses. 



They can also work as a dental surgeon in private practice. Candidates must take an entrance exam to be admitted to BDS. The Nationwide Eligibility Cumulative Entrance Test (NEET) is a national level test for BDS admissions. 

According to the Dental Council of India's data, there are 26,000 places available in BDS programmes. These seats are offered at 315 institutions, including government, private, central, and deemed universities.

BDS Course Highlights-

Particulars

Details

Course name

Bachelor of Dental Surgery

Knows as

BDS

Course level

Undergraduate

Course type

Degree programme

Course duration

5-year

Minimum qualification

10+2

Admission process

Entrance Exam (NEET)

Average course fee

INR 1-6 lakh



Why should you study BDS?

The number of dental patients in the country has grown as a result of greater knowledge about oral hygiene. To treat these patients, one must have a BDS degree, as dentistry in India is only allowed to be practised after completion of the BDS programme.

Students anticipate an increase in job prospects shortly. The reason for this is that the number of dental patients and physicians is rapidly rising, and the government wants to provide better job chances for dentists by expanding employment options.

Students may earn up to INR 15,000 - 40,000 (approx.) during a paid internship, which grows with growing knowledge and experience.

It is an incredible course for the students who desire to choose dentistry as their career.

Students can even practise their abilities outside nations like the United States and Canada to make more money than they would in India. They must, however, pass tests such as the NBDE or NDEB to do so.

BDS Jobs and Scope with Salary 

Graduates with a BDS degree have a wide range of career and employment choices available to them. They can work as dentists, researchers, professors, or even create dental clinics in government or private facilities. Not all grads, however, are eager to work and make money. Some of them choose to continue their education in the field of dentistry.

Because BDS is the sole and mandatory education for practising dentistry in India, most students find work within a year of graduation. Some even pursue advanced degrees, such as MDS.

Job Profile

Job Description 

Average Salary

Dentist

A dentist's employment entails examining patients' oral health, removing tooth decay, filling cavities, and other procedures. If a doctor wants to work at a hospital or start their clinic, it is entirely up to them.

INR 3.5 - 4.5 lacs



Researcher



BDS graduate students who want to perform research work can perform it in advanced dental research.

INR 3 lacs



Lecturer





Students with advanced abilities, knowledge, and experience can serve as lecturers at reputable public or private dental schools.

INR 5 lacs



Dental Officer at Indian Defense Services (Army, Navy, Air Force)

Students who wish to put their expertise to use in the defence services must be Registered Dental practitioners in India to be considered for the position.

INR 9 lacs

Dentist in Indian 

Railways



Candidates can apply to work as a dentist for the Indian Railways when a vacancy arises.

INR 7.2 lacs



As a result, students can find work in the following fields in the job market:

Government Sector: 

Students may find attractive work prospects in India's Defence Services, such as the Army, Navy, and Air Force, as well as Dentists in Indian Railways and state government Dental Surgeon positions. However, opportunities in these fields are accessible based on qualifications, experience, and open positions.



Private Sector: 

Students can find fantastic jobs at private hospitals, dental clinics, and other places in the private sector. Those who do not wish to work in these professions may establish clinics in which to hone their skills.



Dentistry Research: 

Research: Students who desire to widen their intellectual horizons by continuing their education may opt to look into dentistry research.



Dentists in Gulf Countries: 

Students who are disappointed with their professions in India opt to migrate to the Gulf States, where dentists are in high demand.

Students who are willing to opt for higher studies can go for the following :

Master of Dental Surgery ( MDS)

Master of Public Health (MPH)

Master in Health Administration

MBA in Healthcare Management

MBA in Hospitality Management

BDS Entrance Exams–

title: BPT
details:
BPT - BACHELOR OF PHYSIOTHERAPY

What is a BPT course?

A bachelor's degree in physiotherapy is a four-year undergraduate programme that includes a six-month clinical internship and focuses on the science of physical movement with the goal of avoiding illness and disability. 

Candidates must have completed the 10+2 programme from a recognised educational board to be eligible to apply for the course. The training is available across the country for an average yearly course price of INR 1 to 5 Lacs, depending on the institute.

A bachelor's degree in physiotherapy is a four-year undergraduate programme that includes a six-month clinical internship and focuses on the science of physical movement with the goal of avoiding illness and disability. 

Candidates must have completed the 10+2 programme from a recognised educational board to be eligible to apply for the course.





Overview/Highlights of this Course-

Course Level

Undergraduate

Duration

4.5 years

Eligibility

10+2 from a recognized educational Board.

Admission Process

Counseling after qualification of a relevant entrance examination.

Examination Type

Semester System

Total Course Fee

INR 1 to 5 Lacs

Average Starting Salary

INR 2 to 8 Lacs















FIELDS UNDER BACHELOR OF PHYSIOTHERAPY







Pediatrics Physiotherapy

Obstetrics Physiotherapy

Sports Physiotherapy

Neurology Physiotherapy

Orthopedic Physiotherapy

Geriatrics Physiotherapy

Post Operative Physiotherapy

Cardiovascular Physiotherapy



WHY SHOULD YOU OPT FOR THIS BPT COURSE?

The BPT programme is for students who have always wanted to work in the human services industry. Additionally, they can assist you in incorporating complementary and alternative medicine into your practice. In physiotherapy, a student can learn about human anatomy while working with their hands.

Physiotherapists are in great demand in India, with a wide range of patients seeking their services, including those recovering from surgery and accidents, as well as those with chronic diseases like arthritis.

Sports injuries are expected to increase as individuals grow more worried about their health and fitness, resulting in a greater need for physiotherapy services.

A Bachelor of Physiotherapy degree allows graduates to work as sports physiotherapists, physiotherapists, osteopaths, or consultants.



Career opportunities after graduating from Bachelors in Physiotherapy:



Physiotherapist Consultant can work in Multi-specialty Hospitals, Health Care Centres, and Non-Governmental Organizations after pursuing this course.

MNC Health Care Corporate Physiotherapist 

Ergonomic Care Advisor Physiotherapy Instructor in Modern Gyms

Physiotherapist at Physically Challenged Children's Special Schools

Physiotherapist in Senior Living Communities

Entrepreneurs in Rehabilitation Centro

 Private Practitioners conducting Private Physiotherapy Practice 

Sports Physiotherapists in collaboration with Sports Associations, Sports Teams, and individually hired physiotherapists by elite athletes

Researchers working as collaborating with other team members to set project goals, research methodology, and other test criteria is an important aspect of the work. Researchers also provide recommendations on ways to enhance the techniques that are employed.



What can we do further after completing this course?



Students can pursue a master's degree in physical therapy by majoring in any speciality, such as MPT in Neurology, MPT in Sports Physiotherapy, MPT in Paediatrics, and so on.

BPT graduates can go on to get an MBA in Hospital Administration or an MBA in Healthcare Management and work as entrepreneurs, opening their own fitness clubs and clinics.

WHY SHOULD YOU OPT FOR THIS BPT COURSE?

The BPT programme is for students who have always wanted to work in the human services industry. Additionally, they can assist you in incorporating complementary and alternative medicine into your practice. In physiotherapy, a student can learn about human anatomy while working with their hands.

Physiotherapists are in great demand in India, with a wide range of patients seeking their services, including those recovering from surgery and accidents, as well as those with chronic diseases like arthritis.

Sports injuries are expected to increase as individuals grow more worried about their health and fitness, resulting in a greater need for physiotherapy services.

A Bachelor of Physiotherapy degree allows graduates to work as sports physiotherapists, physiotherapists, osteopaths, or consultants.



Career opportunities after graduating from Bachelors in Physiotherapy:



Physiotherapist Consultant can work in Multi-specialty Hospitals, Health Care Centres, and Non-Governmental Organizations after pursuing this course.

MNC Health Care Corporate Physiotherapist 

Ergonomic Care Advisor Physiotherapy Instructor in Modern Gyms

Physiotherapist at Physically Challenged Children's Special Schools

Physiotherapist in Senior Living Communities

Entrepreneurs in Rehabilitation Centro

 Private Practitioners conducting Private Physiotherapy Practice 

Sports Physiotherapists in collaboration with Sports Associations, Sports Teams, and individually hired physiotherapists by elite athletes

Researchers working as collaborating with other team members to set project goals, research methodology, and other test criteria is an important aspect of the work. Researchers also provide recommendations on ways to enhance the techniques that are employed.



What can we do further after completing this course?



Students can pursue a master's degree in physical therapy by majoring in any speciality, such as MPT in Neurology, MPT in Sports Physiotherapy, MPT in Paediatrics, and so on.

BPT graduates can go on to get an MBA in Hospital Administration or an MBA in Healthcare Management and work as entrepreneurs, opening their own fitness clubs and clinics.

IPU CET

Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University organises this exam.

Eligibility

12th class or equivalent from recognized board

Age limit - 18 Years to 21 Years

LPUNEST

It is a national entrance and scholarship examination. LPUNEST is an online test that is administered twice a year for BPT entrance in 2022. Candidates can receive a scholarship of up to 5 lakhs based on their exam results.

Eligibility

10+2 or equivalent degree from a recognized board.

BCECE

The BCECE test is taken entirely online. The test consists of 300 multiple-choice questions from the fields of physics, chemistry, and biology. The test will be conducted in Hindi and English, with negative marking of one point for each erroneous answer..

Eligibility

Applicants must be a citizen of India & domicile of Bihar.

Candidates must have qualified 12th class with 45% marks (40% marks for reserved category).

IEMJEE

The university of engineering and management administers an annual university level test. The IEMJEE is offered in both online and offline formats.

Eligibility

Aspirants must pass with class 10 & 12th with a minimum aggregate of 50%.

title: BUMS
details:
Bachelor of Unani Medicine & Surgery 

ABOUT

Bachelor of Unani Medicine & Surgery is the full form of B.U.M.S. It is a 5.5-year undergraduate degree programme that combines Unani system application with medical expertise. BAMS and BUMS (Unani) are not the same thing (Ayurveda). 

The Unani system of medicine has been practised on this planet for a long time and continues to be prevalent. B.U.M.S. (Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery) is an undergraduate medical degree in Unani medicine and surgery.



    



 B.U.M.S. is a 5.5-year degree that includes 4.5 years of main course studies and a 1-year internship. The Central Council of Indian Medicine (CCIM), a statutory authority under the Department of Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha, and Homeopathy, regulates B.U.M.S education and training in India (AYUSH). 

According to CCIM's official statement, there are 1,706 B.U.M.S. seats available across the country. Candidates must meet the B.U.M.S. eligibility requirements. NEET is used to admit students to the programme.



B.U.M.S Course Highlights

Degree Name

Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery

Abbreviation

B.U.M.S.

Degree Type

Undergraduate (regular)

Duration

5.5 years

Discipline

Unani Medical Science

Internship Duration

1 year mandatory

Average Fee

Rs. 50,000 to Rs. 650,000





WHY SHOULD YOU STUDY B.U.M.S?

Unani medicine is based on traditional therapeutic methods that use plant medicines.



For almost 6000 years, Unani Medicine has been practised. Unani medicine is founded on the four components of the system. It consists of fire, water, earth, and air. Unani is based on four different comic ideas.

The human body includes blood, phlegm, yellow bile, and black bile. Unani drugs can be used to treat infertility, vitiligo, diabetes, kidney stones, and a range of other diseases.

A Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery is an undergraduate programme that prepares students for a number of careers in Unani medicine. B.U.M.S. graduates can work as professors, therapists, Unani consultants, pharmacists, and a number of other jobs.







Top Job Profiles



The following table includes some of the most common job profiles:









Job Profile

   Job Profile Description

Unani Consultant

Patients receive personalised medical advice and treatment from a Unani specialist. They follow the ideas and practices of Unani medicine.

Unani Institute Lecturer

In Unani Medicine Institutes, a lecturer teaches various Unani Medicine methods and therapies.

Unani Therapist

Patients get unani therapy from unani therapists. Unani therapists have the option of working in a hospital setting or in private practice.

Hakeem

A physician who uses Unani Medicine to treat his or her patients. He treats all major illnesses and performs treatments in accordance with Unani Medicine principles.

Unani Chemist

An Unani Chemist makes Unani medicines for sale purposes, they either sell it directly to patients or to medical shops. 

Unani Medicine Pharmacist

Unani medicinal stores are owned by Unani medicine pharmacists. They have the authority to sell any form of Unani Medicine and can advise customers on which medicine to buy.





HIGHER EDUCATION AFTER PURSUING B.U.M.S

Aspirants can pursue a Master's degree in MS (Master of Surgery) or MD after finishing the BUMS programme (Doctor of Medicine). 

Aspirants who have graduated from BUMS can also take PG courses to advance their careers. MD-Unani (Doctor of Medicine) and MS-Unani (Master of Surgery) courses are the most popular.

WHAT ARE THE ENTRY-LEVEL SALARY PACKAGES & AVERAGE SALARY PACKAGES FOR BUMS GRADUATE?



Following are the entry level salary for top job areas after graduating from BUMS:

      Job Profile

Average Annual Salary

Unani Consultant

INR 3,00,000

Unani Institute Lecturer

INR 4,50,000

Unani Therapist

INR 2,50,000

Hakeem

            INR 3,50,000

Unani Chemist

            INR 6,50,000

Unani Medicine Pharmacist

            INR 4,00,000





TOP ENTRANCE EXAM TO GET INTO YOUR DESIRED B.U.M.S COLLEGE

There are various entrance exams through which you can get through your desired college.

NEET, Combined Pre-Ayush Test (CPAT), CPMEE, KEAM, and other national or state-level medical entrance exams are used to get admission to the BUMS programme. Some medical schools also have their own admission exams to assess candidates' expertise.





BUMS: Top Colleges-



Top BUMS college of India are as follows-

Course Fees

title: MBBS
description: BACHELOR OF MEDICINE & BACHELOR OF SURGERY (MBBS):
details:
BACHELOR OF MEDICINE & BACHELOR OF SURGERY (MBBS): 

                                    



What is MBBS??



Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery, or MBBS, is a professional undergraduate medical science degree.

This programme is designed for those who desire to pursue their ambition of becoming a doctor.

Because it is one of the most prestigious degrees in the world, it is a highly competitive programme.

To gain admission to any of India's top MBBS institutes, one must pass the NEET exam with good marks that is conducted every year by National Testing Agency (NTA).



Highlights of MBBS Course:



Name of the course

Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery

Abbreviation

MBBS

Degree Type

Undergraduate

Eligibility

10+2 with PCB stream

Duration of the course

5.5 years with one year of rotational internship

 Average Fees Incurred

The average MBBS course fee ranges from INR 70,000 - to 21 lakhs per annum



Why should we opt for MBBS?



Interest in the medical field- 



This course is for students who are interested in studying the fundamentals of the human body, how it functions, and how to diagnose and cure disorders.



This course is structured in such a way that it develops a person's psychology toward feelings of service and illness in those who are suffering from various diseases.



Diverse career Scope-



After completing an MBBS programme, there are numerous work prospects in the medical industry.



After earning a bachelor's degree, one can pursue further schooling or pursue a career as a doctor, surgeon, or physician, or work as a medical representative in any other sector.



What can we do after MBBS?



Start A Job-



A graduate of MBBS has a wide range of professional options and is highly valued because of the humanitarian and life-saving nature of their work.

An MBBS degree allows students to work in both the public and private sectors.

Biomedical firms, medical centers, healthcare institutions, laboratories, emergency departments, community health centers, and private practices all have plenty of job openings.



The MBBS degree allows graduates to prescribe medication, and most participants prefer to pursue a master's degree to become well-known physicians in this profession.

    26.  Physiologist



Pursue Higher Studies-



After completing an MBBS programme with honors. Candidates can pursue advanced degrees such as MS, MD, or DNB in any related field.

  

Some major MBBS course specialization areas are:



                                       MBBS Specializations

The average salary for MBBS graduate candidates varies gradually.

The variation may be attributable to the candidate's passion and experience in the firm and workplace, as well as the field of employment.

A student must take the National Eligibility Cum Entrance Test to be admitted to an MBBS programme in India (NEET).





This is the only entrance exam that is accepted by both government and private institutions for admission to an MBBS programme.

The JIPMER MBBS Exam and the AIIMS MBBS Exam were two additional significant entrance exams for admission to MBBS programmes in India.

eligibility: The eligibility criteria to get admission to BA is 10+2 from any recognised board
details:
BA- BACHELOR OF ARTS



What is BA?



BA Full Form is Bachelor of Arts. Bachelor of Arts is a 3 years undergraduate course that is one of India's most popular undergraduate degree programs. 



This course can pursue anyone who has completed their 10+2 in any subject.



Highlights of BA Course



Degree

Undergraduate

Full form

Bachelor Of Arts

Duration

Three-years

Minimum percentage

40% in 10+2

Selection process

Merit-based or entrance exam based

Subject required

Any stream from a recognised university or board at the 10+2 level

Average fees

The average BA course fees in India range from INR 4,000 - to 65,000 per annum



BA vs BA Hons: Course Difference



Apart from B.A, candidates can also do B.Com (Hons) if they want to gain in-depth knowledge in the Arts stream.



BA or BA (Hons) courses are covered under the BA Programme. However, when it comes to the specializations available, they are extremely different. The following is a basic summary of BA & BA (Hons):



BA

BA (Hons)

The BA course covers all aspects of the arts in general because it includes all of the required subjects.

BA Hons provides specializations in any one subject such as English, Political Science, Psychology, History, economics, and so on

BA is of 3 years duration	

BA (Hons) is also an ears duration course

The eligibility criteria to get admission to BA is 10+2 from any recognised board

Aspirants should have passed 10+2 from any recognised board and must have studied the Hons seeking subject at the qualifying level

No specializations offered

It offers specializations

Admission to BA programmes is offered on a merit-based and entrance exam basis.

It is the same for admission to BA (Hons)



BA Specializations-



A Bachelor of Arts is a broad domain that covers a lot of Fields. As a result, the core and elective subjects differ depending on the specialization.



Here is a presented the list of  popular BA field:

English

Sociology

Journalism and Mass Communication

Philosophy

History

Political Science

Education/Physical Education

Environmental Sciences

Fine Arts 

Archaeology

Anthropology

Foreign Language

Psychology

Media Studies

Religious Studies

Public Administration

title: BA ENGLISH
description: BA English is an advanced degree programme in English Literature, Cultural Studies, Rhetoric, and Literary History provided by numerous institutions and colleges.
details:
BA ENGLISH



What is BA English?



BA English is an advanced degree programme in English Literature, Cultural Studies, Rhetoric, and Literary History provided by numerous institutions and colleges.

It is a three-year undergraduate degree programme provided by a number of institutions and colleges around the country.

Students study a variety of plays, novels, and poems from many periods of history in order to debate and analyse the eras in which they were written, the author's motivation, cultural importance, literary acumen, and character depth.









BA English course details

BA English Full Form

Bachelor of Arts in English

Course Level

Undergraduate

BA English Course Duration

3 years

Course Mode

Offline / Regular

BA English Course Eligibility Criteria

Students should clear class 12th board exams with 50% aggregate marks or equivalent CGPA

BA English Course Admission Process

Merit Based / Entrance Exams

BA English Course Fees (INR)

INR 8,000 - 1.3 LPA

BA English Salary (INR)

INR 2.5 - 3.5 LPA









Why should you opt for this course?



A degree in English will help you find a variety of intriguing and exciting work options in industries such as teaching, translating, language experts, journalism, and publishing houses, among others.

Study Abroad: English is a very international subject that provides a choice of modules that may be studied in other countries. Universities are increasingly offering a year abroad programme as part of their course framework, allowing you to adapt your degree to your travel interests.

Improve your language skills: English is one of the most widely spoken languages in the world today. As a result, a degree in English may help you get a good command of the language and can also help you land some of the country's most renowned positions.

There is no hard and fast rule that after earning a Bachelor's degree in English, one must pursue a Master's degree. English is one of the few disciplines in which students may pick from a variety of job opportunities after completing it.



Specialisation of the course-



18th-century British Literature

19th-century British Literature

African American Literature and Language

American Literature After 1900

American Literature Before 1900

Book History and Textual Studies

Contemporary Literature

Creative Writing

Media and Digital Studies

Mediaeval Literature

Modernist Studies

Race and Ethnicity Studies

Renaissance

Rhetoric and Composition

Theory and Cultural Studies

Visual Culture



Career Opportunities after graduating BA English



Job Profiles

Job Description

Teacher

Teachers at schools and colleges are in charge of educating the next generation of students; they prefer to hire BA English Literature graduates.

Content Writer

Content writers are in charge of writing articles for blogs, corporate websites, ads, and product descriptions, among other things.

Journalist

Journalists are employed to track down breaking news and write high-quality pieces for publications.

Script Writer

Scriptwriters are people paid by media businesses to develop screenplays for television shows, movies, and other types of entertainment.

Proofreader

At publishing houses, proofreaders are recruited to read and edit the grammar and language of other writers' work..

Editor

Editors are employed to modify and examine all information before it is published in periodicals, newspapers, or on the internet.



HIGHER STUDIES AFTER COMPLETING THIS COURSE

Students can choose from a variety of career and educational options. 

MA in English, journalism and mass communication; management; tourism administration; law; international relations; linguistics; communication management; film editing and direction; visual communication; and library and information science are all options for meritorious graduates in English language and literature. 

You can enrol in any of the courses that appeal to your skills and interests.

What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for BA ENGLISH graduates?

For reference, the income obtained after completing a BA English course is listed below

Job Profiles

Average Salary (INR)

Teacher

3 LPA

Content Writer

3.5 LPA

Journalist

2.5 - 4 LPA

Script Writer

5 - 6.5 LPA

Proofreader

3 - 3.5 LPA

Editor

3 - 4 LPA

PUCET- The Punjab University Common Entrance Test (PUCET) is a test administered by Punjab University in Chandigarh. For

NPAT- For undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, the Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies (NMIMS) administers the National Test for Programs After Twelfth (NPAT). It is a university-level test that is given for a variety of university programmes.



BHU UET-  NTA administers the Banaras Hindu University Undergraduate Entrance Test (BHU UET) on behalf of the university (BHU). It is a national entrance exam for admission to BHU's undergraduate programmes.	



PUCET- The Punjab University Common Entrance Test (PUCET) is a test administered by Punjab University in Chandigarh. For undergraduate and postgraduate programmes offered by the university, the test is held at the state level.

CUET- The Common University Entrance Test, formerly known as the Central Universities Common Entrance Test, is an all-India test administered by the National Testing Agency for admission to 45 Central Universities of India's Undergraduate, Integrated, Postgraduate, Diploma, Certification, and Research Programs.

title: BA Archaeology docx
description: Archaeology is the study of human history via tangible remains, both ancient and recent. Archaeology is frequently seen as a subfield of sociocultural anthropology.
details:
BA ARCHAEOLOGY



What is this course? 



Archaeology is the study of human history via tangible remains, both ancient and recent. Archaeology is frequently seen as a subfield of sociocultural anthropology.

In layman's terms, archaeology is the scientific study of historic or prehistoric people and their cultures via the examination of their artifacts, monuments, and other exceptional remnants, particularly those that have been unearthed.

Archaeology's fundamental goal is to investigate and comprehend how and why human behavior has changed over time. They look for patterns in the progression of major cultural events, as well as the reasons for their occurrence. The use of material artifacts in archaeology helps us understand history.

The Archaeology course will help you concentrate on how to unearth, interpret, and preserve material human history. You'll learn fresh facts about human history as well as new perspectives on iconic things and events. The course provides a variety of work prospects in the fields of archaeologist, officer, instructor, and many more interesting positions that will aid you in your exploration of the field.



Highlights of this course:



Popular courses



Undergraduate: Undergraduate archaeology classes that focus on archaeology and related fields such as history, museology, and ancient culture.

Duration



Undergraduate: 3 years.



Eligibility



Undergraduate: 12 standard.



Average fees

Undergraduate: INR 50,000 – 15,00,000 per annum.





Fields/ Streams of the course-



Archaeologist.

Historic buildings inspector/conservation officer.

Museum/gallery curator.

Heritage manager.

Lecturer.

Documentation Specialist.

Museum education officer.







Why should you study this Course?



Archaeology students examine the ruins of early civilizations all around the world to learn about the lives of ancient humans. 

This provides a window into the past, allowing us to see how various tribes lived, expanded, and, in some cases, died. 

Fieldwork is an important part of any archaeology degree, whether it is a BA or BSc. It allows you to build practical skills, do research, and even create your own archaeological discoveries.

 Some universities provide opportunities to perform excavations in nations like Egypt or Greece.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profile

Job Description

Archaeologist

Archaeologists use excavation, dating, and analyzing items and historical materials to learn about the past and different elements of human life.

Museum Curator

The Museum Curators are in charge of assembling, cataloging, and managing the artistic and cultural collection, as well as assisting in its presentation and display. They are in charge of a museum's collection of exhibits.

Heritage Manager

A heritage manager is someone who oversees and administers historical site access as well as manages and conserves heritage sites.

Lecturer/Professor

They can work as a Lecturer or Professor in any college or university to teach the many disciplines contained in Archaeology after earning a higher education degree.





What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



Students can pursue the following:

MA in Archaeology

MA in Ancient Indian History and Archaeology

Master of Science in Archaeology

Master of Philosophy in Ancient Indian History, Culture and Archaeology

Post-Graduate Diploma in Archaeology

Doctor of Philosophy in Ancient Indian History, Culture and Archaeology

What are the entry level salary packages & average salary packages for this course graduates?



Job Profile

Average Annual Salary

Museum curator

Rs. 3 to 6 Lakhs p.a. (Approx.)

Heritage Manager

Rs. 4 to 7 Lakhs p.a. (Approx.)

Archaeologist

Rs. 3 to 8 Lakhs p.a. (Approx.)

Professor

Rs. 5 to 8 Lakhs p.a. (Approx.)



Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?



Admission to several colleges and universities is based on merit. Candidates who are deserving and have received good grades in their academics are given places.



The Andhra University Common Entrance Test (AUCET) is used to give admission to several Andhra universities' postgraduate programmes. The exam is conducted once a year and is conducted in an offline format.



The Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test (IPU CET) is held for candidates seeking admission to undergraduate and postgraduate programmes offered by associated colleges and institutions.



Calcutta University Entrance Exam (CUEE) is an entrance exam held by the University of Calcutta for undergraduate and postgraduate programmes.



List of top colleges in India related to this course-



Institute of Archaeology, Archaeological Survey of India, New Delhi

Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi

Barkatullah Vishwavidhyalaya, Bhopal

The Delhi Institute of Heritage Research & Management, New Delhi

Kurukshetra University, Kurukshetra (Haryana)

Department of Ancient Indian History, Culture and Archaeology, Panjab Un

title: BA Anthropology docx
description: Anthropology is the scientific study of human evolution and social-cultural change over time.
details:
BA ANTHROPOLOGY







What is this course?



Anthropology is the scientific study of human evolution and social-cultural change over time.

Anthropology is divided into several branches, including sociocultural, biological-physical, applied, linguistic, and archaeological.

Anthropology is the study of numerous elements of human development.

Archaeology, Culture and Society, Tribal and Rural Development, and other disciplines are included in the BA Anthropology programme.







Highlights Of this course:



Course Level

Undergraduate

Full Form

Bachelor of Arts in Anthropology

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester Examinations

Eligibility

Candidates must have received at least 55 percent in their 10+2 exams.

Admission Process

Entrance/ Merit-Based



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Archaeology - Archaeology is the study of historical peoples and cultures.

Anthropology of Biology -  Evolution, genetics, and health are the focus of biological anthropology.

Anthropology of Culture -  Cultural anthropology is the study of human communities and cultural aspects.

Anthropology of Language.



Why should you study this Course?

In terms of personal development and career advancement, the BA Anthropology programme is one of the most popular in India. There are numerous advantages to taking the course. The following are some of the advantages and benefits of taking this course.



BA Anthropology provides a variety of job opportunities in a variety of industries and organizations. Students who complete this course can work in the private sector as art galleries, tour guides, curators, and other positions. BA Anthropology students can apply for government positions such as archaeological survey guide or government museum manager.

Deals with Social Issues: This training will prepare you to deal with NGOs and social issues. Anthropology research can help with birth control, juvenile delinquency, and other social issues.

The majority of the positions are in the research and survey of historical documents.

The study of social and cultural components of human evolution, as well as statistical methods for learning more about the behavioral aspect and historical facts, make this subject intriguing to study.

Popular in Other Countries: The course is popular in other countries, with jobs available at UNESCO, UNICEF, and WHO.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profile

Job Description

Tour Guide

Tour guides assist visitors by guiding them on informative journeys with groups of tourists or individuals to notable locations. A tour guide can lead tours to historical, cultural, or regional points of interest. Their main work study is educating tourists, vacationers, and students about places.

Archivist

Archivists are in charge of collecting and managing historical documents. An Archivist can be hired by an individual, organization, or government entity.

Cultural Resource Manager

The Cultural Resource Manager is in charge of managing arts and heritage through the use of cultural resources. Cultural Resource Managers manage these resources using cultural heritage, traditional, and historical management abilities.

Urban Planner

An urban and regional planner's role is to create plans and programmes that make the best use of the land while maintaining optimum usability. They assist in the creation of communities, the accommodation and adjustment of population growth, and the revitalization of physical facilities in cities, countries, and metropolitan areas.

Curator

A curator gathers museum and art exhibits and establishes an antiques collection. A curator is in charge of assembling and administering cultural collections.

Linguist

BA ANTHROPOLOGY







What is this course?



Anthropology is the scientific study of human evolution and social-cultural change over time.

Anthropology is divided into several branches, including sociocultural, biological-physical, applied, linguistic, and archaeological.

Anthropology is the study of numerous elements of human development.

Archaeology, Culture and Society, Tribal and Rural Development, and other disciplines are included in the BA Anthropology programme.







Highlights Of this course:



Course Level

Undergraduate

Full Form

Bachelor of Arts in Anthropology

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester Examinations

Eligibility

Candidates must have received at least 55 percent in their 10+2 exams.

Admission Process

Entrance/ Merit-Based



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Archaeology - Archaeology is the study of historical peoples and cultures.

Anthropology of Biology -  Evolution, genetics, and health are the focus of biological anthropology.

Anthropology of Culture -  Cultural anthropology is the study of human communities and cultural aspects.

Anthropology of Language.



Why should you study this Course?

In terms of personal development and career advancement, the BA Anthropology programme is one of the most popular in India. There are numerous advantages to taking the course. The following are some of the advantages and benefits of taking this course.



BA Anthropology provides a variety of job opportunities in a variety of industries and organizations. Students who complete this course can work in the private sector as art galleries, tour guides, curators, and other positions. BA Anthropology students can apply for government positions such as archaeological survey guide or government museum manager.

Deals with Social Issues: This training will prepare you to deal with NGOs and social issues. Anthropology research can help with birth control, juvenile delinquency, and other social issues.

The majority of the positions are in the research and survey of historical documents.

The study of social and cultural components of human evolution, as well as statistical methods for learning more about the behavioral aspect and historical facts, make this subject intriguing to study.

Popular in Other Countries: The course is popular in other countries, with jobs available at UNESCO, UNICEF, and WHO.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profile

Job Description

Tour Guide

Tour guides assist visitors by guiding them on informative journeys with groups of tourists or individuals to notable locations. A tour guide can lead tours to historical, cultural, or regional points of interest. Their main work study is educating tourists, vacationers, and students about places.

Archivist

Archivists are in charge of collecting and managing historical documents. An Archivist can be hired by an individual, organization, or government entity.

Cultural Resource Manager

The Cultural Resource Manager is in charge of managing arts and heritage through the use of cultural resources. Cultural Resource Managers manage these resources using cultural heritage, traditional, and historical management abilities.

Urban Planner

An urban and regional planner's role is to create plans and programmes that make the best use of the land while maintaining optimum usability. They assist in the creation of communities, the accommodation and adjustment of population growth, and the revitalization of physical facilities in cities, countries, and metropolitan areas.

Curator

A curator gathers museum and art exhibits and establishes an antiques collection. A curator is in charge of assembling and administering cultural collections.

Linguist

Linguists work with forensics, historical records, and languages to understand, translate, and research them.
