details:
BA FOREIGN LANGUAGE





What is this course?



BA Foreign Language focuses on the background, history, and culture of foreign countries, as well as grammar and communication skills in that language.

 Basic language skills such as grammar, reading, listening, speaking, and writing comprehension are taught to students. 

Foreign Language Trainer, Research Associate, Tourist Guide, Interpreter, Air Hostess or Flight Steward, Freelance Writer, Translator, Interpreter, and more employment are available to graduates.



Highlights Of this course?



Degree

Bachelors

Full Form

Bachelor of Arts in Foreign Languages

Duration

The Bachelor of Arts [BA] (Foreign Languages) programme lasts three years.

Age

No Age Limit

Minimum Percentage

50%



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Foreign Language Trainer

Research Associate

Tourist Guide, Interpreter

Air Hostess or Flight Steward

Freelance Writer

Translator

Interpreter

Why should you study this course?



Experts in foreign languages make life easier, and this very concept may entice people to continue this degree because they are rejecting traditional methods owing to a change in lifestyle. This undergraduate programme emphasizes critical thinking and market research, both of which are life-saving abilities.

BA Foreign Language can assist students in learning new languages, which will benefit them in the future in their careers or academics.

Previously, BA degree holders had to prepare for difficult job interviews, but due to changes in the employment market, students now have more options.

Students will learn about many social, financial, environmental, and functional elements through this course. The fundamentals of this course with upcoming digital skills are communication and functionality.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Role

Job Description

Foreign Language Instructor

In schools and universities, they are in charge of instructing and leading pupils.





Translator

They are in charge of translating the language for others who do not speak it.

Content Editor

They are responsible for content management, language and grammar, and editing.

Actor

Actors in films, television shows, and commercials mostly work in front of the camera.





Theater Artist

Works on a live stage setting with live audiences, primarily in open theater or plays.

Dramatherapist

To attain therapeutic goals, use drama or the theatrical process.

Director

Responsible for the creation of a motion picture, whether it be a film, a play, a commercial, or a television series.





What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



In India, graduates with a Bachelor's degree in Foreign Languages have the option of working or continuing their education. An additional degree increases career prospects and adds value. Graduates can choose to specialize in a particular field. The following are some of the higher education options:



MA Foreign Languages

M.Phil Foreign Languages

PhD

Certificate courses

2 Lac to 4Lac

4lac to 6 Lac

3 Lac to 5 Lac

5 Lac to 8 Lac

2 Lac to 4 Lac

5 to 6.5 Lac

6.5 to 10Lac

In India, admission to BA Foreign Languages programmes is primarily through entrance tests. BA Foreign Language entrance exams are normally held at the university level. Applicants for entrance tests are typically:



JNUEE- The JNUEE (Jawaharlal Nehru University Entrance Examination) is an All India Level Entrance Examination for admission to JNU. For all programmes of study, excluding M.Phil. and Ph.D., the final selection of candidates will be based on their performance in the computer-based test. Candidates should attend the Viva-voce for M.Phil. and Ph.D. selection.

title: BA in Psychology. docx
eligibility: The Christ University Entrance Test is held to get admission to Christ University's programmes.
details:
BACHELORS IN PSYCHOLOGY

What is the BA psychology course about?

BA in psychology stands for bachelor of arts in psychology.

It is a three years undergraduate program which emphasizes understanding Human behaviour, emotions, Human development, personality, etc. It also offers a conceptual understanding of Organizational behaviour, needs for statistical data etc.







It is one of the growing choices of students from every stream.

Overview of Bachelors in psychology

Level - Undergraduate

Duration- 3 years

Course fee- INR 3000- 3.15L

Minimum Qualification –Must have completed  10+2 with any stream from a recognised board where some universities even look for a minimum 50% score in 10+2	

Selection Process- Merit-based or entrance based

Difference between BA in psychology and a BSc in psychology-

Both BA and BSc psychology are undergraduate programs offered by various universities but in some states, Psychology falls under a science subject whereas in some it falls under the humanities stream. For example, in Odisha, we have a BA in psychology which is considered a humanities subject.



Apart from this, there is a bit of difference between a BA in psychology and a BSc in psychology. Students who pursue BA in psychology have to read both the scientific and philosophical aspects of psychology. 

This includes child psychology, Organisational psychology, health psychology etc whereas students who pursue a BSc in psychology study the mathematical and scientific aspects of psychology.



Students pursuing BA in psychology can opt for fields such as Law, Journalism, Clinical psychology, Human resource whereas those with a BSc in psychology can opt for fields such as research and methodology, lecturer, psychologist, IT companies etc,



Apart from this, the duration for both the courses is the same 3 years.

Why should you study BA in Psychology -

1- Growing needs for mental health professionals

There’s a huge need for mental health professionals to know the human mind and behaviour.

Apart from this, it will help to eradicate the stigma related to this field.

2- Exploring the World of “Why"

A bit of psychology is understanding why a human behaves in a certain situation. They explore the reason behind each activity.

3- Great Career Prospects

Psychology needs a bit more experience and study qualifications for jobs such as clinical psychologists or therapists. But for exploring another career such as HR, Law, or a mental health activist in an NGO

you need to have a minimum bachelor’s degree in psychology.

4- A big room for self-development and understanding

Psychologists try to apply things to themselves. For being a good psychologist or therapist or counsellor a person needs to understand themselves first. 

This will help you to talk about your issues and communicate with others and also healthily express your emotions.

Career opportunities with their salary packages for students after pursuing a bachelor's in psychology

Those who have completed their bachelors in psychology can opt for Civil Service, trainee in IT companies, NGO workers etc. However to be a psychologist, therapist or counsellor you should at least have a Master's Degree in psychology.

Profession

 Description

Sales Representative

A student in a BA Psychology programme develops key interpersonal abilities that can be used in various sales and marketing professions.



Average annual salary- Rs 3-4 lack

Advertising Agents

Persuasion is a fundamental aspect of psychology education and can be used effectively in advertising-related occupations.



Average annual salary- Rs 4-5 lack



Career Counsellors

A degree in psychology comes in handy when working as a career counsellor as it requires understanding the skills and potentials of an individual and guiding them towards their right career path.



Average annual salary- Rs 3-4 lack



Recruiter

A psychology graduate can also succeed as a company recruiter because it necessitates the ability to recognise an individual's talents and skills and apply them to the benefit of the corporation.     



Average Annual Salary- 10-14 LPA for permanent govt. Colleges



Social Worker

A bachelor in Psychology can find employment at NGOs and rehabilitation centres. This function includes assessing a community's psychological needs, offering counselling services, and so on.



Average annual salary- Rs 2-3 lack



Teacher	

One can readily enter the teaching profession after earning a bachelor's degree in psychology. In India, however, having a B.Ed. degree boosts your chances of finding work as a teacher.



Average annual salary- 





Higher studies after BA in psychology -

Higher Studies courses in Psychology -

Master of Arts in Applied Psychology

Master of Arts in Psychology

Master of Arts in Counseling Psychology

M.Phil. courses in Psychology

Master of Philosophy in Applied Psychology

Master of Philosophy in Psychology

Master of Philosophy in Clinical Psychology

Master of

title: BA MEDIA STUDIES
details:
BA MEDIA STUDIES



What is BA Media Studies?



Critical studies, communication and marketing, and interactive narrative are all included in the BA in Media Studies. Students can master the principles of critical theory while researching and analysing the historical evolution of the media and entertainment sector.

Communication and marketing courses teach students how to conceive, construct, and utilise numerous media tools and approaches. 

To engage audiences and customers in all parts of media, these strategies incorporate design, psychology, and mass communication. Traditional types of storytelling and production methods, as well as contemporary approaches like game design and transmedia story creation, are covered in interactive narrative, which allows students to develop their own unique intellectual assets as part of the curriculum.



BA Media Studies: Course Highlights

Course level

Undergraduate

Full form

Bachelor of Arts

Duration

3 years

Examination type

Semester

Eligibility

passed 10+2 from a recognized board of secondary education.

Admission process

Based on marks scored in 10+2 (or) entrance exam followed by an interview. 

Average fee

INR 10,000 – INR 3,00,000 (PA)

Average salary

INR 12,000 – INR 25,000 ( as a fresh graduate per month)

Top companies in placements

Hindustan Times, The Times Group, The Pioneer, The Hindu, Malayala Manorama, The India Today Group, Outlook, Jagran Prakashan Group, India TV, Zee Network, Network 18, NDTV, All India Radio (AIR), Doordarshan, Indian Express, Star India, Sahara One Media & Entertainment Limited, Balaji Telefilms Limited



Why should you opt for this course?

Bachelor of arts is often regarded as the most comfortable stream because of the abundance of colleges/universities, courses, and affordability for the majority of the people.

It offers greater job prospects, higher education chances, and a wealth of information and potential. Jobs and earnings may differ based on the specialised course and the educational board through which it is studied.

Fields under this course



Journalism

Broadcasting

Publishing and editing 

Documentaries 

Public relation

Event management 

Advertising

Film Making 

Career opportunities after completing this course 

One can work professionally after earning a bachelor's degree in media studies. Many firms provide recent grads opportunities; while this may not result in a high income, it does provide opportunities to develop skills and get valuable job experience.

Marketing, producer, manager, journalist, editor, event manager, director, and more positions are available.

In the media, government, think tanks, and other institutions, as a writer, critic, and analyst

Publishing Production Assistant (print, electronic, and internet publishing)

Media Buying, Marketing, Advertising, Public Relations, and Event Planning Project Assistant

In New Media businesses, Project Assistant (cloud computing, online advertising and online gaming)

Cross-Cultural/International Media Business Development Coordinator

Long-distance learning allows students to work while studying.

In long-distance studies, a candidate must attend on test days or as needed by the college.

Higher studies after completing BA media studies

MA: It is a postgraduate graduate programme that lasts between one and three years. Working professionals who wish to advance quickly in their careers and expand their areas of competence can obtain an MA.

PhD: A PhD is the second program option for students who desire to continue in the same field of education. It is a three-to- five year programme with qualifying requirements including a master's degree in a relevant field.

What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for this course?

Job Profile

Annual Salary

Journalist

INR 1.8-3.6 Lakh

writer

INR 1.8-5 Lakh

Editor

INR 1.2-4.5 Lakh

Marketing Director

INR 2.5-4 Lakh

The Digital Content Creator

INR 3-6Lakh



Top entrance exam to get into your desired college for this course

CUCET: The Common University Entrance Test, formerly known as the Central Universities Common Entrance Test, is an all-India test administered by the National Testing Agency for admission to 45 Central Universities of India's Undergraduate, Integrated, Postgraduate, Diploma, Certification, and Research Programs.



DUET: This is conducted by Delhi University for their various courses.



BHU UET: NTA administers the Banaras Hindu University Undergraduate Entrance Test (BHU UET) on behalf of the university (BHU). It is a national entrance exam for admission to BHU's undergraduate programs.



PU CET: The Punjab University Common Entrance Test (PU CET) is a state-level entrance exam held by Punjab University in Chandigarh for individuals seeking admission to various undergraduate and graduate programmes. Undergraduate courses are covered by PU CET UG.



Top colleges in India offering BA media studies

title: BA PUBLIC ADMINISTRATION
details:
BA PUBLIC ADMINISTRATION



What is this course?



The BA Public Administration course covers a wide range of topics in administration, including public services, public organizations, and the constitutional framework.

Students pursuing a BA in Public Administration learn about themes such as secularism, equality, law and order, and justice, as well as how these principles are reflected in Public Administration policies and other government policies.

The course provides information on the mechanisms used by important political institutions and agencies to formulate, implement, and regulate diverse public policies.

The current political, economic, and social conditions are also discussed. It also entails researching those who actively participate in the policymaking process, such as interest groups, expert communities, the media, and government agencies.





The course equips multitasking professionals to examine the prevalent ideas currently influencing the creation and successful implementation of public policies.

By managing administrative policies, the course aims to effectively improve public life in the social sphere.





Highlights Of this course:



Course

BA Public Administration

Full form

Bachelor of Arts in Public Administration

Course Level

Undergraduate

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester based

Eligibility

10+2 from a recognised board with a minimum of 50% in the aggregate.



Fields/ Streams of the course-

 Homeland Security. 

Non-Profit Management. 

International Affairs. 

Health Field.

Criminal Justice.

Urban Development.

 Transportation Systems.



Why should you study this course?



In terms of personal growth and job potential, the BA in Public Administration course is one of the most popular in India. There are numerous advantages to taking the course. The following are some of the advantages and benefits of taking this course.



The training fosters knowledge improvement while also providing prospects for job advancement.

Students who have exceptional communication skills, excellent computer abilities, are skilled at observing the smallest of details, and are familiar with Public Administration rules and regulations should take this course.

The focus of the course is on both the theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. Its curriculum includes a variety of coursework, projects, and internships that give students real-world experience.

The course offers a good salary package ranging from INR 3 to 5 lakhs.

The course opens up various career opportunities in front of students. Students can choose to work in the public sector. Apart from this, yet another option for students is to pursue higher education.







What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



The following are some of the higher education alternatives available to students:



Public Administration Master's Degree

PhD in Public Administration

 Master of Business Administration (MBA)





Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profile

Job Description

Corporate Operations Manager

The Corporate Operations Manager oversees the association's operations and gives expert advice, notably on how to run and manage a business.

Administrative Officer

Any organization's seamless operation is the responsibility of an administrative officer. He or she is in charge of processing, managing, coordinating, and resolving employee issues over remuneration.

Business consultants

Various parts of business administration, such as human resource policy and pharmaceutical services, are handled by business consultants. They offer valuable business advice to customers or clients in order to help them improve various elements of their organization.

Public Relations and Fundraising Manager

Managers of public relations and fundraising research the country's current political, social, and economic conditions. They provide valuable campaign and fundraising counsel to their consumers after conducting thorough study and analysis of such trends.

details:
BA STATISTICS



What is this course?



BA Statistics is a three-year undergraduate degree programme that covers a wide range of topics, as shown below:



The course teaches students how to collect the fundamentals of research.

It focuses on survey data and extracts useful information from it for a company's or organization's business and financial gain.



The course also assists students in learning about various facets of mathematics.

Learners are given excellent placement possibilities in both domestic and international industries.

The training allows students to improve their technical and mathematical abilities.

title: BA Sociology
description: The Bachelor of Arts (BA) in Sociology is a three-year programme that spans six semesters.
eligibility: Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?
details:
BA SOCIOLOGY



What is this course?



The Bachelor of Arts (BA) in Sociology is a three-year programme that spans six semesters.

 The course covers a variety of topics in the social environment, such as the psychological nature of men and women and the boundaries that exist between social classes. To put it another way, sociology is the systematic study of society, culture, and the relationships between different social levels.







 It also explains the connections between different people, their experiences, and their perspectives.



Highlights of this course:



The eligibility requirements for BA sociology are 10+2 in any stream from a recognised board with an aggregate of at least 50% and English as one of the primary courses.

St. Xavier's College, Fergusson College, Christ University, Loyola College, and Mount Carmel College are among the best BA Sociology schools.

Distance Education is also accessible for BA sociology, with major colleges like IGNOU, Tamil Nadu Open University, and Annamalai University.

Average Course Fee for BA Sociology: INR 3,000-INR 3,00,000

After completing a BA in sociology, the typical pay varies from INR 3 LPA to INR 8 LPA.

The top career positions connected with BA sociology include counselor, lecturer, community service officer, teacher, social researcher, welfare rights advisor, and others.



Why should you study this course?









According to recruiters, BA Sociology is the most favored degree for pursuing a career as a sociologist, social worker, or counselor.

According to the US Bureau of Labor Statistics, employment prospects for sociologists are expected to grow by 4% from 2019 to 2029, which is faster than many other occupations.

Annual salaries for sociologists might reach INR 7 lakhs. With an average income of INR 8,32,500 in India, human resource manager is the most paid position after BA sociology.



Sociologists can work in non-governmental organizations, various welfare organizations, and in non-traditional roles.



Students interested in pursuing professional, MA/MS, and/or PhD degrees in business, development studies, law, psychology, sociology, urban planning, and other social sciences can benefit greatly from a BA in sociology.



Fields / Streams of the course-



Students studying B.A. Sociology is immersed in the study of society.



 Demography

Community

 Crime & Delinquency

Collective Behavior

 Cultural Sociology

 Comparative Sociology

  Applied Sociology



These are some of the specific subjects covered by Sociology.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profile 

Job Description 

Social Worker

A social worker watches to people's and their families' diverse needs. They work in the best interests of people and strive to implement social improvements..

Counselor 

A counselor enters a person's head and investigates his or her feelings and emotions. He or she gives their patients the appropriate instructions to enable them to live a better life.

Journalist

A journalist draughts and edits articles for publication in newspapers, periodicals, and journals. Through their writings, they hope to capture the interest of the readers.

Sociologist

A sociologist examines civilizations, groups, and social communities to study social behavior. They also look into the various levels of mankind and how they interact with society.

Content Writer

A content writer is someone who creates content in such a way that it receives the most traffic possible. They make certain that their clients' content is relevant to their customers' needs.

Public Relations Officer

Public relations officers are in charge of preserving a positive image for the company for which they work. They do so by launching campaigns and engaging in other public relations efforts.













What can we do further/Higher after studying this course?



After BA in sociology, you can opt for Masters in Sociology, Masters in Social Work and Masters in Rural Development. They can opt for MBA, Journalism, Mass Communication etc. 









What are the entry level salary packages & average salary packages for this course graduates?

Annual Average Salary

INR 2.5 to 7.24 LPA

INR 2.94 to 7 LPA

INR 3.31 to 8.62 LPA

Sociologist

INR 1 to 1.2 LPA

Content Writer

INR 2.43 to 4.82 LPA

Public Relations Officer

INR 2.38 to 4.79 LPA

title: BA in Journalism and Mass Communication
description: Bachelor of Arts in Journalism and Mass Communication
details:
Bachelor of Arts in Journalism and Mass Communication



What is this course?



The Bachelor of Arts in Journalism and Mass Communication is a three-year undergraduate programme that equips students with the technical skills and social understanding necessary to write about and communicate information about business, history, culture, budget issues, governmental matters, entertainment, and sports to the general public through mass media such as television, radio, and web-based social networks.





Because journalism is focused on truth, autonomy, and transparency, the course provides a holistic knowledge on both an academic and practical level.



Overview of the course-



Course Level

Graduation

Full Form 

Bachelor of Arts in Journalism and Mass Communication

Duration

3 years

Examination Type 

Semester-based

Eligibility

10+2 education or equivalent, in any stream, with a minimum score of 50%

Admission Process

Merit-based/Entrance-based

Average Course Fee (INR)

2,000 - 5 Lakh



Why should you opt for this course?



The programme equips students with the knowledge and skills necessary to work in a range of media companies and with various forms of mass communication technology.

Students can learn problem-solving, interviewing, investigative, and reporting skills, as well as an analytical approach to themes and trends, research skills, and networking skills.

Students will study the principles of mass communication as well as contemporary media trends and difficulties, as well as current technological developments and advances in journalism.

Graduates are exposed to a wide range of print, electronic, and new-age media, as well as a wide range of mass communication technologies, including computer programmes, software, and hardware for print, radio, television, and the internet.

Students learn how to plan, create, design, and strategize high-quality media content for television, radio, film, and a variety of digital platforms such as social media. 

This degree programme aids in refining abilities to acquire, understand, communicate, and present information to a large audience, and one's profession is more revolutionised in non-metro cities due to the spectacular expansion of regional media and the emergence of new media businesses and formats.



Fields related to this course-



The following mentioned fields are available after bachelors in journalism and mass communication: 

Journalism

Public Relations

Corporate Communications

Advertising

Marketing

Graphics

Career options after completing the course-

The following are some excellent  job opportunities after completing this course

Job Position

Description

Feature Writer

Writers for magazines, newspapers, and blogs cover themes and produce features by conducting research.

Photojournalist

Captures, edits, and displays images in a storytelling fashion for publication in many organizations throughout the world or on their own publishing company.

Radio Jockey

Work at private and internet radio stations, where you can talk about music, sports, movies, and current events.

Public Relations Officer

To build effective public relations with stakeholders, the media, and the general public, develops strategies and campaigns and generates press releases and promotional materials.





Higher studies after completing this course-

Students with a BA in Journalism and Mass Communication may pursue further studies. The following are some of the possibilities:

Graduates who want to learn more about journalism and mass communication can get a master's degree in journalism and mass communication or one of its specialities, then go on to get an M.Phil. or a PhD in journalism and mass communication.

Graduates can also pursue academic careers and/or contribute to the large body of knowledge in the field of journalism and mass communication by conducting relevant research.

Candidates may consider studying theater and film studies, literary studies, graphic design, media management, and other topics such as sociology, political science, anthropology, psychology, economics, and religion, all of which are relevant to journalism and mass communication.

What is this course?



The Bachelor of Arts in Journalism and Mass Communication is a three-year undergraduate programme that equips students with the technical skills and social understanding necessary to write about and communicate information about business, history, culture, budget issues, governmental matters, entertainment, and sports to the general public through mass media such as television, radio, and web-based social networks.





Because journalism is focused on truth, autonomy, and transparency, the course provides a holistic knowledge on both an academic and practical level.



Overview of the course-

Graduation

Why should you opt for this course?



The programme equips students with the knowledge and skills necessary to work in a range of media companies and with various forms of mass communication technology.

Students can learn problem-solving, interviewing, investigative, and reporting skills, as well as an analytical approach to themes and trends, research skills, and networking skills.

Students will study the principles of mass communication as well as contemporary media trends and difficulties, as well as current technological developments and advances in journalism.

title: BA Philosophy
details:
BA Philosophy 

What is BA Philosophy?

BA Students who are masterminds or critical thinkers should study philosophy. All important topics of Indian and Western philosophy are covered in this course.



Philosophy is a field of study that investigates the fundamental problems of human existence. Human knowledge, moral and scientific reasoning, art, and society are all concerns.

This course is essential for careers in law, business, and government administration.

Students who are interested in a study in Arts and frequently find themselves contemplating questions of life and existence should choose a Bachelor of Arts in Philosophy.





Overview of the course -

Course Level

Undergraduate

Full-form

Bachelor of arts in philosophy

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester / year wise

Eligibility

10+2 (minimum 50% marks )and relaxation to SC/ST/OBC

Admission process

Merit based selection or entrance exam

Course Fee

INR 5,000- INR 1,50,000

Average Salary

INR 1.56 lakh – INR 10 lakh





Fields related to this course-



Following are the fields related to this course:



Metaphysics

The fundamental essence of reality and existence is discussed. Questions about the existence of things and what causes them to exist.

Epistemology

Deals with the nature and foundations of knowledge, its boundaries and validity, justification, rationality of belief, and other related topics.

Ethics

Systematizing, defending, and proposing conceptions of good and bad behavior are all part of this job.

Aesthetics

It discusses the nature of beauty and taste, as well as the aesthetic philosophy of art.

Political Philosophy

The study of themes such as liberty, justice, property, rights, laws, and the authority's enforcement of laws: what they are, why they are required, and why they are enforced.

Logic

The study of forms and inference, or the relationships that lead to acceptance of a thesis, as well as the examination and evaluation of arguments.

Philosophy of Science

Science's basis, techniques, and implications are discussed. The study of what constitutes science, the validity of scientific hypotheses, and science's ultimate goal.



Why should you opt for this course?



Students who desire to take a Philosophy course will benefit from this course. It gives them the opportunity to pursue a profession in an entirely other industry.

The course provides in-depth information on how to live a healthy lifestyle. In everyday life, one may learn about the theoretical and practical components of philosophy.

The students will learn a variety of abilities over the course. Critical and logical thinking, analytical skills, decision-making, problem-solving skills, and so on are examples of such abilities.

Candidates who want to pursue a BA in Philosophy have another choice. Instead of looking for work, they may continue their education. There are several postgraduate courses available in the Arts discipline. Any relevant course can be chosen based on one's interests.



Career options after completing this course-



Following are best job scope after graduating from BA philosophy 



Job Profiles

Job Description

HR executive

A Philosophy graduate with a foundation in ethics and philosophy is proficient in people skills and hence ideal for a career as an HR Executive.

Sales Executive

A philosophy degree aids in comprehending societal requirements, giving it an ideal foundation for the position of Sales Executive.

Teacher

Those with a talent for imparting information might work as teachers in schools.

PR & Advertising Executive

Students in the BA Philosophy programme gain an educated perspective on life and society, which prepares them for a career in public relations and advertising.

Social Worker

Philosophy graduates understand about a society's ethics and intellectual process, making them good candidates for social work.



Higher studies after BA philosophy-



A philosophy graduate has several options for further education in addition to working.

An MA in Philosophy is an obvious choice, and it is accessible at all major Indian universities that offer postgraduate degrees. A philosophy graduate can also pursue an MA in English Literature, which covers several fields of philosophy as well as their impact on literature.

An MBA in Human Resource Management or an MBA in Marketing are two options for those seeking a more professional choice.



What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for BA Philosophy graduates



Job Profiles

Average Annual Salary

HR executive

INR 2 Lakhs

Sales Executive

INR 3 Lakhs

Teacher

INR 2 Lakhs

INR 2.9 Lakhs

INR 2.5 Lakhs

title: Ba history
description: BA in History, often known as Bachelor of History, is a three-year undergraduate programme that covers the three major periods of history: Ancient, Post-Classical, and Modern.
details:
B.A. HISTORY





What is this course?



BA in History, often known as Bachelor of History, is a three-year undergraduate programme that covers the three major periods of history: Ancient, Post-Classical, and Modern.

 BA in History is one of the most popular degrees in India, and it is taught in a number of colleges.

 Ancient history, European history, world politics, and other topics are covered in the BA in History curriculum.



Highlights Of this Course:



Course Name 

BA History

Full-Form 

Bachelor of Arts in History 

Level of Course 

Undergraduate (UG)

Duration 

3 years 

Eligibility 

10+2 qualified students with at least 50% aggregate marks

Admission Process 

Merit + Entrance based 

Examination Type 

Semester Exams 





Fields/ Streams of the course-





Ancient Indian History.

Medieval India.

Transformation from Modern to Present India.

Ancient World History.

Political Theory.

Pre and Post Colonization.

USA and USSR History.

European Integration and Transformation.



Why should you study this Course?

Transferable Abilities: History classes provide students with a diverse set of skills that are appropriate for a variety of careers. Analytical abilities are necessary for any employment area; the ability to analyze and prioritize data is critical in making decisions.

Relevant Content: History is a discipline that isn't just about the past; it also delves into significant subjects from present history. This aids pupils in comprehending current events, as everything that occurs now is influenced by what has occurred previously.

A degree in history from a reputable and respected university might be striking and desirable on your CV.A history degree can lead to a variety of intriguing occupations, such as archaeologist or historian.

Learn from our mistakes: The best teacher for each individual is his or her own mistakes. History instructs us in a way by stating the errors that have been made and the consequences that have resulted, allowing us to make good decisions based on prior experience.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



Students with a BA in History can pursue a variety of careers-

MA History or Master of Arts

Master's Degree in History (Global History and International Relations)

Master of Philosophy in History (M.Phil.)

History Ph.D. or Doctor of Philosophy in History

History Certificate

Archaeology diploma

Epigraphy Certificate

Museology Certificate

Museum Administration Diploma, Museum Modeling

Archaeology, Ancient Culture, and History Postgraduate Diploma

Museology and Archaeology Postgraduate Diploma

Postgraduate Certificate in Archival Management



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profile

Job Description

Archaeologist

An archaeologist's work is to investigate human history and prehistory by excavating sites and analyzing ancient remains and artifacts.

Historian

A historian is someone who researches and writes about history. They are well renowned for their methodological and narrative representations of history.

Auctioneer

An auctioneer is a person who oversees an auction and is in charge of presenting items for bidding.

Teacher

After completing the BA History degree, one can work as a History Teacher, assisting students in learning about India's and the world's history.

Archivist

An archivist's profession includes evaluating, collecting, and preserving historical records.



What is the entry level salary packages & average salary packages for this course graduates?



Job Profile

Average Salary (INR)

Archaeologists

3,50,000 - 4,80,000

Historians

6,00,000 - 8,00,000

Teachers

3,80,000 - 6,50,000

Auctioneers

5,00,000 - 7,00,000

Archivist

5,50,000 - 8,00,000





Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?



Students are admitted to BA History programmes at some schools and universities depending on their achievement on the entrance exams. The following are some of them:



JNUEE 2022: The Jawaharlal Nehru University conducts the JNUEE for several undergraduate and postgraduate degrees.

IPU CET 2022 is administered by the Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University. This exam is taken entirely online. This is a university entrance exam.

DSAT 2022: The Dayananda Sagar University administers the DSAT, or Dayananda Sagar Admission Test.

title: Ba political science
details:
B.A. POLITICAL SCIENCE







What is this course? 



The numerous topics related to politics are the focus of BA Political Science. The course covers diverse governance systems, economics, sociology, international relations, global politics, psychology, and other topics.

The course gives students an understanding of how governments work and how to assess and analyze political systems. This course is designed for students who are interested in politics and learning about a country's administrative features.

The entire course is centered on the study of governance. It assists students in identifying the critical decision-making component of society as well as the social, economic, and other institutions and practices that influence this process.

The course covers not only current political approaches, but also political development throughout history and different political revolutions around the world.



Highlights of this Course:



Course Level

Undergraduate

Duration of the course

3 years

Number of Universities

Over 300

Eligibility

The student must have received at least 50% in their senior secondary exams from a recognised board.



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Civil Service

Politics

Political Scientist 

Political Scientist 

Social Assistance

Journalism

Marketing

Teaching





Why should you study this Course?



The course provides in-depth knowledge of numerous aspects of political science, such as governance, law and order, socio-economic activities, and so on.

In the government sector, students can find attractive work opportunities.

The course prepares students for rewarding careers in domains such as federal, state, and municipal government, law, business and international organizations, non-profit groups and organizations, electoral politics and polling, journalism, research, and teaching.

Students who pursue a BA in Political Science have the option of continuing their education in a relevant subject after graduation. In any case, their understanding of the issue grows.

Students who want to pass the civil services exam can benefit from the training.They can cover a large percentage of the entrance syllabus with the help of the training.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course- 



After completing a BA in Political Science, one can continue further education. MA Political Science, MA in International Relations, and other academic options are available.

Aside from the usual M.A. possibilities, there is also the option of studying law. After graduating with a bachelor's degree in political science, students can pursue a three-year LLB programme.

After the course, you have the option of continuing your education with an MBA. There are numerous specialties available in MBA as well. An MBA in Marketing, an MBA in Finance, and an MBA in Human Resources are among them.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profiles

Job Description

Budget Analyst

A budget analyst assists firms with budgeting. They also provide politicians with policy analyses and legal counsel.

Public Relations and Fundraising Manager

As these elements have a direct impact on any organization, the Public Relations and Fundraising Manager keeps an eye on the current social, political, and economic challenges. They do thorough study of such trends and make recommendations to their organizations regarding campaigns and fundraising methods.

Political Scientist

The primary responsibility of political scientists is to examine government processes and make recommendations for essential policy changes. To resolve specific challenges, these scientists sometimes rely on public opinion polls.

Political Campaign Manager

A Political Campaign Manager is in charge of hiring and supervising numerous campaign duties. This necessitates thorough research and polling.

Market Researcher

A market researcher obtains data on customer perceptions of a company's products or services. Market researchers may speak with clients to learn about their business objectives and strategy.

The course provides in-depth knowledge of numerous aspects of political science, such as governance, law and order, socio-economic activities, and so on.

In the government sector, students can find attractive work opportunities.

The course prepares students for rewarding careers in domains such as federal, state, and municipal government, law, business and international organizations, non-profit groups and organizations, electoral politics and polling, journalism, research, and teaching.

Students who pursue a BA in Political Science have the option of continuing their education in a relevant subject after graduation. In any case, their understanding of the issue grows.

Students who want to pass the civil services exam can benefit from the training.They can cover a large percentage of the entrance syllabus with the help of the training.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course- 



After completing a BA in Political Science, one can continue further education. MA Political Science, MA in International Relations, and other academic options are available.

Aside from the usual M.A. possibilities, there is also the option of studying law. After graduating with a bachelor's degree in political science, students can pursue a three-year LLB programme.

After the course, you have the option of continuing your education with an MBA. There are numerous specialties available in MBA as well. An MBA in Marketing, an MBA in Finance, and an MBA in Human Resources are among them.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profiles

Job Description

Budget Analyst

A budget analyst assists firms with budgeting. They also provide politicians with policy analyses and legal counsel.

Public Relations and Fundraising Manager

As these elements have a direct impact on any organization, the Public Relations and Fundraising Manager keeps an eye on the current social, political, and economic challenges. They do thorough study of such trends and make recommendations to their organizations regarding campaigns and fundraising methods.

Political Scientist

The primary responsibility of political scientists is to examine government processes and make recommendations for essential policy changes. To resolve specific challenges, these scientists sometimes rely on public opinion polls.

Political Campaign Manager

A Political Campaign Manager is in charge of hiring and supervising numerous campaign duties. This necessitates thorough research and polling.

Market Researcher

A market researcher obtains data on customer perceptions of a company's products or services. Market researchers may speak with clients to learn about their business objectives and strategy.

INR 6,00,000

Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?



The majority of BA political science admissions are based on merit. However, some institutions use admission tests as part of their selection process. Exams for entrance to a BA in political science include:

NPAT: The NMIMS university entrance exam for admission to B.A. Political Science and other Under Graduate programmes at NMIMS University Mumbai and its other campuses is the NPAT exam.

BHU UET: NTA administers the Banaras Hindu University Undergraduate Entrance Test (BHU UET) on behalf of the university (BHU). It is a national entrance exam for admission to BHU's undergraduate programmes.

PUCET: The Punjab University Common Entrance Test (PU CET) is a state-level entrance exam held by Punjab University in Chandigarh for individuals seeking admission to various undergraduate and graduate programmes. Undergraduate courses are covered by PU CET UG.

TISS BAT: TISS BAT is a Tata Institute of Social Science entrance exam for admission to undergraduate programmes at several TISS campuses.

JNUEE: The Jawaharlal Nehru University Entrance Examination (JNUEE) is the All India Level Entrance Examination conducted for admission to JNU.

title: BA Physical education
duration: Course Duration of Bachelor of Arts [BA] (Physical Education) is 3 Years.
details:
BA PHYSICAL EDUCATION



What is BA physical education??



This course covers all aspects of science pertaining to physical knowledge and abilities, as well as their application.

This curriculum is meant to provide students with a good foundation in physical education, and it includes topics such as physical fitness concepts and fundamental anatomy.





A bachelor's degree in physical education is a hands-on study of physical fitness, including leisure exercise, sports, and health.

Students will learn about bodily growth, strength, physical coordination, and agility in this subject.



Overview of this course-

Degree

Bachelors

Full Form

Bachelor of Arts in Physical Education

Duration

Course Duration of Bachelor of Arts [BA] (Physical Education) is 3 Years.

Age

No specific age

Minimum Percentage

50%

Subjects Required

10+2 or equivalent examination in any stream

Average Fees Incurred

INR 6000 - 1 LPA

Similar Options of Study

B.Sc (Physical Education), B.P.Ed, M.P.Ed etc.

Average Salary Offered

INR 3.51 LPA



Why should you opt for BA physical education??





BA Physical Education students are prepared to take on a variety of leadership and administrative responsibilities in the healthcare, fitness, and physical education fields.

To ensure that applicants understand the material, the programme includes a combination of practical tasks and research papers.

Students are taught subjects like Analysis of Research in Physical Education, Human Physiology, Motor Development and Behaviour, Physiology of Exercise, and Sports and Performance Psychology over the course of three years to provide them with the fundamentals as well as the essentials of the human body.

Pupils in the BA Physical Education programme are taught to be hard on themselves since they will have to do the same to their customers and students in the future if they want to be successful in physical training.

Feels related to this course-

Major fields related to this course

Adapted physical education

applied sport science

athletic training

human performance

Ergonomics

sport administration

Physical activity 

fitness and wellness are among the concentration areas accessible to graduate degree applicants.

Career opportunities after graduating from this course:-

A Physical Education degree qualifies a candidate for employment in the government or the business sector, as well as the opportunity to compete in numerous competitions and leagues.

If they have the appropriate schooling, all meritorious athletes are immediately selected for Group 'C' and 'D' positions.

Private companies can also hire a great athlete to promote their sporting goods and equipment.

A BA Physical Education degree may earn you a minimum income of INR 4,00,000 to INR 10,00,000 per year.

Schools, colleges, universities, sports academies, and fitness centres are among the top employers of BA Physical Education graduates.



Higher Education after BA Physical Education



M.P.Ed. :

If you want to stay in the same sector of education, a Master of Physical Education is the best option. It's a two-year programme with prerequisites including a bachelor's degree in physical education.



MBA: 

A big percentage of BA graduates choose to pursue a PGDM or MBA programme in order to seek a career in management. A nationwide entrance exam is used to determine admission. A BA degree combined with an MBA in a chosen field is very desirable, and many companies actively seek such personnel. Examine the best MBA and PGDM colleges in India.

title: BA environmental science
details:
B.A. ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCE





What is this course?



Environmental Studies is an interdisciplinary course that covers issues from geology, physics, chemistry, biology, ecology, earth science, and a variety of other disciplines. The course focuses on the environment and the various processes that occur on the planet. Global warming, pollution, floods, earthquakes, waste management, and sustainable development have all been addressed in depth throughout the course.



 Environmental Studies courses are available at all levels, from undergraduate to PhD. Because of its relevance and professional potential, the Environmental Studies subject has recently become increasingly popular among students.



Highlights Of this Course:



Course Level

Undergraduate

Full Form

Bachelors of Arts in Environmental Science

Duration

3 years

Eligibility

Passed 10+2 with a minimum score of 50% from a recognised board. Some colleges may also administer an entrance examination.

Admission Process

Merit-Based or Direct





Fields/ Streams of the course- 



Environmental science specializations.

Soil ecology. 

Aquatic/marine biology. 

Earth systems. 

Environmental and biological conservation. 

Energy and climate change.

Water resource management. 

Pollution prevention and remediation.

Why should you study this Course?

Environmental science teaches us how to protect the environment in the face of rising human population and anthropogenic activities that destroy natural resources and ecosystems.

The study and understanding of natural and human-caused processes in our natural environment is promoted by environmental science. Students build and improve critical thinking, problem-solving, and creative thinking skills by researching and investigating challenging environmental challenges.

What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-

Diploma in Environmental Science

Diploma in Environmental Law

Diploma in Environment Protection

Master of Philosophy in Environmental Sciences

Post Graduate Diploma in Environment

Master of Science (M.Sc) in Environmental Management



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profile 

Job Description

Environmental Consultant

As an environmental consultant, your duty is to analyze data from specific firms/companies and make recommendations for reducing environmental damage.

Environment Journalist

As an environment journalist, your goal is to report on key environmental news, such as new laws and policies, conferences, and other environmental programmes.

Environment Photographer

As an environmental photographer, you'll be taking photos for publications and newspapers about the environment.

Lecturer

Environmental studies must be taught in colleges and universities as a professor.

Pollution Control Officer

As a pollution control officer, you must analyze pollution levels and relevant data, write a report, and inform the appropriate authorities.

Director of Waste Management

As a waste management director, you must ensure that all of your department's activities are completed correctly, as well as facilitate waste management methods and technology.



What are the entry level salary packages & average salary packages for this course graduates? 



Job Profile

Annual Salary (Approx.)

Environment Photographer

Rs. 2,50,000 – Rs. 4,00,000

Pollution Control Officer

Rs. 3,60,000 – Rs. 8,00,000

Lecturer

Rs. 2,40,000 – Rs. 8,00,000

Director of Waste Management

Rs. 4,00,000 – Rs. 6,00,000

Environment Journalist

Rs. 2,00,000 – Rs. 4,60,000



Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?



Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) Entrance Examination: JNU is one of India's most prestigious universities, with a diverse range of post-graduate programmes. Environmental Studies M.Sc. and doctoral programmes at JNU include entrance tests.

 Banaras Hindu University (BHU) Entrance Examination: In India, Banaras Hindu University is well-known for its courses. It provides an M.Sc. in Environmental Studies programme, and students must take the BHU entrance test to be considered for admission.

 Forest Research Institute (FRI) Entrance Exam: The FRI Entrance Exam is a state-level exam that is taken to gain admission to postgraduate courses at FRI Dehradun, including the M.Sc. in Environmental Studies.

 Indian Institute of Science (IISc): The Indian Institute of Science (IISc) is a prestigious institute in India that offers a wide range of science courses, including environmental studies. JEE Main, JEE Advanced, or KVPY are used to determine admission to the institute. There are no separate entrance exams.



List of top colleges in India related to this course-



IGNOU, Delhi

LPU, Jalandhar

Jadavpur University, Kolkata

UNIPUNE ( Pune University)

DU, Delhi

Integral University, Lucknow

title: BA fine Arts
description: Bachelor of Fine Arts (BFA) is an undergraduate degree programme that focuses on the visual and performing arts.
details:
BA FINE ARTS





What is this course?



Bachelor of Fine Arts (BFA) is an undergraduate degree programme that focuses on the visual and performing arts.

 The programme is also known as Bachelor of Visual Arts (BVA), and the study of Visual Arts encompasses areas including painting, sculpture, photography, literature, and animation. Performing arts, on the other hand, includes areas like dance, theatre, and music. A Bachelor of Fine Arts degree sometimes asks the individual to select a specialisation area based on his or her interests.



BFA is normally a three to four-year degree that can be completed full-time, part-time, online, or through distance learning, depending on the candidate's chosen specialisation.





Overview of this course



Course Level

Undergraduate

Full-Form

Bachelor of Arts in Fine Arts

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester-based

Eligibility

A minimum of 50% in Class 10+2 in any stream

Admission process

Merit-based/ Entrance Examination

Course Fee

INR 1-3 lakh

Average Salary

INR 3-6 LPA

Job Positions

Visual designer, Fine Artist, Art Teacher, Graphic Designer, Painter, Editor, Art Critic, Illustrator, etc.

Top Recruiting Companies

Design, Film, Theatre, Teaching, Animation, Educational Institutions, etc.





Why should you opt for this course?



A BFA programme not only educates but also modifies students' talents, preparing them to become artists in their chosen area. 

Fine arts graduates have several career options, including art teacher, writer, fine artist, actor, art director, and many others. Many students nowadays choose a profession in Fine Arts not only to earn a good salary but also to obtain fame and reputation.



Aspirants can specialise in courses that not only boost their talents and fine-tune their creativity, but also allow them to explore a variety of choices.



Fields under this course



Painting

Applied Arts

Plastic Arts

Pottery & Ceramics

Textile Design

Music

Drama & Theatre

Digital Arts

Calligraphy

Cartooning

Dance

Graphic Designing

Illustration

Photography

Printmaking

Sculpture



Career opportunities after completing this course









Fine arts is an area in today's modern and diversified world where the alternatives are numerous and receiving attention that was before unknown. 

Many students have gone on to achieve well in their jobs after graduating with a BFA, and most importantly, individuals enjoy doing what they love.

 If you enjoy photography, you should pursue it. Pursue your aspirations and work with some of the best companies in the country and beyond. Choreographers, sculptors, fashion designers, theatrical artists, performers, and the list goes on and on. You may either go out and start freelancing or obtain a job right immediately through college placements.

Whatever your passion is, you must work hard and devote a significant amount of time and effort to reach your objectives. 

After graduating with a degree in Fine Arts, students can choose from a range of professional prospects. Students can pursue jobs in cinema, television, design, animation, teaching, and the music business, among other fields. Candidates might have a look at the following job profiles:

title: B.Com LLB (integrated)
eligibility: Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?
details:
INTEGRATED LEGAL COURSE- B.COM LLB



What is B.com LLB?



The BCom LLB is a professional legal curriculum that lasts five years. The Bachelor of Commerce and Bachelor of Legislative Law (BCom LLB) is a combined programme that teaches both commerce and law to students. Business Statistics, Business Communication, Auditing, Economics, and other Commerce disciplines are studied as part of the BCom LLB programme. Candidates are also taught law subjects such as contract law, consumer protection law, constitutional law, criminal law, legal language, statute interpretation, and constitutional law, among others.



The BCom LLB degree has ten semesters, and applicants must finish all of them in eight years, which is the maximum time limit for completing this legal study.











 

 

Streams/Fields of the B.com LLB-

Following are the streams related to B.com LLB 

Business Law

Corporate Law

Taxation





 

Why should you study this course?

Dual Degree in Business and Law: This integrated programme is ideal for students who are interested in both business and law.



You can save a year by completing both courses in less than five years. Furthermore, you will be spared the trouble of taking a second entrance exam for admission to an LLB degree.



Better Career Chances: You are qualified for better job opportunities because you have a dual degree in commerce and law.



 A five-year legal school will provide you plenty of opportunity to engage in MUNs, Moot Courts, and other internships, all of which will broaden your intellectual horizons and prepare you for a career.



        Career opportunities after graduating B.COM LLB:-

Lawyer: After passing the legal school, lawyers can find work almost anywhere. You name it: legal companies, banks, multinational corporations, government agencies, and so on. Lawyers in India are well compensated for their professional experience, reputation, and talents. As a lawyer, you advise and represent your clients in all legal actions and hearings.

Legal Advisor: Businesses seek the advice of legal experts to avoid legal ramifications and consequences. These experts ensure that businesses make successful decisions. Legal advisers provide legal counsel to individuals and businesses in a variety of areas, including workplace disputes, labour regulations, pay concerns, and more. This is why multinational corporations and corporations are always on the hunt for new B.Com LLB grads.



Corporate Lawyer: A corporate lawyer is a lawyer who specialises in corporate and business law. These experts are in charge of the company's transactions, employee contracts, labour regulations, and taxation. Furthermore, as the business sector expands, corporate attorneys will have plenty of career prospects. Candidates with several years of experience might easily earn Rs. 40,000 to Rs. 60,000 per month.

Solicitor-To become a solicitor, individuals must first complete a vocational course known as the Legal Practice Course (LPC). Solicitors often specialise in one field of law after graduating, such as tax, real estate, family law, or litigation. As solicitors, applicants can work in private law firms or in the legal divisions of private and public organisations, providing legal advice and completing tasks such as contract preparation and court representation.



Start a Legal Practice: B.Com LLB degree graduates with subsequent professional experience can even start their own law firm because they already have the necessary business abilities. Unlike bigger law firms, independent law companies will give professionals greater independence and flexibility. Aside from that, the need for attorneys is continually increasing. With good marketing, professionals may discover new clients. Starting a legal practice is a highly profitable choice for professionals who have the financial resources.

 

What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course:-



Certificate Course in Infotech Law

Diploma in Administrative Laws

Diploma in Alternative Dispute Resolution System

Diploma in Co-operative Law

Diploma in Corporate Laws and Management

Diploma in Environmental Laws

Diploma in Human Rights

Diploma in Intellectual Property Rights

Diploma in International Laws

Diploma in Labour Law and Personnel Management

Diploma in Labour Laws (D.L.L)

Diploma in Labour Laws and Industrial Relations

Diploma in Labour Laws and Labour Welfare

Diploma in Taxation Laws

Doctor of Laws (LL.D)

Integrated BA LLB

M.A. Human Rights

M.A. Human Rights and Duties Education

M.Phil. Law

Master of Comparative Laws (MCL)

Master of Law (LLM)

P.G Diploma in Women’s Rights and Human Rights







 

 

What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for B.com LLb graduates?

 



Job Profile



Entry Level Annual Salary (Approx.)



Corporate Lawyer



Rs. 7 lakhs



Law Reporter



Rs. 5 lakhs



Legal Manager



Rs. 11 lakhs



Legal Advisor



Rs. 6 lakhs



Attorney



Rs. 4 lak

Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?

Following are some top entrance exams related to this course

                                                         Top entrance exams 

CLAT-

The Common Law Admission Test is a centralised nationwide entrance exam for admission to India's twenty-two National Law Universities. In India, most private and self-financed law schools utilise these scores for admittance.

LSAT India- 

The LSAT India scorecard includes a percentile rank and scaled scores on a scoring band of 420-480. More than 50 law institutions in India take the test for admission to the five-year Integrated LLB programme, three-year LLB programme, and LLM programme.

AP LAWCET- 

The Andhra Pradesh Common Law Entrance Test (APLAWCET) is a state-level entrance exam administered by Sri Padmavati Mahila Visvavidyalayam, Tirupati on behalf of the Andhra Pradesh State Council of Higher Education (APSCHE) for admission to law programmes at Andhra Pradesh universities and affiliated colleges.

TSLAWCET- 

TS LAWCET is a state-level law admission test that is administered by Osmania University in Hyderabad on behalf of the Telangana State Council of Higher Education (TSCHE). The legal entrance test is held for admission to Telangana state universities and institutions' five-year integrated LLB and three-year LLB programmes. A total of 30,150 people applied for the TSLAWCET test in 2020.

ULSAT- 

ULSAT is the Law School Admission Test for admission to the University Of Petroleum & Energy Studies' 3 year LLB, 5 year LLB (BA LLB, BBA LLB, BCom LLB, BTech LLB), and LLM programmes. The admission exam is a computer-based test (CBT). The test consists of multiple-choice questions.

MHCET Law - 

The MHT-CET, or Maharashtra Common Admission Test, is an annual entrance test held by the Maharashtra government. The Directorate of Technical Education is in charge of it.

title: BA LLB (Integrated)
description: BA LLB (Bachelor of Arts – Bachelor of Legislative Law)
details:
BA LLB (Bachelor of Arts – Bachelor of Legislative Law)







What is BA LLB?



BA LLB (Bachelor of Arts - Bachelor of Legislative Law) is an integrated 5-years programme that applicants are eligible to pursue after completing Class 12, wherein they have to study LLB along with different Arts subjects such as Sociology, History, etc. 



Overview/Highlights of this Course-



Level

UG

Duration of the course

5 years

Course fee

1.5 lakhs – 7 lakhs

Minimum qualification

10+2 (Intermediate) with at least 55% aggregate marks or equivalent CGPA

Selection process

Entrance Exam based





Difference between BA LLB & BBA LLB –



BA LLB (Bachelor of Arts - Bachelor of Legislative Law) is an integration of Arts and Law topics and is of five-year duration whereas BBA LLB (Bachelor of Business Administration & Bachelor of Legislative Law) is an incorporated course in which students will gain valuable insights in both the business functions and corporate laws.



Anyone who is keen to work as a general lawyer can pursue BA LLB but if anyone has interest in becoming a corporate lawyer, he/she can go with BBA LLB programme.



In short, BA LLB is a complete package of law and arts(humanities) whereas BBA LLB completely deals with the corporate laws, acquisition and mergers.



Streams/Fields of BA LLB -



BA LLB includes interdisciplinary studies of the humanities such as sociology and history, as well as studies of law. BA LLB subjects include –



Environmental Law

Corporate Law

Intellectual Property Law [IPR]

Human Rights

Psychology and Law or Legal Psychology 

Constitutional Law 

Contracts 

Criminal Law 





Why should you study BA LLB??  

                   OR

Why Should you opt for BA LLB??







As it is an Integrated course, you will get two degrees, the first one for B.A. and another for LLB.



After completing BA LLB, you’ll be eligible to work as a practitioner.



After BA LLB, you can also proceed for higher studies like LLM, master’s in law, etc.



BA LLB gives you the opportunity to develop yourself both financially and Intellectually.





Career opportunities after graduating BA LLB: -



After the successful completion of the course, you’ll have a wide range of career opportunities. Some of the major job opportunities are given below:



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course: -



As a BA LLB graduate, you’ll have a variety of courses to pursue. Some of the most popular courses are listed below:



LLM 

MA in Public Administration

MA Political Science/Sociology/History

MA Psychology

MA Criminology

MA Social Work

MBA

Masters of Business Law

Bachelor of Education (B.Ed.)

MA in Human Resources Management

Masters in Management Sciences 



What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for BA LLB graduates?



BA LLB graduates are given attractive job offers by various recruiters. However, the salary increment completely depends upon their work and field chosen. For your reference, we have listed some of the most popular jobs with their starting salary packages and average salary packages below:





Starting salary

Average salary

Lawyer

2 – 3 LPA

9 – 10 LPA

Corporate Lawyer

5 LPA

10 LPA

Legal Analyst

2 – 3 LPA

7 LPA

Civil Lawyer

2 – 3 LPA

20+ LPA

Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how??

                                            Or

Popular entrance exams to get admission in BA LLB Course-



CLAT (Common Law Admission Test)

AILET (All India law Entrance test)

LSAT (Law School Admission Test)

MH CET LAW (Maharashtra Law Common Entrance test)

DU LLB (Delhi University LLB Entrance Test)

JMI BA LLB (Jamia Millia Islamia BA LLB Entrance Exam)

PU LLB (Panjab University LLB Entrance Test)

ULSAT (University of Petroleum and Energy Studies Law Studies Aptitude Test)

KIITEE (KIIT Entrance Examination)

title: B.Com General
details:
BACHELOR OF COMMERCE (B.Com)



What is B.Com Course About?



B.Com stands for Bachelor of Commerce. 

It is a three-year undergraduate program that offers a conceptual understanding of Accounting, Economics, Business Law, Taxation, Insurance and Management.

It is one of the most popular courses among class 12 graduates with a commerce stream.

According to AISHE report, After Class 12 B.Com has been the third most desired course in India.



  



Overview of B.Com (Bachelor of Commerce)-



Some fundamental details of B.Com are given  below in the table:



Level

Undergraduate

Duration

3 years

Course fee

INR 10,000 - 100,000

Minimum qualification

10 + 2 with commerce stream

Selection process

Merit-based or entrance exam



Difference between B.Com (Hons) and B.Com-



Apart from B.Com, candidates can also do B.Com (Hons) if they want to gain in-depth knowledge in the commerce stream.



Both B.Com (Hons) and B.Com are the same in most things even though both of them are different courses.



Features

B.Com

B.Com (Hons.)

Duration-

3 years

3 years

Curriculum-

B.Com (general) programmes  is an overview of all the subjects in the field of commerce.





The subjects are the same as in a B.Com (Hons.).

Students obtaining a B.Com degree will not be able to specialize in a particular field.



Although most of the subjects are the same, the major difference is that this programme allows you to specialize in a specific subject or field.

Students can choose a subject in final year for specialization studies.



The objective of the Course-



This programme is designed to familiarize students with many facets of the business world. Students who take the course will learn how to conduct business and trade.



B.Com (Hons.) is a business-oriented course that helps students enhance their business abilities and gain an in-depth understanding of the field of commerce.

If students choose to study Chartered Accountancy and this course at the same time, they will have an advantage.





Fields of B.Com-



B.Com Course is divided into 38 types of programs.

The fields of.BCom are listed below-



BCom Applied Economics

BCom Corporate Secretaryship

BCom Economics

BCom E-Commerce

BCom Marketing

BCom Financial Markets

BCom Accountancy

BCom Financial Accounting

BCom Finance

BCom Foreign Trade Management

BCom Accounting and Finance

BCom Professional

BCom Banking and Finance

BCom Management Studies

BCom Banking Management

BCom Statistics

BCom Banking and Insurance

BCom Supply Chain Management

BCom Tourism and Travel Management

BCom Tax Procedure and Practice

BCom Business Economics

BCom Capital Market

BCom Business Administration

BCom Taxation

BCom Computer Applications

BCom Computer Science

BCom Accounting



Only a few BCom specialties, such as BCom General, Banking, Finance, Accountancy, Honors, Economics, and others, are popular among the above-mentioned categories.



Popular B.Com (Hons.) Specialization-



Here in the image are some of the popular & preferred B.Com (Hons.) specializations are presented-

      



Why Should You Study B.Com ??



Prepares students for employment and entrepreneurship-



B.Com degree prepares graduates for the Corporate world as well as Entrepreneurship, unlike any other Bachelor's course. 

The course provides business and financial knowledge to adapt to the professional world.



Huge Job domains- 



Bachelor of Commerce graduates can work in a range of industries, including banks and capital management, accounting firms, investment banking, and budget planning, among others.



Basic knowledge for further studies-



The foundational understanding of the M.Com course curriculum will be imparted in B.Com, making it an excellent choice for M.Com aspirants.

Following a B.Com degree, B.Com students have several possibilities, including M.Com, MBA, CA (Chartered Accountant), CS (Company Secretary), MCA, and a variety of diploma programmes.

       



Growing demand for candidates in this sector-



The demand for Bachelor of Commerce candidates has increased in both the public and private sectors over time.



Career opportunities after B.Com-



The demand for commerce degrees is very high across the board.

The Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com) programme prepares students for a variety of job roles.

With a B.Com degree, a candidate is qualified to work in a variety of sectors, including marketing, banking, public accounting, policy planning, consulting, and investment.

The following are some of the most common job roles available to students with a Bachelor of Commerce degree. 



Options

B.Com



Employment-

areas



Finance



BPO



Human Resources



Business Consultancy



Investment Banking



Human Resources



Auditing



Financial Analysis



Job roles/ profile

Accountant



Auditor Business Consultant



Consultant Certified Public Accountant



Company Secretary



Chartered Accountant



Business Analyst Chief Financial Officer

title: B Com in Applied Economics
duration: This degree, which can take up to two years to complete, is for students who wish to start a career in management.
details:
B. COM - APPLIED ECONOMICS



What is B.Com in Applied Economics-

B.Com, or Bachelor of Commerce in Applied Economics, is a full-time undergraduate programme that teaches students how to apply economic analysis to specific situations in both the public and private sectors.

This is a well-known undergraduate curriculum that gives an overview of various trading or economics methods and is expected to become increasingly valuable in the future. PDM University, Poornima University, Sanskriti University, Garden city university, and Chandigarh group of institutions are some of the colleges that provide B.com in Applied Economics in India.



In India, the B.Com Applied Economics programme is the most popular economy-related bachelor's degree. It permits students to work in practically every area of the economy while still allowing them to stay in school.

BCom in Applied Economics Quick Facts Highlights-

Full-Form

Bachelor of Commerce in Applied Economics



Average Fees

INR 30,000 to INR 400,000 per annum



Admissions

Merit-based or Entrance based



Colleges

Loyola College, School of management studies,St. Xaviers College, Mumbai, Institute of Management-Christ 

University, Bengaluru, and many more



Entrance test

DU JAT, CUCET, AU CET, etc.



Types of Course

regular, online, distance education mode.



Subjects

Accounting, Applied Economics, Macro Economics, Business Regulatory Framework, etc.



Job Profile

Statistical Assistant, Financial Analyst, Financial Director, Business Analyst, Investment Banker, and many more.

Top Recruiters

State Bank of India, KPMG, PWC, HDFC Bank, ICRA, etc.



Average Starting Salary

INR 3-5 LPA

Job areas

private and government sectors of banking, accounting, finance and insurance.

Future Scope

M.Com, MBA, PGDM, CFA, CA, PhD, etc.





Why should you pursue a BCom in Applied Economics?



Because of the growing need in economics, investment banking, and the benefits that the course provides, BCom Applied Economics is in great demand.

Candidates can work in a variety of commercial centres, educational institutes, banks, marketing businesses, financial departments, and other places after completing the course.

The following are some of the benefits of a BCom in Applied Economics:

A BCom Applied Economics degree may lead to work prospects in a variety of fields, including Assistant Manager/Officer, Statistical Assistant, Service Quality Leader, Market Research Analyst, Lead Modeller, Economics Teacher, Curriculum Developer, and many others. 

Students can pursue higher education after completing the course by enrolling in one of numerous M.Com, MBA, or PGDM programmes.

Research opportunities: BCom Applied Economics provides good opportunities for the student to devote themselves to various research institutes and companies & organizations.

Competitive Salary:A BCom Applied Economics graduate in India can expect to earn between INR 4-6 lakh per year. Students with over 10+ years of experience can earn up to INR 8,00,000 per annum.

Networking Opportunities: It allows students to collaborate with their classmates, as well as seniors, instructors, and alumni, to grow their businesses. The fundamental goal of networking is to establish a network of contacts for future career prospects.

Government Jobs: Candidates with a BCom degree are eligible to sit for a variety of government tests, including civil service exams and exams connected to finance and accounting, such as import-export, income tax, and excise departments.
