Further studies after BCom Applied Economics -

As the globe becomes more digitized, the economic sector continues to grow. Students with a BCom degree are most likely to work in banking and finance. The majority of the candidates are studying for MCom, MBA, and other highly regarded national and international management and accounting certification courses.

Because of the growing need in numerous trade businesses, the Import-Export or Financing sector is always changing. After earning a BCom degree, students can pursue careers in banking, finance, and import/export. After graduation, the majority of candidates begin studying for MCom, MBA, and other highly regarded national and international certification courses in management and accounting.



M Com-

The most popular course following graduation is M.Com, which is a postgraduate master's degree. Students who have completed their BCom can enrol in this two-year master's degree programme. Several entrance tests, such as the BHU PET, CUCET, PU CET, and others, are used to determine admission to this programme. For students interested in economics, banking, investing, financial services, investment, accounting, and marketing, this is the ideal option.

MBA-

After finishing a bachelor's degree programme, an MBA is one of the most sought-after management level degrees.

This degree, which can take up to two years to complete, is for students who wish to start a career in management.

Entrance exams such as the CAT, XAT, MAT, and other analogous national or university level examinations are used to determine admission.

For those with a passion for marketing and management, this is the perfect option.

Following the completion of an MBA, you will have a wealth of work choices and greater career prospects in a variety of high-paying industries such as management, finance, international business, business analytics, and supply chain management, among others.

Doctor of Philosophy (PhD)-

After completing a master's degree programme, one can pursue a PhD programme that lasts 3-5 years. Entrance tests such as the NET, JRF, and other analogous national or university level examinations are used to determine admission. This is the greatest option for those who want to pursue a career as a lecturer or professor.

Entrance Based Admission

Some the colleges and universities conduct an entrance test for offering admissions to this program

DU JAT, CUCET, AU CET etc are some entrance tests for admission 

For this method of admission, you need to apply on their authority website

Submit the required paperwork and keep the receipt for future reference.

Then give the entrance exam and do your best in it.

Students who pass the entrance exam must go through the counselling process and accept the admission offer by paying the required costs.

title: B.Com Travel and Tourism Management
description: Bachelor of Commerce in Tourist and Travel Management
details:
Bachelor of Commerce in Tourist and Travel Management

About-

The Bachelor of Commerce in Tourist and Travel Management is a three-year full-time undergraduate degree programme focusing on the financial and economic management of the travel and tourism business.

Business Communication, Corporate Accounting, Principles of Insurance, Holistic Development, Tourism Products, Tourism Marketing, Fundamentals of Hotel Management, Business Economics, and other topics are taught in the BCom Tourism and Travel Management Course Curriculum.



BCom Tourism and Travel Management Course Highlights

Course Level

Under-Graduate

Full-Form

Bachelor of Commerce in Travel and Tourism Management

Duration

3 years

Examination type

Merit-based/College-Entrances

Eligibility

10+2 Examination cleared with a minimum aggregate of 50%

Admission Process

Based on Performance in College Entrance/Interviews/Board Marks.

Average Annual Fees

INR 30,000 to 2,00,000



Streams or Subjects under Tourism and Travel Management Course

The main subject areas under the BCom Tourism and Travel Management Course are Business Communication, Corporate Accounting, Principles of Insurance, Holistic Development, Tourism Products, Tourism Marketing, Fundamentals of Hotel Management, Business Economics etc.















What are the Benefits of a B.Com? Tourism & Travel Management Course?

The course prepares students for careers in airlines, travel agencies, tour operators, resorts, shipping firms, hotels, and state tourism departments, among other places.

Graduates may expect a diverse range of job prospects, including opportunities for development to management positions.

The course will help students prepare for positions in the travel and tourism industry at all levels, including travel agencies, hotels, travel consultants, and companies.

The course provides students with professional and academic inputs to help them adapt to the changing needs of the travel, tourist, and hospitality industries.



What are Job Prospects and Career Options after BCom Tourism and Travel Management?

Job prospects for BCom Tourism and Travel Management graduates are excellent. Graduates of this degree are eligible for a variety of employment in the public sector, including Tourism Boards and State Commutation Services.

The tourist business is the country's third-largest source of revenue, thus there are several chances.

Assistant managers, hotel managers, and other officer-level positions in travel and tourism are among the job descriptions.

Most occupations are linked to the financial and management aspects of several businesses, particularly the travel and tourist industry. If you want to work in the tourist industry, you should consider continuing your education with a master's degree.

Some of the popular jobs of BCom Tourism and Travel Management doctorates are mentioned below with their average salary packages.

Job Profiles



Job Description



Average Annual Salary

Travel and Tourism Operator

These operators play a crucial role in interaction with their travellers/tourists as they need to take care of all the back-end work(travel arrangements/direct contact with their travellers). These included machines used for interaction with people and much more

INR 2,00,000 to 4,00,000

Travel Content Writer

Travel content writers often earn a living by producing articles, reports, and other material for a variety of travel-related magazines, agencies, and websites. These travel writers contribute to many articles and write as authors. Transportation, historical locations, cultural and natural places, and much more are among their main interests.

INR 2,00,000 to 4,50,000

Tourism Trainer

Tourism trainers serve as guides for tourism firms. They also direct travellers to information centres and motels. They work for a variety of clients, including contractors, cruise ships, and other workers. They make certain that these businesses are properly guided so that the relevant information and services are delivered.

INR 3,00,000 to 4,85,000

Airline Manager

By monitoring the basic airline operations, airline managers prefer to take care of customers (travellers). They essentially aim to ensure that the entire process operates well, including regulatory compliance and much more

INR 1,80,000 to 5,00,000

Hotel Manager

Hotel managers are in charge of supervising personnel who are in charge of planning, administering, and marketing hotel services. Catering, lodging, and other hotel amenities are among these services.

INR 4,00,000 to 6,00,000

Travel Sales Consultant

Travel Sales Consultants are travel agents that have been in the industry for a long time and have a lot of expertise. They must get certification from a trade association. They recommend fun vacation spots to their consumers. They also manage airlines, hotels, rental cars, and other travel-related services.

INR 2,00,000 to 3,25,000

What are the Future Scopes of BCom Tourism and Travel Management?

The BCom Tourism and Travel Management subject has grown in popularity among students in recent years. A rising number of students are pursuing this subject. The course prepares students for a variety of careers. This just adds to the course's enormous popularity among students. Following are the most common educational options after completing a BCom Tourism and Travel Management degree.

MTTM: 

An MTTM is the first programme to consider if one desires to continue in the same sector of education. It's a two-year programme that requires a bachelor's degree in travel and tourism as one of the prerequisites.



MBA: 

A big proportion of BCom graduates choose to pursue a PGDM or MBA programme to seek a career in management. Admissions are decided through a national entrance exam. A BCom degree combined with an MBA in a chosen field is very advantageous, and many companies actively seek such applicants.



Competitive examinations:

 Another way that graduates can go is to prepare for competitive examinations. Examinations that are meant for job opportunities in mostly the Government sector.Jobs in this industry are safe, with guaranteed high salary and regular raises.

Some Famous Entrance Exams for B.com in Travel and Management

BHU UET: 

BHU UET is a national level entrance exam held by Banaras Hindu University to select applicants for admission to degree programmes such as BSc, BA, BCom, BPEd, BEd, LLB, BPA, BVSc, BFA, and others.

LPUNEST: 

The Lovely Professional University administers the LPUNEST, a university-level entrance test. The exam is conducted for shortlisting students for providing admission into various courses like Engineering, Law and MBA. In addition to admission, the exam is used to award several scholarships.

details:
B.COM BUSINESS ECONOMICS 

      What is B.com Business Economics? 



B.Com in Business Economics is a programme that teaches students how to think like a businessperson. Accounting, management behaviour, and micro and macroeconomics are all covered in this course.

A few more important facts regarding B.Com in Business Economics courses are shown below.

Students can pursue advanced knowledge by enrolling in postgraduate courses such as MBA, M.Com, M.A. Economics, M.Phil. in Economics, and so on.

Students can use this degree to improve their chances of landing a job in any sector. Many students who get a B.com in Business Economics go on to work as economists, investment risk analysts, data analysts, and so on. a financial planner

This course allows you to focus on topics other than economics, such as Micro and Macro Economics, Labor Economics, Industrial Economics, and Interdisciplinary Economics.



    Highlights- B.com Business Economics: 

      Bachelor of Commerce in Business Economics is the title of the programme.

The course is of 3 years duration. 

The admission process for this course is twofold merit-based and through the entrance 

The common entrance test for admission in this course is CA CPT, CUCET, MDU CEE, DUET and DUET. 

Exams are scheduled according to semesters during these three years.

Every year, the course fee ranges between INR 20,000 and 1.5 lakhs.

The students eligible for this course should have completed 10+2 with the commerce stream with an average of 50% scored. 

This training can lead to a job in banking, research corporations, financial organisations, and other fields.

A person pursuing this course works in the post of Financial Analyst, Financial Director, Business Analyst, 	Investment Banker, Finance Business advancement. 

    B.COM BUSINESS ECONOMICS: Subjects of Study 

English Language 

Regional Languages: Kannada/ Sanskrit/ Urdu/ Tamil/ Telugu/ Marathi/ Hindi 

Financial Accounting 

Principles of Management 

Business Mathematics 

Business Economics 

Human resources management



Business Statistics 

English and Business Communication 

Corporate Accounting 

Marketing Management 

International Business Environment 

Indian Financial System 

Indian Constitution 

Financial Management 

Law and Practice of Banking 

Cost Accounting 

Computer Fundamentals 

Methods & Techniques of Cost Accounting 

Income Taxes 

Entrepreneurship Development Programme 

Computer Business Applications 

Business Laws 

Management Accounting 

Services Management



     IMPORTANCE OF B.COM IN BUSINESS ECONOMICS-

Higher degree programmes in corresponding disciplines might be pursued for additional study and then research.

The curriculum is designed to help students develop excellent critical thinking, analytical, and research abilities. The degree offers a wide education that can lead to a variety of professional choices.

Throughout the course, students will learn how to conduct business the way it is done in the real world. They will look at current market concerns and participate in hands-on activities that imitate real-world business settings.

title: B.Com Computer application

details:
B.COM COMPUTER APPLICATION

ABOUT THE COURSE 

The B.Com Computer Applications programme is a three-year full-time undergraduate programme that integrates computer science with commerce to study a variety of topics in both fields. 

It's a combination of commerce and computer applications for business disciplines that demand computer skills. The goal of this course is to provide students a thorough grasp of business and computer systems.

Furthermore, this study opens up a variety of job opportunities in the domains of information technology and commerce. After completing the course, students can work as a Business Analyst, Business Consultant, Auditor, Computer Programmer, App Developer, and other professions.



Key Highlights for BCom Computer Applications

Full-Form: Bachelor of Commerce in Computer Applications

Duration: 3 years

Eligibility: 10+2 with Commerce with at least 45% marks.

Admission Process: Merit-Based

Entrance Exam: JET, VEE, Loyola Entrance Exam, etc

Top Institutes: Loyola College, Women’s Christian College, Sacred Heart College, Mahatma Gandhi University, etc.

Average Course Fees: INR 15K to INR 1 lakh

Fields and stream of B.Com Computer Application-

There are, in total, six semesters in the program. B. Com Computer Applications subjects list is as follows:

Financial Accounting

Business Economics

Corporate Accounting

Programming with C++

Relational Database Management System

Computerized Accounting

Why should we opt for a B.Com computer application?



A candidate with a degree in this field is expected to be knowledgeable in both commerce and computers.

Accountancy, economics, and other useful abilities in the sphere of commerce that may be employed for one's personal advancement and future professional progression.

You'll also have valuable computer skills, which will enable you to understand and assist others by providing advice or services connected to computer repair, such as software issue fixes.

It provides you an advantage over computer grads since you have a background in commerce as well, making you a desired prospect for IT companies.

You can work as an auditor, budget analyst, CA, CS, financial analyst, business consultant, and so on, with a wide range of career prospects.



Career opportunities after graduating the scores with their Celerio packages-

Certain job options with their description and annual salary are mentioned in the table below:

Job Position

Job Description

Average Annual Pay Scale

Auditor

They check the correctness of the financial records and audit the business records. They will bring up any inconsistencies or deception.

INR 4,80,000

Computer Programmer

They'll have to work on existing computer languages and keep updating them, or create a new language if that's possible.

INR 8,50,000

They assist clients in improving their businesses by evaluating, strategizing, and planning, among other things.

INR 9,80,000

They oversee developing and testing programme applications for mobile devices.

INR 5,00,000

They help the company by guiding businesses in improving processes, products, services, and software through data analysis

INR 6,50,000



Higher studies after graduating this course-



They can pursue master’s degree courses like M.Com Computer Applications, Masters Computer Application in Software Systems (MCA), M.Com Finance, etc.

 

They can also prepare for the Chartered Accountant exams like the CA exams or the ACCA.

They can go for an MBA in Finance, etc. if they wish to create a career in the management field.



Popular entrance exam to get admission into best colleges of India



Very few colleges conduct entrance exams for BCom Computer Applications. Majorly, colleges admit students for this course, based on merit. Few of the exams are mentioned below:



Jain Entrance Test:

 This exam is conducted by Jain University for admission to its UG programs. The students are selected on the basis of their performance in the exam.



VEE VELS Exam: 

This exam is conducted by VELS University, Chennai for admission to its UG programs. The students are selected based on their performance in the exam. 



Loyola College Entrance Exam: 

The exam or interview is conducted for admission to UG programs in Loyola College, Chennai

B.COM E-COMMERCE

         What is B.com E-commerce?

The B Com e-commerce programme is a three-year undergraduate programme that focuses on e-commerce accounting principles.

E-commerce is a new type of trading that uses the internet and technology to its advantage.

The B com in e-commerce degree programme is designed to provide students with theoretical and practical knowledge of future commerce and trade practices.

Candidates having a 10+2 degree in Accounts, Business Studies, and Economics from a recognised board are eligible to enroll in this programme.





    Highlights Of B.com E-commerce:

Course Level - Undergraduate Course

Full Form - Bachelor of Commerce in e-commerce Duration - 3 years

Examination Type - Semester Examinations

Eligibility - Candidates should have passed 10+2 examinations with at least 50% marks. Admission Process - Merit Based/Entrance examinations

Course Fee - INR 5K-30K Average Salary - INR 3-6 lpa

Top Recruiting Area/Organization - 

Banks, Business Consultancies, Foreign Trade, Industrial Houses, Inventory Control, Educational Institutes, Investment Banking, Public Accounting Firms, Marketing, Merchant Banking, Policy Planning, Public Accounting Firms, Working Capital Management, Treasury and Forex Department, among others.

Job Positions - Accountant, Bookkeeper, Finance Manager, Marketing Manager, Assistant Manager, etc.

     Fields Of B.com E-commerce:

      There are 3 main areas in E-commerce-

Online Retailing

Electronic Markets

Online Actions

    Why should you study B.com E-commerce?

B.com e-commerce provides students a variety of benefits, including a solid package, an intriguing job profile, and a secure career. Here are a few reasons why it's a good idea to go with it: -



B com e commerce opens up a variety of job opportunities in a variety of sectors and companies. Students who complete this programme can work in the private sector, such as accountancy firms or private banks.

B.com e commerce students can work in government occupations such as policy planning, public accounting, treasury, and the FX department.



Deals with the accounting and technology aspects of the business:- The course is unique in that it focuses on technology advancements in the accounting field.





      What can we do after B.com E-commerce?

After completing B.com in E-commerce anyone can pursue MBA with a specialization in e-commerce management. E-Commerce Master's Degree A master's degree in information technology and e- commerce is available. E-Commerce and Web Design Master's Degree.

    Career opportunities after B.com E-commerce:

B com e commerce is a course that allows students to work in a variety of fields. After completing this course, a student will have numerous job prospects in a variety of sectors. This training is in high demand everywhere, from the school industry to the banking sector. After earning a B.com. in e- commerce, you can work in finance or on e-commerce platforms.

Organizations that deal with e-commerce transactions

Banks and other financial institutions

Firms that specialize in networking and marketing

State, local, and federal government accounting departments.

6-10 lacs

title: B. Com in Financial Markets
description: THE BACHELOR OF COMMERCE IN FINANCIAL MARKETS (BFM)
details:
THE BACHELOR OF COMMERCE IN FINANCIAL MARKETS (BFM)

WHAT IS BFM?

The Bachelor of Financial Markets (BFM) is a three-year undergraduate degree that gives students a grasp of finance and money, investment economics, communication skills, legal constraints, and other facets of the financial market. 



It covers topics like organising, planning, and managing financial resources, among others.







The course provides aspirants with knowledge in the areas of Equity Market, Debt Market, Capital Market, Skill Enhancement Course, Ability Enhancement Course, Elective Course, Discipline Elective Specific Course, Technical Analysis, Financial Derivatives, Mutual Funds, Taxation (Direct and Indirect), Macro Economics, Corporate and Security Law, Professional Communication Skill, and Information Technology. It changes you from a layperson to someone who understands the fundamental ideas and mechanisms with ease.



OVERVIEW OF THE COURSE



Students can pursue a master's degree or a job in a different subject, or they can take extra professional courses, after completing the BCom Financial Markets undergraduate programme.

Any student who works hard to finish the course will get great critical thinking abilities.

This course is extremely beneficial in understanding the financial market since it covers how an investor trades stocks, bonds, commodities, and other financial instruments on stock exchanges or stock markets.



The training will transform you from a novice to someone who can rapidly grasp the basic principles and processes that are often ignored while trading financial products.



The course will concentrate on the country's primary financial markets and how they influence the economy.

WHY SHOULD YOU OPT FOR B.COM (FINANCIAL MARKETS) COURSE?



The course will benefit students by providing an additional source of self-employment and suitable and trained personnel for the financial services sector, as well as preparing graduates to compete with degree holders from private and foreign universities and establishing strong coordination between the services sector and higher education institutions.

Students can use the course to advance their careers in sectors such as MBA, M.Com., CA, CS, ICWA, Finance, Banking, Insurance, Consultancy, and Computer Accounting, among others.

full_content:
THE BACHELOR OF COMMERCE IN FINANCIAL MARKETS (BFM)

WHAT IS BFM?

The Bachelor of Financial Markets (BFM) is a three-year undergraduate degree that gives students a grasp of finance and money, investment economics, communication skills, legal constraints, and other facets of the financial market. 



It covers topics like organising, planning, and managing financial resources, among others.







The course provides aspirants with knowledge in the areas of Equity Market, Debt Market, Capital Market, Skill Enhancement Course, Ability Enhancement Course, Elective Course, Discipline Elective Specific Course, Technical Analysis, Financial Derivatives, Mutual Funds, Taxation (Direct and Indirect), Macro Economics, Corporate and Security Law, Professional Communication Skill, and Information Technology. It changes you from a layperson to someone who understands the fundamental ideas and mechanisms with ease.



OVERVIEW OF THE COURSE



Students can pursue a master's degree or a job in a different subject, or they can take extra professional courses, after completing the BCom Financial Markets undergraduate programme.

Any student who works hard to finish the course will get great critical thinking abilities.

This course is extremely beneficial in understanding the financial market since it covers how an investor trades stocks, bonds, commodities, and other financial instruments on stock exchanges or stock markets.



The training will transform you from a novice to someone who can rapidly grasp the basic principles and processes that are often ignored while trading financial products.



The course will concentrate on the country's primary financial markets and how they influence the economy.

WHY SHOULD YOU OPT FOR B.COM (FINANCIAL MARKETS) COURSE?



The course will benefit students by providing an additional source of self-employment and suitable and trained personnel for the financial services sector, as well as preparing graduates to compete with degree holders from private and foreign universities and establishing strong coordination between the services sector and higher education institutions.

Students can use the course to advance their careers in sectors such as MBA, M.Com., CA, CS, ICWA, Finance, Banking, Insurance, Consultancy, and Computer Accounting, among others.

They may also work in businesses that deal with financial planning, liquidity management, or even the preparation of a merger or acquisition.

         Jobs

Job Description

Finance Analyst

Financial analysts are in charge of analysing a company's financial performance against a plan, generating projections based on business performance and market circumstances, and giving monthly reports to senior management to help them make tactical and strategic choices.

Consultancy Firms

Management consultants help customers identify solutions to their changing business needs. They must be informed about industry changes and how they can affect existing and potential clients.

Stock Market

On the stock market, buyers and sellers may negotiate pricing and make transactions. The exchange then keeps track of the supply and demand for each listed stock, allowing investors to buy and sell amongst themselves.

Tourism Sector

Assist customers in choosing the finest holiday package for their requirements and interests. Provide guides, itineraries, brochures, and maps to travellers with all vital and necessary information about their journey. Sell the right travel package to the right customer.

Investment Banking

Many financial models for valuing debt and equity in mergers and acquisitions, as well as capital raising operations, have been developed. Develop product proposals, private equity transactions, mergers and acquisitions, and valuation recommendations.

Share and Capital Markets

In charge of the company's initial public offering (IPO) on stock exchanges to obtain funds. Engage closely with capital markets and regulators to complete the listing process for a firm via IPO or Private Placement.

Banking Sector

Account management for clients, including account creation and closure, as well as transaction control. Payments, deposits, and withdrawals are all handled. Loans and overdrafts are approved and examined.

Educational Institution

Educational institutions, as well as a range of other educational institutions, provide instructional services or education-related services to students.

Commercial Banking

Commercial banks provide its clients with a wide range of services and products, allowing commercial bankers to deal with a diverse set of clients while specializing in their field.

Share and capital Markets

Any trading platform where financial securities and assets are traded for actual currency is referred to as a capital market. Bonds, derivatives, and commodities may be traded alongside stocks in capital markets. Stock markets are a type of capital market where only corporate shares are traded.

Stock Broker

A stockbroker is a trade expert who buys and sells stocks on behalf of his clients. Other terms for a stockbroker include registered representative and investment advisor.

Auditor

An auditor is a person with the power to check and confirm the accuracy of financial papers as well as to ensure that firms adhere to tax regulations. They protect firms from fraud, point out accounting inconsistencies, and, on occasion, operate as consultants, assisting businesses in identifying methods to improve operational efficiency.







What can we do further after completing this course?



After completing the BCom Financial Markets programme, students can pursue a master's degree, which is a popular choice since BFM graduates can enrol in postgraduate courses such as MBA in Finance, Financial Risk Management (FRM), CFA, CS, and Chartered Accountant.

Other Higher study options after Bachelor of Commerce in Financial Market are

M.B.A. (Financial Service)

title: B. Com Financial Accounting docx
details:
B. COM FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING

ABOUT

The Bachelor of Commerce in Financial Accounting is an undergraduate curriculum. This curriculum will take three years to complete. Students who took commerce as an option in 12th year might choose to pursue B.Com. It helps students comprehend the essential principles of finance, commerce, and accounting. Students from the sciences and the arts can also pursue a B.com financial accounting degree as an undergraduate degree.



B.com graduates can work in industries such as commerce, accounting, finance, banking, and insurance. Students who want to pursue Chartered Accounting following their undergraduate studies should do the B.com course.



Students with a B.Com can pursue careers in education, advertising, law, journalism, mass communication, law, and design. B.com graduates get a starting salary of Rs. 3.5 lakh.







BCOM FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING: OVERVIEW

Course Level

Undergraduate

Duration of the Course

3 Years

Examination Type

Semester

Eligibility

50% in 10th and 12th board exam

Admission Process

Entrance exam and Merit-Based

Course Fee

INR 10,000 to 1,00,000



B.COM FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING FIELDS 

Finance

Marketing

Accounts

Human resource management

Banking & Insurance

Taxation law

Financial market

Investment management

Auditing





WHY SHOULD YOU OPT FOR B.COM FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING?

B.Com Financial Accounting graduates have a wide range of work options and job possibilities.

Graduates of the Bachelor of Commerce in Financial Accounting have a very high employment rate when they have the suitable wage package.

The need for an Account Executive or a Commerce Manager to handle financial matters makes a B.com graduation essential.





B.COM FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING JOB SCOPES

You can explore several useful opportunities as a B Com Accounting and Finance graduate because it offers up a plethora of options for pupils. A graduate in accounting and finance has several choices and can pursue other educational certifications.





Job Profile

Description

Business Analyst

A business analyst's responsibilities are many, and they include monitoring corporate performance and developing ways to enhance it.

Finance Manager

Plan how the company's financial resources will be distributed to ensure a higher turnover and budget planning.

Accountant

Maintains an accurate and efficient record of the organization's transactions and analyses it to create reports.



Risk Analyst

Makes conclusions from the firm's investment and abroad links and utilises them to create a report outlining the possible dangers the company may face, as well as suggestions for reducing the risk of loss.

Auditor

They examine a corporation's finances to keep track of the different transactions that occur inside the organisation and to guarantee that these transactions are valid.

Marketing Manager

They are in charge of assessing market conditions and designing strategies to increase sales of a company's products.







HIGHER STUDIES AFTER COMPLETING B.COM FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING



M.Com: The second programme of option for those who desire to continue in the same field of education is M.Com. It is a two-year programme with qualifying requirements including a BCom degree in a relevant field.



PhD: If a student wants to enhance their study or enter the teaching profession. A PhD is the primary programme of choice. A Ph.D. takes about 3-5 years to complete.

Job Designations

Average Annual Salary

INR 3 to 4 Lacs

Tax Accountant

INR 1 to 3 Lacs

Financial Officers

INR 4 to 6 Lacs

Stockbroker

INR 4 to 9 Lacs

NPAT - The NMIMS university entrance exam for admission to BBA 2022 and other Under Graduate programmes at NMIMS University Mumbai and its other campuses is the NPAT 2022 exam.

BHU UET- NTA administers the Banaras Hindu University Undergraduate Entrance Test (BHU UET) on behalf of the university (BHU). It is a national entrance exam for admission to BHU's undergraduate programmes.

JET- The JET Exam Board, an autonomous body within the Ministry of Corporate Affairs of the Government of India, conducts a competitive examination in India.

Dayananda Sagar Admission Test (DSAT)- The Dayanand Sagar Admission Test (DSAT) is held by Dayananda Sagar University (DSU) for undergraduate and graduate programmes. DSAT is a national admission test that is offered in both offline and online formats.

title: Bcom in Foreign trade management 1
details:
B.COM FOREIGN TRADE MANAGEMENT 



What is B.com Foreign Trade Management? 

Foreign Trade Management is a three-year bachelor's degree programme in commerce. Top commerce colleges offer the degree with the goal of instilling skills and information in applicants in the sphere of international business and market place. 

 

Candidates for BCom Foreign Trade Management must have finished their 10+2 from a recognised board with a 50 percent grade. Despite the fact that the candidate may have finished their 10+2 in any subject, the course is primarily attended by commerce students who want to pursue a career in foreign trade management. 

 

The performance of the candidate on their qualifying exam is used to determine admission to most universities. Certain schools and universities, on the other hand, require candidates to take an entrance exam. 

 

Highlights Of B.com in Foreign Trade Management: 



Full-Form 

Bachelor of Commerce in Foreign Management 

Course Level 

Undergraduate 

Duration 

3 years 

Examination 

Type 

Semester wise 

Eligibility 

10+2 with a minimum score of 50% in any stream ( Preferably commerce) from a recognised school board. 

Admission 

Process 

Merit-based/ Entrance Exam 

Course Fees 

INR 25,000 to INR 5,00,000 for the entire course 

Average 

starting salaries 

INR 3,00,000 to INR 8,00,000 

Top Recruiting areas 

Trade Management, International business management, Accounting, Financial 

Advisory, Government and Private sector banks, Aviation and Transport agencies, Export and Import Industry, Custom Houses, Universities and colleges and more areas which deal in Foreign trade management 

Job Positions 

Business Analyst, Market Analyst, Auditor, Foreign Trade operations manager, Professor/ Lecturer, Marketing Manager. 



Field of B.com Foreign Trade Management: 

The major fields under this course are as follows



Trade Management

International business management

Accounting

Financial Advisory

Government and Private sector banks

Aviation and Transport agencies

Export and Import Industry

Custom Houses



Why should you study B.com Foreign Trade Management? 

 

For those interested in pursuing a master's or doctoral degree, a B.com in foreign trade management serves as a foundation. Candidates who complete the course successfully can apply for masters programmes in foreign trade management such as MBA or PGDM. 

Working in the field of international trade management is a useful and secure option as the global market grows in size. 

Candidates with a BCom in Foreign Trade Management can work in export businesses in a variety of capacities, including sales, finance, and marketing. For the candidates, the training does really offer up new horizons and chances. 

Candidates can apply for executive programmes after having expertise in Foreign Trade Management following their BCom. 

 

What can we do further after B.com Foreign Trade Management? 



➢ Graduates can continue their education in similar fields such as MCom in foreign trade management or MBA. 

 

Career Opportunities after B.com Foreign Trade Management: 



Since globalisation, market size has grown and global reach has been more accessible. As a result, a career in foreign trade management can be quite rewarding. Candidates with a BCom in Foreign Trade Management have an entry-level potential for a more profitable career. After gaining expertise, individuals can go up the corporate ladder and simply create and change international trade policy. 

 

After completing their BCom Overseas Commerce Management, applicants can work for large multinational corporations that have a global presence and deal in foreign trade for their goods and services. The course offers opportunities for the following companies, to mention a few.

On the official website of Banaras Hindu University, interested candidates can fill out online applications. 

 

 

The eligibility criteria for DU B.Com are determined by the Delhi University Exam, which is conducted by the University of Delhi. The entrance exam is a 10+2 exam with a minimum score determined by the college's requirements. 

 

Punjab University UG Entrance Exam (PU CET): This exam is administered by Panjab University and is abbreviated as PU CET.

title: B.Com Professional docx
description: A bachelor's degree in commerce is known as a B.Com. Professional. The course's major goal is to develop professionals who can deal effectively with economic and financial issues. The course aids the applicant in the development of managerial and accounting abilities.
details:
B.COM. PROFESSIONAL 

ABOUT

A bachelor's degree in commerce is known as a B.Com. Professional. The course's major goal is to develop professionals who can deal effectively with economic and financial issues. The course aids the applicant in the development of managerial and accounting abilities.



Students who have completed their Higher Secondary (Class 12th) education can enrol in the undergraduate degree in Commerce, which is offered as a part-time or full-time programme.



A student who completes this course will get conceptual understanding and analytical abilities that will aid them in numerous fields of work. This course seeks to provide a thorough understanding of accounting, finance, taxation, law, and management, among other topics.



B.COM PROFESSIONAL: COURSE HIGHLIGHTS

Course Level

Graduation

Duration

3 years

Examination type

Either Semester or Yearly System

Eligibility

50%-60% minimum marks in Class 12th/Higher Secondary/HSC Examination

Admission Process

Entrance Exams based on the college and university being selected

Course Fee

INR 14,000 to 2.5 Lakhs



STREAMS OR FIELD RELATED TO THIS COURSE

Accounting and Auditing

International Banking

Information Technology Services

Government Services

Telecommunication Services and BPO’s

Automobile, 

Retail

Tax Advisory Services

Financial Services

Commercial Banking

Insurance Services

Manufacturing Services

Hospitality

Media



REASONS TO OPT FOR B.COM PROFESSIONAL



The rich employment options in the field of business and commerce are one of the numerous reasons to pursue this study. Accounting, General Aptitude, Marketing, and Finance are just a few of the challenging professions available.

This also prepares students for exams like the MBA, CA, Actuaries, CFA, and other financial exams.

After a reasonable number of promotions, one can easily advance to mid and senior management positions.





B.Com Professional: Jobs Profile



f Job Profile

Description 

Business Analyst

Although this role is typically offered at the MBA level, many companies are on the lookout for BA roles to give to fresh graduates passing out from reputed colleges in the country. It also gives a practical viewpoint of the entire industry as a whole

Business Consultant

Consultancy encapsulates a wide variety of roles and industries and companies are always on the lookout for hiring top candidates in this role

Financial Risk Manager

One of the numerous renowned positions available to students once they complete a B.Com (Professional) programme. Risk management, financial analysis, and wealth management are examples of job positions.

Auditor

One of the most crucial aspects for any company is to verify their financial transactions with a third party and herein comes the auditor with an exceptional and desirable skill set

Investment Banker

An investment banker is in charge of portfolios, which he or she manages so that their value continues to rise.

Account Executive

An account executive has to work with various other teams like marketing, sales, finance and coordinate the various activities of the business

Government Jobs

 Because there are multiple government tests held throughout the year, this is always a potential employment opportunity for every graduate. . For a B.Com (Professional) graduate, banking jobs would be more in alignment with their overall career trajectory

Insurance Manager

An insurance manager will be in charge of running an insurance company's day-to-day operations.





HIGHER STUDIES AFTER B.COM PROFESSIONAL

•	Masters of Business Administration. 

•	Masters in Commerce (M.Com)

WHAT ARE THE ENTRY-LEVEL SALARY PACKAGES & AVERAGE SALARY PACKAGES FOR B.COM PROFESSIONAL?

Name of Job Profile

Average Salary Range in INR

Business Analyst

4 to 6 Lakhs

Business Consultant

6 to 10 Lakhs

Financial Risk Manager

10 to 17 Lakhs

Auditor

3 to 6 Lakhs

Investment Banker

2.5 to 30 Lakhs

Account Executive

3.5 to 5 Lakhs

Government Jobs

5 to 12 Lakhs

Insurance Manager

7 to 10 Lakhs







TOP ENTRANCE EXAMS FOR B.COM PROFESSIONALS

Some colleges also conduct their own examinations.

 Banaras Hindu University - Undergraduate Admission Test (BHU UET),

 NMIMS NPAT

Telangana Gurukulam Undergraduate Common Entrance Test (TGUGCET)

Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test are some of the other common entrance tests (IPU CET)

title: B. Com Economics
description: BCom Economics is a three-year undergraduate programme in economics. This curriculum focuses on teaching students the fundamentals of business. The goal of this course is to teach students about many areas of operations, cost accounting, and business.
details:
BCOM ECONOMICS 

About the course

BCom Economics is a three-year undergraduate programme in economics. This curriculum focuses on teaching students the fundamentals of business. The goal of this course is to teach students about many areas of operations, cost accounting, and business.



To be eligible to apply to this programme, candidates must have completed Class 12 or equivalent with Mathematics as a required subject from a recognised board.

Admissions to BCom Economics are normally made based on a candidate's performance in an entrance exam or on merit. Financial accounting, business communication, partnership accounting, macroeconomics, and other disciplines are covered in the BCom Economics Syllabus.

BCom Economics Course Highlights 

Course level



Undergraduate

Full form 



Bachelors in Commerce in Economics

Duration 



3 years

Eligibility 



50% aggregate in Class 12 from a recognized board.

Admission Process



Entrance/ Merit-Based

Average Annual Fees



INR 5,000 - 6,00,000

Average Annual Salary



INR 3,60,000 - 6,00,000



Why should you study B.Com in Economics-

The B.Com Economics programme is a three-year undergraduate programme that focuses on a commerce degree with concentration and opportunities in economics and business management techniques. Mathematics, economics, and commerce are the three subjects that are related to each other.

This course motivates Students to graduate and work for the best organizations & companies. The course is all about research and production and distribution of goods and services in the industrial sectors. The job opportunities for the young graduate students  are available in most of the sectors, public or private industries or companies such as banking, auditing, financial companies, research companies, account management, etc. 

With increased career scope and chances, the pay of graduates with a B.Com in Economics is also increasing.

What Do Economics graduates do?

BCom economics is a three-year undergraduate programme for graduates with a variety of job prospects available after completion. Banking, finance, and treasury are some of the most frequent professional opportunities.

Research and Development: 

Economists also conduct research and development on their business practises over time.

Designing Product: 

During the examinations, the designed idea is scrutinised, and a significant change is made.

Systems Management: 

Systems management processes the work of competent and experienced economists..

Marketing:

After completing the assignment and creating the idea, the product is sold out in the market to top companies who want to use the business idea.

Reasons Why B.Com Economics Can Fetch You a Rewarding Career?

Demand: 

At first, people's desire for business and commerce is strong, but with time, business practices and instruction have evolved into new forms. 

The need for economists rises in tandem with the emergence of new business ideas in everyday life. Third, firms and industries rely on economists for new ideas and strategies to grow their empires.



Job Growth and a Reasonable Salary: 

The average income for a B.Com Economics in India is INR 7 LPA, according to Payscale, with the maximum package being INR 25 LPA. This is dependent on the candidates' experience and abilities.

Top Job opportunities-

Salary

INR 14 LPA

INR 15 LPA

INR 12 LPA

INR 9 LPA

INR 7 LPA

M.Com: If a student desires to continue in the same field of education, he/ she can opt for an M.Com Economics course which also comes with a master's in business economics, master's in applied economics etc.

Competitive examinations: BCom degree holders also have the option of preparing for competitive examinations. They can sit for a variety of government job examinations and obtain a respected position.

Ample opportunities are present in the field of the banking sector, stock exchangers, BPOs, Financial Agencies, Financial Department of various industries, BPOs and many more.

Students can also peruse a master's in Economics after a B.com.

The following are some of the most popular BCom Economics Entrance Exams:

BHU UET: It is a national level entrance exam which is conducted by the Banaras Hindu University to select candidates for admission to BSc, B

title: B.Com Management Studies
description: THE BACHELOR OF COMMERCE (B.COM) IN MANAGEMENT STUDIES
details:
THE BACHELOR OF COMMERCE (B.COM) IN MANAGEMENT STUDIES

ABOUT

The Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com) in Management Studies is a three-year undergraduate management programme that covers organisational behaviour, how organisations work, how to explore the principles of managing people in organisations, and how to develop a modern perspective by examining a part of the context.



10+2 with a minimum of 65 percent is necessary for admission to the B.Com in Management Studies programme. B.Com in Management Studies admission is based on a merit list or an entrance exam.

OVERVIEW

Course Level

Undergraduate

Full form

Bachelor's of Commerce in Management Studies

Duration

3 years

Age

17 years

Examination type

Semester type

Eligibility criteria

Candidates must have passed their 10+2 with a minimum of 60% marks from any recognized education board.

Admission Process

Based on merit-list or score in the entrance exam.

Course Fee

Average fee ranges from INR 10,000 to 1,00,000



WHY SHOULD YOU OPT THIS COURSE?



Great Job: Students seeking a B.Com in Management Studies should expect high employment possibilities because every organisation nowadays needs a Commerce specialist, analyst, or accountant to operate a profitable operation.

Candidates who complete the course will learn how to manage organisations and how to establish a sense of effective management, which is critical for creating innovative and productive settings.

B.Com in Management Studies graduates can work as teachers and lecturers, quality specialists, assistant professors, and business development managers. This provides a clear path into the labour market.

B.Com in Management Studies also increases presenting and problem-solving abilities, allowing qualified people to discover a wide range of work prospects in the corporate sector.





STREAMS RELATED TO B.COM MANAGEMENT STUDIES-

Foundation of Human Skills

Principles of Management I

Introduction to Financial Accounts

Introduction to Computers

Business Law

Business Environment

Business Statistics

Industrial Law

Business Communications

Computers application in Business





JOB SCOPE OF B.COM MANAGEMENT STUDIES 











Job position

Description

Business Analyst

Planning, monitoring, aiding with the business case, and managing needs are all responsibilities of business analysts. They also give problem-solving solutions and product testing.

Data Analyst

Data analysts are in charge of creating reporting systems, automating data processes, monitoring audit data, and evaluating data. Data analysts compile all of the information into dashboards, benchmarking, and evaluating data sets, utilising a variety of tools and approaches.

Project Manager

A project manager is in charge of planning, budgeting, and managing a team's work on a particular project. A project manager might operate in a variety of advertising sectors.

Management Consultant

Management consultants are in charge of identifying management issues such as sales, supply, and manufacturing, as well as providing solutions to such issues by applying their expertise and aptitude to give advice and assistance in producing a specialty product.

Forensic Accountants

A forensic accountant is in charge of evaluating the reports, disseminating them, discussing with them, and finding the issues and solutions. They create, produce, and suggest improvements to reports.









HIGHER STUDIES AFTER COMPLETING B.COM MANAGEMENT STUDIES

A B.Com in Management Studies graduate can pursue an MMS/M.Com, which is a higher level of management studies degree.

Masters of Business Administration [MBA]: MBA is the most common post-graduate education choice for graduates from any area, not only those with a Commerce background. A new postgraduate might easily get an annual pay package of INR 7,50,000 after completing the MBA.

What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for B.COM management studies graduate?



Job position

Average Salary

Business Analyst

INR 4,00,000 to 6,00,000

Data Analyst

INR 3,00,000 to 4,00,000

Project Manager

INR 8,00,000 to 10,00,000

Management Consultant

INR 6,00,000 to 7,00,000

Forensic Accountants

INR 6,00,000 to 7,00,000





TOP ENTRANCE EXAM TO GET INTO YOUR DESIRED B.COM MANAGEMENT STUDIES COLLEGE 	







IPU CET- This test is for both undergraduate and postgraduate students. A total of 117 colleges have signed up to participate. Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University holds this test every year. For which a substantial number of students have filled out applications.	

CUCET- The Common University Entrance Test, formerly known as the Central Universities Common Entrance Test, is an all-India test administered by the National Testing Agency for admission to 45 Central Universities of India's Undergraduate, Integrated, Postgraduate, Diploma, Certification, and Research Programs.



B.COM MANAGEMENT STUDIES: TOP COLLEGES

Some of the Top colleges that offer B.Com in Manage

title: B. Com Statics
description: B.Com. Statistics is not only academic, but also includes certain practical topics. It is a six-semester curriculum, with certain universities using a credit system.
eligibility: Admission can be granted based on both entrance exams and merit.
details:
B.COM STATISTICS

ABOUT

B.Com. Statistics is not only academic, but also includes certain practical topics. It is a six-semester curriculum, with certain universities using a credit system.

Statistics is the science of gathering, storing, assessing, interpreting, and presenting numerical and categorical data. A bachelor's degree in business administration is known as a B.Com. For the holistic development of students, the (Statistics) degree programme covers Actuarial Science, Applied Information Economics, Biostatistics, Business Statistics, Data Analysis, Demography, Econometrics, and other particular areas.







Analysis, probability theory, statistical techniques, database management systems, and other topics are covered in the advanced education curriculum. In the financial field, the degree programme is critical.



OVERVIEW

B.Com. Statistics is a three-year full-time undergraduate programme in statistics.

Applicants must have received a 10+2 diploma from a recognised board or university.

They had to study English as well as one of the following subjects: accounting, business studies, economics, or mathematics.

Admission can be granted based on both entrance exams and merit.

The admission exam can be taken both online and offline.

Group Discussions and Personal Interview rounds are also held at certain universities.

The usual course costs range from INR 5,000 to INR 5 lakhs.



WHY SHOULD YOU OPT FOR THIS COURSE



Students can pursue higher study (M.Com., MBA, etc.) or a job in banking, insurance, finance, auditing, or accountancy in both the public and private sectors after finishing a B.Com. (Statistics) degree programme.

Entry-level work in banking, insurance, and other financial industries are best suited for those having a B.Com. (Statistics) degree.

The B.Com. (Statistics) degree provides as a basis for further study in this field, such as M.A., Ph.D., and M.Phil. degrees in Statistics, all of which qualify one for a teaching post at any university or institution upon completion.

Statisticians are needed anywhere there is data, and nearly every business requires well-formulated and organised data, therefore those with a B.Com. (Statistics) degrees have a number of job opportunities.



STREAMS RELATED TO THIS COURSE







Actuarial Science

Applied Information Economics

Biostatistics, Business Statistics

Data Analysis

Demography

Econometrics

Energy Statistics

Engineering Statistics

Psychological Statistics

Social Statistics





B.COM STATISTICS: JOB PROFILES 

Some of the posts that are offered to a B.Com. Statistics graduates are:

Job Profile

Job Description

Salary (INR)

Accountant

Creates asset, liability, and capital account entries by organising and dissecting account data. Enters account information to record monetary transactions. Examines bookkeeping choices in order to assign financial responsibilities.

INR 1 LPA- INR 5 LPA

Cashier/ Teller

The account services provided to customers include receiving deposits and loan payments, cashing checks, issuing savings withdrawals, recording night and mail deposits, selling cashier's checks, traveler's checks, and series e bonds, answering questions in person or over the phone, and referring to other bank services.

INR 1 LPA- INR 3 LPA

Biostatistician

Biostatistics integrates math, measurements, and science to give answers for addressing and avoiding basic medical problems. In investigations on general health and treatments, biostatisticians employ factual standards.

INR 10 LPA- INR 12 LPA

Econometrician

An Econometrician focuses on factual information and financial components in their area of expertise, such as work and back. They collect, analyse, and communicate data using a quantifiable approach and numerical model in order to explain financial phenomena and forecast market slants.

INR 13 LPA- INR 18 LPA







HIGHER COURSES AFTER B.COM. STATISTICS



M.Com. (Statistics)

M.Phil. (Statistics)

Ph.D. (Statistics)









ENTRY-LEVEL SALARY PACKAGES FOR B.COM STATISTICS GRADUATES

Some of the posts that are offered to a B.Com. Statistics graduates are:

Job Profile

Salary (INR)

Accountant

INR 1 LPA- INR 5 LPA

Cashier/ Teller

INR 1 LPA- INR 3 LPA

Biostatistician

INR 10 LPA- INR 12 LPA

Econometrician

INR 13 LPA- INR 18 LPA







B.COM ENTRANCE EXAMS

JET: The JET Exam Board, an autonomous body within the Ministry of Corporate Affairs of the Government of India, conducts a competitive examination in India.

CUET: This is organised by the Central University for various UG courses for 45 colleges across India.

BHU Undergraduate Entrance Test: Banaras Hindu University's undergraduate entrance exam, which includes B.Com., is known as the BHU Entrance Exam. B.Com. from HU The entrance exam is computer-based and takes place in 200 places around the world. On the official website of Banaras Hindu University, interested candidates can fill out online applications. 

Dayanand Sagar Admission Test: - The Day

title: B. Com Supply Chain Management
details:
BCom Supply Chain Management





What is this course?



BCom Supply Chain Management is an undergraduate specialised study divided into six semesters. The goal of this course is to ensure that the chain operates efficiently and effectively while lowering costs and increasing customer satisfaction.



Any organisation's success depends on having ample and suitable administrative abilities as well as a firm grasp of supply chains. BCom Supply Chain Management focuses on preparing students to deal with business difficulties and pressures in a competitive market environment.



BCom Supply Chain Management is a vibrant and active programme that provides students with comprehensive understanding of the rapidly increasing field of supply chain management. A well-executed supply chain management system has been identified as the primary tool in today's most important company and employment victories.



An overview of this course



Type of Course

Undergraduate

Duration

2 years divided into 6 semesters

Examination Type

Semester-Based

Eligibility

The eligibility criterion for this course is a 10+2 or identical qualification in most preferably commerce streams from a recognized university, college, or educational board, with a minimum aggregate score of 50%.

Admission Process

Merit-based or Entrance exam-based or based on counselling once they qualify for the entrance exam.

Fees

INR. 35,000- INR. 11,00,000

Average Salary

INR. 2,00,000- INR. 20,00,000 [Average]











Why should you opt for this course?



The goal of BCom Supply Chain Management is to equip students with hands-on experience and information in order for them to become specialists in this industry.



This course will assist students in pursuing and excelling in the field of commerce or management. It also trains graduates with entrepreneurial experience to advance in the corporate sphere. It gives financial, business, and professional information that may be used to improve one's future.



Students would flourish in the sphere of business or management.

Students that have a strong interest in supply chain management administration should opt for this.

This curriculum is designed to provide students with knowledge of current ways of handling management challenges as well as novel conclusions.

This course is also appropriate for students who want to pursue further degrees, such as research work, once it is completed.



Fields under this course





The Supply Chain Management Specialisation consists of four fundamental courses in logistics, operations, planning, and sourcing, as well as a capstone course in Supply Chain Management Strategy. 

Transportation, warehousing and inventory, and logistics network architecture will all be covered in the Supply Chain Logistics course.

 Functional English 

 Humanities and social science foundations 

 Economic theory 

 business organisation 

 Fundamentals of accounting 

 Business maths statistics 

 Workplace skills 

 SCM delivery operations 

 General English 

 Science and technology foundation 

Business law 

Advanced accountancy 

 Money banking 









Career opportunities after completing this course







Job Profile

Job Description

Supply chain consultant

Their job includes organising management consultants and resolving supply-chain issues for customers. Import, export, and shipping charges are all part of their job. They concentrate on the task that is the most cost-effective for delivering manufacturer's goods.

Quality Manager

Their job is to ensure that the items and products are of acceptable quality. They must ensure that personnel coordination and production procedures are in accordance with the company's requirements. They provide novel methods for improving product quality and standards.

Production Manager

They are usually in charge of a staff of assistant directors and production coordinators. In the realm of employment, they have power over scheduling, managing, budgeting, and recruiting insurance difficulties. They guarantee manufacturing safety, union regulations, and guild.

Operation Assistant

They collaborate with other employees and supervisors to ensure that the firm runs smoothly. They work in a variety of industries, with different duties allocated to them. They are responsible for dealing with client complaints, checking trade, and filling orders.

Manager

They usually keep an eye out for clients, check through portfolios, examine writings, or do audits. They maintain the company up to speed with current industry trends by reading product magazines and networking with others.



Higher education after completing this course



Ph.D., Doctor of Philosophy

After completion, students can also opt for a doctoral degree, i.e., a Ph.D. at  universities like the University of Delhi and the University of Hyderabad, etc.

PURSUE M. Com

M.Com. degrees are also available to students. After finishing this course, many students choose to pursue an M.Co

title: B. Com Tax Procedure and Practice
description: B.COM IN TAX PROCEDURE AND PRACTICE
details:
B.COM IN TAX PROCEDURE AND PRACTICE 







WHAT IS B.COM TAX PROCEDURE?

Bachelor of Commerce in Tax Procedure and Practice (B.Com (Tax Procedure and Practice)) is an undergraduate accounting study. 



This course assists students in developing economics, accounting, and finance abilities. There are several forms of taxes, such as VAT, property tax, income tax, service tax, and so on.



Students enrolled in a Bachelor of Commerce programme in Tax Procedure and Practice are often exposed to aspects of indirect taxes such as wealth tax, customs tax, and central excise tax. 



The Indian Tax System, Principles of Management, Financial Accounting, Value Added Tax and Central Tax Procedure and Business Communication are among the subjects covered in the Bachelor of Commerce degree programme in Tax Procedure and Practice.



Quick Overview of this Course -



Full-Form

Bachelor of Commerce (Tax Procedure and Practice)

Duration

3 years

Type

Degree

Selection Mode

Merit-based or Entrance examination

Course Level

Graduate

Examination Type

Semester and Annual

Eligibility

10+2 or any equivalent exam with Commerce as one of the Subjects

Syllabus

Indian Tax System, Value Added Tax, etc.

Admission Process

Entrance exam and Merit-based

Average Course Fee

Ranges between INR 5000 to INR 10,00,000



Why should you opt for this course-



The B. Com (Tax Procedure and Practice) course assists students in becoming knowledgeable in taxes, finance, and accounting topics.



Following a B. Com (Tax Procedure and Practice) with further courses will boost a candidate's employment chances.



The programme trains students for both entrepreneurial and corporate life.



You can also work for the government, commercial companies, or start your own business.



They can advance their careers by pursuing postgraduate degrees such as M.B.A. and M.Com, or by enrolling in professional courses such as CA, CS, and ICWA.

Fields under this course- 

Indian Tax System

Financial Accounting

Principles of Management

Value Added Tax and Central Tax Procedure

Financial Accounting and Business Communication.

Career opportunities after graduating from this course: -







This course teaches students financial, bookkeeping, and accounting skills. 



There are several types of assessments, including Value Added Duty, Property Charge, Income Charge, and Service Charge, among others. 



Individuals who pursue this course are frequently exposed to the intricacies of erroneous charges such as riches charge, traditions evaluation, and focused extract charge.

INR 3 to 4 LPA

PhD: It is a second program option for students who desire to continue in the same field of education. It is a three-to-five-year programme with qualifying requirements, including a BCom degree in a relevant field.

title: B. Com Capital markets
details:
BCOM CAPITAL MARKET



What is BCom Capital Market?



The course focuses on finance and prepares students for positions such as accounting officer, accounting firm partner, finance director, audit manager, and other related positions.

Finance, investing, stocks, and other topics are covered in this course.

The capital market is a dynamic and ever-changing profession that necessitates knowledge and experience working with statistics and data.

B.Com. Capital Markets is a programme that provides intensive training to individuals interested in these subjects.







Commodities, gold prices, financial assets, foreign exchange policies, economic policy, and other topics are covered in this course.

If interested applicants enrol in the programme, they will have access to many job and research possibilities.



HIGHLIGHTS OF THIS COURSE

Course Level

Graduate

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester System

Eligibility

10+2 with Commerce subjects as focus

Admission Process

Based on counseling after qualification of entrance tests such as BHU-CET

Course Fee

INR 40,000 to 1,00,000

Average Starting Salary

INR 2 to 8 LPA



STREAMS RELATED TO THIS COURSE

Following are some important stress for this course



Principles of Investment

The environment of Financial System

Micro – Economics

Macroeconomics

Basic Statistics

Statistical Applications

Basic Computer Skills

Communication Skills

Financial Accounting

Management Accounting

Business Environment

Principles of Business Management









WHY SHOULD YOU OPT FOR BCOM CAPITAL MARKET

Because of its complex course structure, the course has numerous advantages. The following are some of the major advantages of pursuing a B.Com Capital Market degree:

It's a hands-on course with a syllabus that's relevant to today's economic demands.

Students in B.Com. can now specialise in Finance as part of their Bachelor's degree rather than a Master's degree.

Graduates of the programme will be prepared with practical and professional understanding of the capital and stock markets, making them instantly employable.

The degree leads to several work opportunities in a variety of industries, as well as appropriate jobs in government financial agencies.



Career opportunities after graduating B.com capital market:-



Organizations associated with Finance and Economics, as well as job descriptions in this area, are common career alternatives and employment prospects for B.Com. Capital Market graduates. The following are the annual wages and job descriptions:



Job Position

Job Description

Accounting Associate

They assist Auditors and Accountants with clerical work, bookkeeping, and account administration. They also communicate with vendors, clients, and departments to handle overdue payments. They handle records and pay invoice invoices and transactions.

Directors (Accounts)

The Director of the Accounts Department is in charge of supervising executives seeking advice and direction. They are in charge of the accounting department's general expansion.

Accountant

By assessing and compiling account information, accountants assist in the preparation of liability, asset, and other entries. They also offer suggestions for examining account data.

Accounts Receivable Clerk

They are in charge of cash, credit card, and check transactions, as well as client payment records. They also assist with revenue posting by inputting and validating transactions from local depositories and lockboxes.

Bookkeeper

By designing applicable accounting systems, bookkeepers are responsible for creating account charts of financial transactions. They also establish bookkeeping rules and processes, as well as maintain subsidiary accounts by assigning, confirming, and publishing transactions, as well as ensuring that entries for subsidiary accounts are balanced.

Chief Accounting Officer

Chief Accounting Officers are in charge of an organization's whole accounting department. They are in charge of accounting operations, financial reporting, and the whole accounting and administrative workforce.

Accounting Firm Partner



Accounting Firm Partners performs audits and accounting to determine the financial state of a company. They aid in the organization's reputation building by clearly documenting and presenting transactions.

Audit Manager

Audit Managers are in charge of coordinating and planning an organization's internal audits in accordance with the yearly audit plan, as well as managing the audit team.

Forensic Accountants

They are in charge of researching and auditing the financial statements in order to look for any fraud. Forensic accountants are experts in the field of accounting.







What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course:-

Because the financial industry is always evolving and of highest significance, with new things coming up every year, the Capital Market may be flexible in terms of what they want to pursue. Students can pursue post-graduat

title: B.com Taxation
details:
B.COM TAXATION 

WHAT IS B.COM TAXATION?

The Bachelor of Commerce in Taxation is a three-year degree programme. Of course, the type is still in school.

Students will learn about taxation and bookkeeping, as well as managing monetary arrangements.

Taxation, finance, and accounting students develop into professionals.

Because they specialise in taxation, finance, or accounting, they are given the simpler job of leadership or management in the financial industry.

A vast range of options has opened up in this profession, and more people are considering it as a career option.







Overview of B.Com Taxation Course 

Course Level

Undergraduate

Full form

Bachelor of Commerce in Taxation

Duration

3 Years

Examination type

Semester

Eligibility

10+2 examination with English and Mathematics

Admission process

Merit-based or entrance exam

Course fee

INR 5,000 - INR 5 lakhs

Average salary

INR 1.2 lakhs - INR 10 lakhs



Fields of this course 

Business mathematics

Financial accounting

Income tax law procedure

Income tax law procedure

Fundamental principles of accounting

Partnership accounting

Business organization

Business regulatory framework

Indian contract act and sale of goods act

Business communications













Why should you opt for B.Com in Taxation?



After earning their bachelor's degree, students can pursue a lucrative career in the field of taxes.

Students interested in becoming tax consultants can take this course to learn how to establish business plans.

Students will study managerial and quantitative tools to expand their career options.

You can work in marketing, foreign commerce, public accounting, and other professions.





Career opportunities after graduating B.com Taxation

Chartered Accountant, financial risk manager, tax consultant, tax expert, senior tax manager, tax compliance manager, cost estimators, lecturer, stockbrokers, financial analyst, actuaries, and more employment responsibilities are available after completing a B. Com in Taxation.



Job Profiles

Job Description

Tax Specialist

Tax professionals, often known as tax advisors, play a vital role. They are professionals that specialise in tax law, planning, and compilation. Businesses and individuals, as well as clients, will benefit from the updated new tax legislation and services.

Senior Tax Manager

Senior tax managers play an important role in evaluating tax paperwork and preparing timely tax returns. They guarantee that the company's accounting operations comply with state and local requirements.

Actuaries

An actuary supervises and analyses a company's or organization's financial status. They employ statistics, mathematics, and the company's financial data to forecast a future occurrence.

Stockbrokers

A stockbroker's job is to sell and purchase equities on the stock exchange. They make purchases and sales on behalf of investors.

Cost Estimators

A cost estimator's job is gathering data and evaluating it in order to meet the needs of manufacturing a product, constructing a building, or providing any other service. They look at things like time, money, labour, and other resources.









Higher studies after completing this course

Master of Business Administration (MBA), M.Com, Chartered Accountant (CA), Company Secretary (CS), Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA), Business accounting and taxation (BAT), certified management account (CMA), actuarial science, B. Ed., US Certified public accounting (CPA) are some of the higher studies available after completing a B.Com in Taxation



What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for this course?



Job Profiles

Average Annual Salary

Tax Specialist

INR 6.87 lakhs

Senior Tax Manager

INR 30 lakhs

Actuaries

INR 15 lakhs to INR 20 lakhs

Stockbrokers

INR 3.95 lakhs

Cost Estimators

INR 11.73 lakhs





Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?

Following are some famous entrance exam for this course

JUEE: Jammu University Entrance Test

IPU CET: Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test

CUCEE: Central Universities Common Entrance Test, etc.

title: B.COM Computer Science
details:
B.COM COMPUTER SCIENCE 

About

With the growth of business and commerce, the demand for additional apps that may simplify transactions and other processes has grown. The B.Com. The Computer Science programme combines commerce with computer science to create a new genre for managing an organization's operations.

In order to tackle jobs connected to accounting and programming, the programme combines commerce and computer science. Candidates must practise and concentrate intensely in order to internalise the abilities required to fully comprehend the subject. This course benefits from understanding of programming, computer hardware, and software, as well as a general interest in mathematics and science.



Course Highlights

Course level

Graduate

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester

Eligibility

Must Qualifying 10+2 examination in business & commerce with a minimum aggregate of 50% marks

Admission process

Merit List

Course Fee

Up to INR 10,200 to 1.95 Lacs

Average salary

Approx. Up to INR 2 to 7 Lacs per annum



Why should you opt for B.com Computer Science?



Computer engineers' job market is continually increasing, and Computer Science students have a greater chance of getting a job. Another excellent way for them to land a good job with a major corporation is through campus placement. Thousands of new employment are expected to be created in the following years.



Education, health, finance, trade, science, engineering, and transportation are among fields where computer science careers may be found. Every sector has software being created. Computer scientists are charged with streamlining the job in each of them.



B.Com Computer Applications graduates can get work in a variety of IT and commerce companies. Students with a B.Com Computer Application degree are employed in a variety of growing areas, including edutech, fintech, and software. Students develop programming skills as well as business knowledge over the course, preparing them for a number of careers.

Fields related to the scores

Income tax law & Practice

Accounting Principles	

Introduction to Information Technology

Principles of Management

E-Commerce	

HTML 

JavaScript

Career opportunities after graduating B.Com Computer Science 

B.Com Computer Applications graduates can get work in a variety of IT and commerce companies. Students with a B.Com Computer Application degree are employed in a variety of growing areas, including edutech, fintech, and software. Students develop programming skills as well as business knowledge over the course, preparing them for a number of careers. Graduates' work opportunities are not limited to a particular industry, but rather include a wide range of fields. Some of the greatest professional professions for B.Com Computer Application graduates are as follows:

Chartered Accountant

Business Analyst

Budget Analyst

Financial Consultant

Computer Software Developer

Auditor

Computer Programmer

App Developer

Higher Studies after Completing This Course

Animation-After BCom, an animation course might be one of the greatest computer courses available. Students will learn how to make animations in this course.

Web Designing- Managing online pages and their structures, developing content and layout for websites, and constructing new websites for customers are all examples of web design.

MCM-Master of Computer Management is an acronym for Master of Computer Management. It's a two-year postgraduate computing programme.

Master of computer application-Master of Computer Application is abbreviated as MCA. It's also a post-graduate programme that takes between two and three years to finish ( two years for BCA students and three years for other relevant stream students ).

Master of Science is abbreviated as M.Sc. It's a post-graduate programme. It's the type of study that can be followed after graduation. The majority of students enrol in this programme to study a specific topic, such as mathematics, chemistry, or physics.
