title: B.Com Accounting
details:
B.COM IN ACCOUNTING



What is B.com Accounting?



B.Com Accounting is a three-year bachelor's degree programme in commerce. It is organised into six semesters, each with an examination at the end.

Financial accounting, company and management accounting, corporate tax legislation and practice, and other topics are covered in this course.

Banks, colleges, schools, hospitals, law firms, multinational corporations, government sectors such as the income tax agency and the Public Works Department, industries, and hotels are all potential employers for this course.

Accountant, Finance Manager, Operations Manager, Account Executive, Financial Analyst, Executive Assistant, Chartered Accountant, Chief Accounting Officer, Accounting Firm Partner, Finance Director, Audit Manager, and other positions will be available to students.







BCom Accounting: Course Highlights

Course Level

Undergraduate

Full Form

Bachelor of Commerce in Accounting

Duration of the Course

3 years

Examination Type

Semester wise

Eligibility

45% marks in class 12 from recognized Education board

Admission Process

Entrance exam based / Merit Based





Course Fee

Between INR 10,000 to 1 lakhs

Average Starting Salary

INR 2 LPA to 10 LPA



Field Under B.com Accounting:



Business Consultancies.

Educational Institutes.

Industrial Houses.

Public Accounting Firms.

Policy Planning.

Foreign Trade.

Banks.

Budget Planning.



Why Should you opt for this B.com Accounting?



Students can pursue higher education in India or overseas and secure a high-paying job.

They can pursue higher education in subjects such as M.Com, MBA, and others.

What is B.com Accounting?



B.Com Accounting is a three-year bachelor's degree programme in commerce. It is organised into six semesters, each with an examination at the end.

Financial accounting, company and management accounting, corporate tax legislation and practice, and other topics are covered in this course.

Banks, colleges, schools, hospitals, law firms, multinational corporations, government sectors such as the income tax agency and the Public Works Department, industries, and hotels are all potential employers for this course.

Accountant, Finance Manager, Operations Manager, Account Executive, Financial Analyst, Executive Assistant, Chartered Accountant, Chief Accounting Officer, Accounting Firm Partner, Finance Director, Audit Manager, and other positions will be available to students.

title: B.Com Marketing
description: B.Com Marketing is a bachelor's degree programme in marketing that prepares students for professional jobs in the sector.
details:
B.COM MARKETING

About-

B.Com Marketing is a bachelor's degree programme in marketing that prepares students for professional jobs in the sector.

Bachelor's degree in commerce- A marketing degree focuses on marketing management and planning approaches and procedures. Students follow a set of steps across the curriculum, culminating in the creation of a marketing strategy. The curriculum focuses on established procedures and the application of theory in the marketing environment, including research, the nature of consumers, sales management, advertising, quality management, legislation, and ethics.



The curriculum also teaches students critical thinking skills by presenting them with practical marketing ideas, examples, and case studies, all of which help students strengthen their cognitive talents and design marketing plans for their companies.

B.Com Marketing: Course Highlights

Course Level

Graduate

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester System

Eligibility

HSC with 45 % aggregate marks

Admission Process

Merit based or entrance test based depending upon college or university.

Course Fee

INR 5,000 to 2 Lakh for entire course

Average Starting Salary

Rs. 2 to Rs. 10 lacs per annum

Top Recruiting Companies

Banks, Budget Planning, Inventory Control, Merchant Banking, Marketing, Business Consultancies, Educational Institutes, Industrial Houses, Public Accounting Firms, 

Policy Planning, Foreign Trade,

Job Positions

Trademark manager product and innovation manager Market development manager

 Distribution manager Research project manager Marketing manager Market research analysts

 Advertising managers Public relations managers

Fields/streams of the course-

Indian Economy



Principles of Marketing

Principles of Management



Statistics

Financial Accounting



Advanced Financial Accounting

Corporate Accounting



Banking System and Financial Services

Managerial Economics



Entrepreneurship Development and Small Business Management

Legal Systems of Business



Advanced Corporate Accounting

Practical Auditing



Creative Advertising

Elements of Cost Accounting



IDE - Tourism Management

Marketing Research & Consumer Behaviour



Management Accounting

Business Taxation



Retail Management

Integrated Marketing Communication



International Marketing



NME



Services Marketing





What are the Benefits of a B.Com Marketing Course?

The course will help you improve your research abilities and prepare you for a successful career in either the public or commercial sector.

They can also create their own advertising companies or form their consulting firm that provides marketing advice and recommendations to businesses.

Applicants may also have work in advertising, sales, management, and product promotion creative writing, among other fields.

Department shops, computer businesses, energy companies, construction companies, food producers, manufacturing companies, and arts groups are all places where students can work.

A successful Project Manager is one who can handle numerous tasks at once while maintaining a focus on the broad picture while also being extremely specific. The individual must have excellent organisational abilities and the ability to collaborate with others, even if they do not report directly to you. You must be able to communicate effectively and frequently with your team and management, highlighting any difficulties or problems as they develop. You must also be able to break down and plan tasks in order to allocate resources and calculate costs.

15,77,000 LPA

Market development manager

Resolve any issues or concerns raised by those partners. They are supported by solid research and financial data that drives their industry throughout the business negotiation process. As a result, they are able to guide marketing teams in the implementation of new marketing strategies.

607,935 LPA

Distribution manager

In charge of warehouse operations Order machine testing, maintenance, repairs, or replacements. They are also in charge of overseeing the transit of goods from the manufacturing site to the consumer to the customer. Make a logistical plan and put it into action.

570,000 LPA

Research project manager

The Research Project Manager is responsible for the program's strategy, management, technology approach, implementation methodology, quality control, and support; conducts complex experiments, data collection, and analysis; interprets and presents data to a variety of audiences; interfaces with multiple levels of the organisation and external entities to discuss services and technology; establishes and maintains collaborative relationships with administrators, faculties.

889,720 LPS

Marketing manager

Manages the marketing department's operations as well as the company's marketing. Oversees the company's marketing budget and coordinates marketing initiatives with sales activity. They also develop and carry out marketing strategies. Analyze the possibilities for strategic partner connections in the marketing of your organisation.

644,431 LPA

Advertising managers

A company's advertising operations are overseen by an advertising manager. During a specific advertising campaign, a manager must estimate the budget, authorise the advertising content, and evaluate the campaign's overall performance once it has ended.

500,505 LPA

Public relations managers

Public relations managers are in charge of maintaining a favourable public image for their clients, as well as creating press releases and programmes to help them do so. All public relations initiatives should be coordinated, and the social media staff should be directed to engage audiences in both conventional and new media. They also handle media enquiries and requests for interviews. Ongoing evaluation of collaboration, sponsorship, and advertising options.

484,604 LPA



Further/higher studies after B.Com Marketing-



            MBA-

MBA is the most popular and one of the greatest degrees offered to students following B.Com, not only for those graduating from commerce but for those graduating from any discipline. This course teaches you how to manage all aspects of a business based on your area of expertise.



           Chartered Accountancy (CA)-

The first subject that springs to mind as a career option for a commerce student in CA. After B.Com, CA is one of the greatest professional possibilities. Unlike an MBA, students can begin CA immediately after graduating from high school. CPT, IPCC, and CA Finals are the three stages of CA. After passing all three levels and completing a 2.5-year internship, you become a certified chartered accountant. Without a question, it is one of the most rewarding occupations.



Master of Commerce (M.Com)

The master's degree in commerce is known as M.Com.This degree can be achieved at any government-approved institution or college in India and takes two years to complete.This course will assist you in mastering the principles you learned as a B.Com student and applying them in the workplace.



 Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) -

The Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) credential is a globally recognised professional title offered by the CFA Institute.The CFA programme lasts around 2.5 years and is meant to assess and certify financial analysts' skills and integrity. If you're worried about post-B.com careers and salaries, CTA is an excellent option



Business Accounting and Taxation (BAT)

Still looking for choices before settling on a career path after BCom? The Business Accounting and Taxation (BAT) Course is a rigorously constructed programme by industry experts to prepare students for accounting job roles in the business. It's a fantastic job choice for B.Com graduates. This programme will not only provide you with hands-on experience, but it will also teach you how to use some of the most in-demand tools in the accounting and taxation business, making it one of the top courses after a B.com.

B.Com Marketing: Admission Process

The admission process of this course is based on the marks that are obtained in the 12th class examination. Students with higher grades have a better probability of getting into college than students with lower grades. In addition, there are some entry tests.

details:
B.COM IN ACCOUNTANCY

What is B.Com in Accountancy?

B.Com (Accountancy), sometimes known as Bachelor of Commerce in Accountancy, is a commerce course for undergraduates. Accounting is the art of expressing financial and commercial data. Accountancy teaches students how to apply and present a company's financial and statistical data.



It is one of the methods that the organisation uses to keep track of both its expenses and earnings.

The Bachelor of Commerce in Accountancy is a three-year programme that is career-oriented and pioneering in nature, opening many doors for applicants after they complete it in a variety of industries.

B.Com Accountancy Course Highlights-

Degree



BCom Accountancy

Full-Form



Bachelor of Commerce in Accountancy

Duration



Course Duration of Bachelor of Commerce in Accountancy is of 3 Years.

Age



There is no specific age limit

Minimum 

Percentage



A minimum of 45% to 50% is required in (10+2) to be eligible for admission in the course

Subjects Required



(10+2) Commerce stream

Average Fees Incurred



INR 5,000 to INR 1,00,000 per annum

Top Recruiting Companies



Every year Companies from the sectors of various Business Consultancies /agencies, Educational Institutes (both private/government), Industrial Houses, Public Accounting Firms, Policy Planning, Foreign Trade, Banks, Budget Planning, Inventory Control, Merchant Banking, and Marketing, etc come for the recruitment of candidates.

Average Salary Offered



INR 10,00,000 to INR 20,00,000 per annum

Employment Roles

Accountant, Finance Manager, Operations Manager, Account Executive, Financial Analyst, Executive Assistant, Chartered Accountant, etc.



B.Com Accountancy Subjects-

Many disciplines are required in the B.Com Accountancy course curriculum for students pursuing a degree in commerce, finance, or accounting. Some subjects are from plus two classes, and the course also includes some new topics. Let's take a closer look at the B.Com Accountancy subjects covered in the course:

Some of the B.Com Accountancy subjects listed in the course curriculum are listed below:

Core Subjects:

Financial Accounting

Banking Law and Operations

Retail Management

Corporate Accounting

Financial Management

Elective Courses:

Corporate Administration

Public Relations and Corporate Communication

Cost Accounting

Principles of Event Management

Costing Methods

Business Taxation

Why should you pursue a B.com in Accountancy-

There will always be a demand for BCom Accountancy graduates. The employment ratio is expected to rise in the near future. Some fascinating statistics regarding why people opt to pursue a BCom Accountancy degree include the following:

The course explores subjects such as economics, social science, cultural needs and aspects.

It is ensured that they are democratically run and can also be found in rural areas.

This aids in the preservation and protection of the member's interest in the field. An applicant interested in pursuing a career has a wide range of alternatives.

Because the aspect ratio is high and the field is evergreen, a candidate might choose to continue their education in this field.

Candidates with a bachelor's degree might choose between working in the public or private sector.





B.com in Accountancy job options-

The private and Public sectors provide good opportunities to BCom Graduates. Some of the popular professional jobs for graduates of this course are listed below with their salaries-

Job Position

Job Description

Average Annual Salary

A Business Analyst's job is to develop practical solutions for business-related challenges in order to sort out progressive sales of the company's efforts, beginning with altering, analysing, and recording requirements.

8,65,699 LPA

The Finance Officer's job is to help financial organisations develop procedures by making appropriate recommendations to improve intentional thinking and direction, financial and accounting data, and analysis. They are also in charge of setting functional goals that are aligned with corporate goals.

3,40,950 LPA

A Sales Analyst's job is to prepare, gather, and analyse sales forecast data in order to estimate sales targets. title: B.Com finance
details:
B.COM FINANCE

What is B.Com Finance?

BCom Finance is a bachelor's degree programme in business administration. The programme lasts three years (full time/part-time) and is divided into six semesters. Principles of financial planning, economic theory, micro and macroeconomics, money and banking system, commerce and its features, and other topics are covered in the course.



While academic courses, such as a B.Com degree, are designed to provide theoretical knowledge, professional courses, such as CS, and vocational courses, such as Banking & Finance, prepare students for employment.

Overview of the course 

Level

(UG)

 3 years/6 semesters (Full time /Part time)

Course fee

INR 10,000 – 6,00,000

Minimum qualification

10+2 with at least 50% marks in aggregate

Selection process

(Merit-based or entrance exam based )

MBA Finance

MBA Finance gives students a head start in the business world. It is available to students once they have completed their undergraduate studies. MBA students have access to a wide range of jobs in marketing, finance, information technology, and other fields.



Chartered Accountant (CA)

CA has been a popular alternative for students after completing their CA studies for decades. Unending professional advancement prospects, high pay scales, and so on are some of the main reasons for this. 

The best advantage is that students may prepare and take the C.A. and B.Com exams at the same time because they have identical syllabuses and subjects.



Company Secretary (CS)

The salary scales for CA and CS are both attractive. The main distinction is that CS is primarily concerned

 with the legal aspects of a company and will serve as an executive secretary to the Managing Director (MD) and Chief Executive Officer (CEO).



Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA)

CFA is the ideal choice for those who wish to pursue a career as an investment professional after 

completing their B.Com. CFA is one of the most well-respected qualifications among businesses all around

 the globe, and it gives worldwide work prospects.



Certified Management Accountant (CMA)

Companies benefit from CMAs. They offer firms a lucrative pay scale by drafting audit reports,

 assisting them in the preparation of tax filings, and other executive-level activities.



Actuarial Science





Banks, insurance companies, and other financial organizations all need an actuary on staff to assist them to maintain economic stability and assess risks.



Bachelor of Education (B.Ed)





The noblest vocation is teaching. After completing their B.Com, students who desire to share their knowledge as a teacher can pursue a B.Ed. It helps teachers improve their pedagogical knowledge and skills, which will enable them to educate primary pupils more effectively.



How Can You Get Into A Good BCom Finance College?

For many individuals, getting into the finest universities for a Bachelor of Commerce in Finance programme is a challenging undertaking. However, you may prevent problems by following the admissions process for B- institutions. There are two key grounds or pillars in the admissions process at premier universities.

You should be able to study a topic in-depth, thus you must have the necessary skills and determination, as well as be incredibly diligent when taking this course.

You will have no trouble getting into any B-school if you have solid information on these two.

Your percentage in 10+2 percentage should be a minimum of 55% in the exam from any recognized board throughout the country along with this your entrance exams rank should be the cut-off list for the particular college where you took the entrance exam. So in that,your rank should be very good only then the students will be able to take admissions to a good college.

If institutions need on-campus interviews, prepare as thoroughly as possible.



There are Some top entrance exams-



DUET - The Delhi University Entrance Test is held on a national basis by Delhi University to admit students to programmes such as PG, UG, MPhil, and PhD. DUET is administered by the National Testing Agency (NTA) on behalf of Delhi University. It's a computer-based exam with an online mode. Candidates must have secured at least 60% aggregate in four subjects in Class 12 to appear in this entrance exam.



The Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test (IPU CET) is a test that allows students to apply for admission to different undergraduate and postgraduate programmes at the university. The IPU CET (Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test) is a national level examination. Candidates must have completed a 10+2 or equivalent examination with a minimum of 55per percent aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics, as well as passing each subject separately. Candidates must also have completed English as a subject in high school.



Dayanand Sagar Admission Test (DSAT) is a national level test held by Dayananda Sagar University every year. Exams are accessible in two formats: online and offline. You'd also have two hours to complete all 75 questions.



BHU UET - The Banaras Hindu University Undergraduate Entrance Exam is held for admission to the university's and affiliated colleges' undergraduate programmes. It's a test that takes place entirely online. And you would get 2 and a half hours to solve all the questions.



The official entrance test for admissions to Undergraduate Degree and Integrated Degree Programs provided by constituent schools at the Mumbai, Shirpur, and Bengaluru campuses is the NPAT - NMIMS Program after Twelfth. The overall difficulty level of the NPAT is moderate. The time limit for the NPAT exam has been increased from 100 to 120 minutes.

details:
BACHELOR OF COMMERCE IN BANKING AND FINANCE

What is a Bachelor of Commerce in Banking and Finance?

The Bachelor of Commerce in Banking and Finance Management is a three-year programme with an intermediate 10+2 level as a prerequisite.

Banking is the process through which a financial intermediary takes deposits and then routes those funds into providing events, either through capital markets or directly. Insurance is essentially a risk management company that makes money by hedging against unknown risks.





Subjects studied under this program are Business Communication, Environmental Educations, Business Economics Paper, Accountancy, Financial Management Paper, Mathematical and Statistical Methods.

Business Communication, Environmental Educations, Business Economics Paper, Accountancy, Financial Management Paper, Mathematical and Statistical Methods are among the subjects covered in this curriculum.

Overview of the course 

Course

Bachelor of Commerce in Banking and Finance

Duration

3 years

Application Mode

Online and Offline

Examination Type

Semester Based

Placement

Provided by Institutes

Admission Procedure

Merit Basis

Eligibility

10+2 in Commerce from a recognized board



BCom Banking and Finance Subjects

The Subjects taught while pursuing the Bachelor of Commerce in Banking and finance program may vary from one college to the other but a few topics are listed below:

Environment and Management of financial services

Financial accounting

Introduction to Computer systems

Business Law

Laws Governing Banking and Insurance

Universal Banking

Management accounting

Entrepreneurship management

International Banking and Finance

Financial Reporting and Analysis

Auditing

Human Resource Management in Banking and Insurance

Turnaround Management

Business ethics and corporate governance



Why should you study BCom Banking and Finance?

Banking and Finance studies provide you with the foundation for a variety of vocations in banking, brokerage, consulting, funds management, insurance, and superannuation.

Someone interested in studying could work for a company in the financial markets or the government. Of course, this kind can transport them wherever in the globe and make money easy for them.

This type of education provides practical assistance as well as an awareness of banking and commercial issues.

Candidates will have developed a comprehensive and extensive grasp of the two study components after finishing the Bachelor of Commerce programme. They will have acquired the conceptual services and logical process required to address problems in their areas of expertise. In their future professions, they will be competent in developing operational policies, managing risks, and evaluating operational outcomes.

After completing this course, a candidate may opt to continue their education. Learners with a Bachelor of Commerce degree have a broad yet solid educational foundation in areas such as banking, economics, accounting, business management, finance, and more.

2 to 3 LPA

What is a Bachelor of Commerce in Banking and Finance?

The Bachelor of Commerce in Banking and Finance Management is a three-year programme with an intermediate 10+2 level as a prerequisite.

Banking is the process through which a financial intermediary takes deposits and then routes those funds into providing events, either through capital markets or directly. Insurance is essentially a risk management company that makes money by hedging against unknown risks.





Subjects studied under this program are Business Communication, Environmental Educations, Business Economics Paper, Accountancy, Financial Management Paper, Mathematical and Statistical Methods.

Business Communication, Environmental Educations, Business Economics Paper, Accountancy, Financial Management Paper, Mathematical and Statistical Methods are among the subjects covered in this curriculum.

Why should you study BCom Banking and Finance?

Banking and Finance studies provide you with the foundation for a variety of vocations in banking, brokerage, consulting, funds management, insurance, and superannuation.

Someone interested in studying could work for a company in the financial markets or the government. Of course, this kind can transport them wherever in the globe and make money easy for them.

This type of education provides practical assistance as well as an awareness of banking and commercial issues.

Candidates will have developed a comprehensive and extensive grasp of the two study components after finishing the Bachelor of Commerce programme. They will have acquired the conceptual services and logical process required to address problems in their areas of expertise. In their future professions, they will be competent in developing operational policies, managing risks, and evaluating operational outcomes.

After completing this course, a candidate may opt to continue their education. Learners with a Bachelor of Commerce degree have a broad yet solid educational foundation in areas such as banking, economics, accounting, business management, finance, and more.

What is it About?

Bachelor of Commerce in Banking and Insurance (B.Com (Banking & Insurance)) is an Undergraduate Commerce programme. 

The Bachelor of Commerce in Banking & Insurance degree includes coursework in banking, accounting, banking law, insurance law, insurance risk, and insurance regulations, among other topics. 



In other words, a Bachelor of Commerce in Banking & Insurance is a degree programme in which the major focus is on the systematic study of ideas in specific areas and issues connected to the Banking & Insurance business, in addition to a basic course study in Commerce. 

They can pursue further studies in a variety of professions after completing the Bachelor of Commerce programme in Banking and Insurance.

Semester System

Based on counselling after qualification of entrance test

Accountant, Financial Advisor, Marketing Agent, Sales Representative, among others.

Course Fee



INR 14,000 to 10 lakhs

BACHELOR’S OF ACCOUNTING AND FINANCE



What is this course?



Here is a description of the BCom Accounting and Finance course, including all a student needs to know about it. It offers information about the topics covered in this course, what you'll learn, and what skills you'll gain as a result of taking it:



Money, business, and management are all subjects covered in accounting and finance, with a focus on professional jobs in these fields.

Accounting is concerned with the examination of data for many areas of a business, whereas finance is clearly concerned with a business's monetary resource.

Accounting and finance degrees teach you about accounting procedures, business, industry, and finance.

This course will assist you in developing a variety of subject-specific and technical skills, as well as more general skills such as mathematics and quantitative abilities, oral and writing communication abilities, entrepreneurship, the capacity to argue and negotiate, and so on.





Highlights Of this course:



Course

BCom Accounting and Finance

Full form

Bachelor of Commerce in Accounting and Finance

Duration

3 years

Eligibility

10+2

Level

Undergraduate





Fields/ Streams of the course-

Auditing.

Budget analysis.

Business strategy.

Financial accounting.

Financial reporting.

Forensic accounting.

Information systems.

International accounting.





Why should you study this Course?

If you appreciate math and want to apply it to business, whether it's management, company law, or economics, accounting and finance can be a good fit.



Opportunities for employment: The programme prepares students for work in a variety of industries and areas. BCom accounting and finance graduates are mostly hired in the investment management and Banking and Financial Service Industry (BFSI) sectors, which are estimated to employ over 8.4 million people in the next 8-10 years.

Good remuneration: According to the payscale website, BCom accounting and finance graduates can expect to earn INR 492,424 per year. They are also expected to earn other benefits such as bonuses, TA, DA, provident funds, and retirement benefits.

As previously said, the course prepares students to work in a variety of industries. Every industry requires someone to crunch statistics.

Opportunities abroad and cultural exchanges can help you gain a better grasp of the global economy while also learning about a new culture and improving your language skills.

Accounting and finance play a critical role at the heart of any business. It is impossible to operate in the public and private sectors, including non-profit organisations, without operational funds.



Companies of all shapes and sizes are now being investigated for their financial operations. As a result, qualified and capable accounting experts are in high demand.





What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-

The scope of BCom Accounting and Finance is very broad, and applicants who complete the course will have access to several excellent prospects in a variety of disciplines all over the world. 

Apart from doing additional academic courses like MCom, MBA, CA, and so forth.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Jobs

Description

Personal Finance Consultant

Depending on their financial goals, a Personal Finance Consultant advises clients on taxes, retirement planning, investments, and insurance issues.

Financial Analyst

Financial analysts' responsibilities include tracking a company's financial performance against a plan, analysing business performance and market circumstances to develop projections, and presenting monthly reports to aid senior management make tactical and strategic choices.

Accounts Assistant

Reconciling finance accounts, maintaining spreadsheets, credit control, confirming computations, working with the Accounts system, reconciliation of direct debit mandates, handling petty cash transactions, and so on are all responsibilities of an Accounts Assistant.

Money Manager

Money managers' responsibilities include researching and locating companies, funds, and industries in which they can invest, selecting and advising bonds, stocks, currencies, and other investment commodities for funds, developing investment strategies for individuals and companies, analysing past and current financial data, strategy, policy, and management of companies, and examining industry trends and economic factors such as inflation, unemployment rates, and so on.

Senior Accountant

A senior accountant is in charge of keeping track of an organization's assets, liabilities, profit and loss, tax liability, and other financial activities, as well as investigating budget and variance concerns, analysing financial data, and preparing budget predictions.





What is the entry level salary packages & average salary packages for this course graduates? 



Jobs

Average Salary

Personal Finance Consultant

INR 9,81,627

Financial Analyst

INR 5,85,380

Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?

Here is complete information about each entrance test in India held by various schools and universities to provide admission to BCom Accounting and Finance courses, including general information about the exam:



DU JAT - The Delhi University Joint Admission Test (DU JAT) is an entrance exam for admission to the university's prestigious degrees. Quantitative Aptitude, Verbal Ability, Logical Reasoning, and GK & Business Awareness are the four areas of the DU JAT admission exam. It takes place entirely online.

NMIMS Programs - NPAT The entrance exam for Undergraduate Degree and Integrated Degree Programs takes place after the 12th grade. The exam is held once a year, and you will have two hours to complete all of the questions.

SET - The Symbiosis International University administers the Symbiosis Entrance Test (SET) once a year for admission to undergraduate programmes at the national level. You will have two hours and thirty minutes to complete all of the questions. It's also an online exam. General English, logical reasoning, numeric skills, and general awareness are all part of the SET curriculum.

MHT CET - The Maharashtra common entrance test is held at the state level by the Maharashtra common entrance test. MHT CET is an online exam that takes 90 minutes to complete.

IPU CET - The Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University administers the IPU CET, or Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test, which is a university-level exam.

What is BBA?



BBA stands for Bachelor of Business Administration. This undergraduate degree course helps develop entrepreneurship skills and can be pursued full-time, part-time and as an online course. 



Various BBA Integrated Courses are also available in India which complete over 5 years.

Why should we pursue BBA?



Below we discuss the reasons why people seek a BBA degree after high school.

High salary and benefits

A BBA degree is sure to give you a successful career with excellent benefits. One can bag a high-end professional job with a BBA degree.



Job satisfaction 

A BBA degree promises a job that has more to offer than just a high salary. One can attain personal development goals and expand their network thereby resulting in job satisfaction.



Career options after BBA



BBA opens a myriad of career routes that one might consider for a successful future. Below we discuss some of the career options after BBA.

Specialisation in Management (MBA)

MBA is a 2-year postgraduate degree course focusing on the fundamentals of modern business management. 

For those who want to pursue higher studies, MBA is the most preferred choice after completing BBA. Post-MBA, one can apply for management roles in sales, business development, HR, finance and related departments in companies.



Eligibility for MBA: Bachelor’s degree with a minimum of 50% marks.

Common Admission Test (CAT)

Product Manager 

Blockchain expert

title: BBA Human resource
details:
BBA HUMAN RESOURCE



What is BBA Human Resource ?

BBA Human Resources Management is a three-year undergraduate programme that focuses on the fundamentals needed to run any HR institution or organisation. The course equips students with sufficient information and imparts key skills that will assist them in working professionally.

The BBA Human Resources Management course content is organised into six semesters. Candidates who want to advance their careers in this industry or start their own human resources firms should take this course. Business basics, management, and economic planning and policies are all included in the BBA HRM study syllabus.

The typical annual costs for a BBA Human Resources Management programme vary per college and range from INR 30,000 to 6.0 lakhs.



 

Overview/Highlights of this Course-

 

Course Level

Bachelors

Examination Type

Semester-wise/ Year-wise

Eligibility for the program

10+2 qualified with a good school.

Duration of the program

3 years

Average Tuition Fee

INR 4-7 Lakhs

Average Salary (per annum)

INR 3-5 Lakhs

Job Profiles

HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, IT Recruiter, HR Coordinator, Admin Executive, etc.





Streams/Fields of the this course-

Training and Development

Compensation and Benefits

Recruiting

Organisational Leadership

Financial Management

Why Should you opt for BBA Human resource

Students will benefit from doing a BBA Human Resources Management programme because it is one of the most demanding courses in the near future. The following are some of the advantages:

 Candidates will study a variety of business methods, as well as business and financial management ideas, that will assist them in starting their own company or working in high-profile positions.

One can discover rich and high-profile job possibilities that not only allow them to make a high wage but also allow them to travel for a variety of reasons.

After finishing the course, students will be entitled to pursue an MBA, PGDM, or any other course that would enable them to increase their wage.

In comparison to other degrees, the average pay of a BBA Human Resources Management graduate is fairly high. With a few years of expertise, one can make INR 4-7 per hour, with the potential to develop to INR 30 - 45 per hour.

Career opportunities after graduating from this course:-





Job Profiles

Job Descriptions

Average Annual Salary

Employee Relations Manager

They are in charge of bridging the gap between the employer and the employee and serving as a liaison between the two. Employee concerns and grievances are heard and addressed.

INR 4 - 6 LPA

HR Manager

They are responsible for supervising and managing company's or organisation's processes related to recrutement, hiring, interviewing and building strategic talent resources plans.

INR 7 - 9 LPA

HR Executive

Human resources executives are in charge of monitoring the recruiting and referral procedures. They're also in charge of keeping HR rules up to date, as well as managing and monitoring benefit programmes.

INR 3 - 5.5 LPA

Personnel Manager

They oversee and conduct fundamental managerial tasks such as organising, directing, managing, and planning a company's procedures. They also offer guidance on a variety of topics for the company's advantage and profit.

INR 3 - 20 LPA

Quality Assurance/ Quality Control Manager

They are in charge of managing the quality inspection at a specified level of quality. They also establish strategies to regulate quality for the company's advantage.

INR 11 - 15 LPA

MBA Human resource management

Masters of human resource management

MBA( Masters in business administration)

 

 

What are the entry-level salary packages & average salary packages for  graduates of this course?



Job Profiles

Average Annual Salary

Employee Relations Manager

INR 4 - 6 LPA

HR Manager

INR 7 - 9 LPA

HR Executive

INR 3 - 5.5 LPA

Personnel Manager

INR 3 - 20 LPA

Quality Assurance/ Quality Control Manager

INR 11 - 15 LPA







Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how??

 

NMIMS NPAT

For undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, the Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies (NMIMS) administers the National Test for Programs After Twelfth (NPAT). It is a university-level test that is given for a variety of university programmes.

IPU CET

 It is a university-level entrance exam administered by Indraprastha University for admission to various undergraduate and graduate programmes at IP University.



DU JAT

NTA administers the DU JAT 2022 on behalf of Delhi University for admission to the BMS (Bachelor of Management Studies), BA (Hons) in Business Economics, and BBA programmes (FIA). Each year, the exam is held, and admissions are offered to qualified students depending on their performance in both the entrance exam and the class 12 board exam.

title: BBA INTERNATIONAL BUSINESS
details:
BBA INTERNATIONAL BUSINESS 



What is this course?



The following is information and details regarding the BBA International Business programme.



All commercial actions that take place to facilitate the transfer of products, services, resources, ideas, and technology beyond national geographical boundaries are referred to as international business.

Students will study about several areas of international business while pursuing this degree, such as Global Business Administration, International Business Programs, Buying and Selling Concepts, and so on.



Theoretical and practical understanding of trade and business will be provided to students.

International Marketing, Management of Cross Cultural Issues, Global Supply Chain Management, and other topics are covered in depth in the BBA International Business programme.





Highlights of this course-



Course type

Graduation

Duration

3years

Examination Type

Semester / year wise

Eligibility

10+2 in Commerce Stream with a minimum of 50% marks

Admission Process

Entrance/ Merit-Based





Fields/ Streams of the course-



Marketing Executive.

Sales Executive.

International Brand Manager Business Development Manager.

Human Resources Officer.

Data Analyst.

International Logistics Manager.

Event Manager.

International Export Manager.





Why should you study this Course?



These are the following reasons to pursue this course:



High pay: For both commercial and public enterprises, average packages for BBA International Business graduates are on the upper side.

Opportunities for employment include: A person with an International Business degree can pursue a wide range of professional options. There is no limit to the number of instructional programmes available. The sky's the limit when it comes to PhDs, management positions, and competitive tests for government jobs.

Students who complete this programme may expect to earn salaries ranging from INR 3,00,000 to INR 10,00,000, depending on their level of skill in the subject.

International Human Resources Managers, International Training Managers, International Operations Managers, Global Distribution Managers, and other positions in banks, MNCs, and other organisations will be available to BBA International Business degree graduates.

Candidates can continue their education by pursuing research degrees such as a PhD or a Doctor of Science.







Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profile

Job Role

Export Manager

An Export Manager's job is to manage the export of products. He's also in charge of finances and transactional processes.





International Brand Manager

To optimise sales in the worldwide market, he organises, develops, and implements product marketing.

Global Business Manager

Develop and implement new marketing, budget and spending management, and marketing plans for increased earnings and sales.

International Logistics Manager

In order to achieve service level standards, the International Logistics Manager communicates with foreign vendors to handle import activities.

International Finance Manager

An international finance manager's job includes coordinating financial management functions such as accounting, grant administration, budgeting, and financial forecasting.





What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course- 



The following are the most common educational options after earning a BBA in International Business.



MBA: The majority of BBA degree graduates prefer to continue their education and enrol in a PGDM or MBA programme. A nationwide entrance exam is used to determine admissions. A BBA IB degree combined with an MBA in a chosen concentration is very advantageous, and many companies actively seek such personnel.



What are the entry level salary packages & average salary packages for this course graduates?



Job Profile

Average Annual Salary

Export Manager

INR 6,67,000

International Brand Manager

INR 10,77,000

Global Business Manager

INR 5,00,000

International Logistics Manager

INR 6,18,000

INR 5,16,000





Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?



Admission to the course is normally determined by a candidate's performance on an entrance exam. CUCET, DSAT, BUMAT, and other key admission tests are used for this purpose.

CUCET - The Common University Entrance Test (CUET), formerly known as the Central Universities Common Entrance Test (CUCET), is an all-India test administered by the National Testing Agency for admission to a variety of Undergraduate, Integrated, Postgraduate, Diploma, Certification, and Research Programs.



DSAT - The Dayananda Sagar Admission Test is abbreviated as DSAT. Dayananda Sagar University (DSU) organises it for UG and PG courses under their university. The DSAT is a nationwide admissions test. Candidates can take the entrance exam after meeting the eligibility requirements for the course they want to pursue.

There is no limit to the number of instructional programmes available. The sky's the limit when it comes to PhDs, management positions, and competitive tests for government jobs.

Students who complete this programme may expect to earn salaries ranging from INR 3,00,000 to INR 10,00,000, depending on their level of skill in the subject.

International Human Resources Managers, International Training Managers, International Operations Managers, Global Distribution Managers, and other positions in banks, MNCs, and other organisations will be available to BBA International Business degree graduates.

Candidates can continue their education by pursuing research degrees such as a PhD or a Doctor of Science.

Job Role

An Export Manager's job is to manage the export of products. He's also in charge of finances and transactional processes.





International Brand Manager

To optimise sales in the worldwide market, he organises, develops, and implements product marketing.

Global Business Manager

Develop and implement new marketing, budget and spending management, and marketing plans for increased earnings and sales.

International Logistics Manager

In order to achieve service level standards, the International Logistics Manager communicates with foreign vendors to handle import activities.

International Finance Manager

An international finance manager's job includes coordinating financial management functions such as accounting, grant administration, budgeting, and financial forecasting.

DSAT - The Dayananda Sagar Admission Test is abbreviated as DSAT. Dayananda Sagar University (DSU) organises it for UG and PG courses under their university. The DSAT is a nationwide admissions test. BBA COMPUTER APPLICATION 



What is this course?



BBA Computer Applications is a three-year full-time undergraduate programme that provides advanced understanding in a wide range of computer applications so that students who complete it are prepared to tackle any difficulties or possibilities in the IT business.



The goal of this course is to generate qualified computer professionals, to equip students to tackle a variety of difficulties and possibilities in the IT sector, and to develop competency in a certain business field.



Highlights of this course-



Course Level

Graduation

Duration

3 Years

Eligibility Criteria

10+2 level probably in Commerce Stream with 50% marks from any recognized board of education 

Admission Procedure

Merit Based/ Entrance Test + Counselling Based 





Fields/ Streams of the course-



Technical Specialist.

3D Animation / Graphic Designer.

Software Analyst.

Database Expert.

Software Engineering.

Networking Analyst.

Hardware Expert.

Programmer / Software Developer.



Why should you study this Course?



The objectives and goals of each individual will determine whether or not they pursue a BBA Computer Applications degree. The following are some of the reasons why you should take this course:



A bachelor's degree in business administration with a concentration in computer applications is known as a BBA Computer Applications. Students who pursue this course not only open the door to a career in the IT business, but also to jobs in other areas where they may employ computer applications and new technology.

This bachelor's degree can lead to a variety of employment prospects and work jobs in a variety of industries, including the information technology industry, banking, commercial enterprises, central services, defence, and more.

After successfully completing this course, alumni will be able to apply for possible career openings. They work for companies like Infosys, Wipro, Capgemini, Accenture, Bajaj Capital, HCL Technologies, L&T, IBM, Aon Hewitt, and others as software developers, system programmers, web designers, technical support engineers, network administrators, network analysts, sales executives, test engineers, and more.

They can also work as an assistant professor or research assistant at any reputable institution or research centre. Graduates can make an average yearly income of INR 2,00,000 to INR 8,00,000 per year, depending on their experience and competence.





What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course- 



After completing this programme, BBA Computer Applications graduates might choose to work or pursue further education. The following are the most popular educational options after earning a BBA in Computer Applications.



MBA: The majority of BBA degree graduates prefer to continue their education and enrol in a PGDM or MBA programme. A nationwide entrance exam is used to determine admissions. A BBA degree combined with an MBA in a chosen field is very advantageous, and many companies actively seek such people.



Master of Computer Applications (MCA) is a two-year postgraduate degree that provides advanced expertise in many elements of computer science and its application.



PGDBM: A professional postgraduate diploma programme in business management that lasts two years. The PGDBM programme teaches you all you need to know about business economics, organisational behaviour, marketing management, and more.



Competitive examinations: Graduates also have the option of preparing for competitive examinations. The most popular exams are those for job openings in government-sponsored enterprises. Jobs are safe, with guaranteed high salary and regular raises.



Certificate Courses: After completing this course, students can enrol in Certificate Courses in Management.

title: BBA ACCOUNTING
details:
BBA ACCOUNTING



What is BBA Accounting?





The BBA Accounting programme combines financial and business subjects. Auditing, accounting, and financial reporting are all topics that students may study about.

This course will cover popular methods and tactics for dealing with financial concerns and budgets, such as cost analysis, planning, monitoring, and accumulation.

Tax, Corporate Financial Laws, and Accounting Management will all be covered in the course.



Students will learn how to extrapolate and evaluate data from costs in order to make educated business decisions and system modifications.



The course is aimed to provide students a thorough knowledge of management education and to properly train them in communication skills that will help them become more entrepreneurial and make better decisions.



The focus of the course will be on managerial skills and critical thinking, both of which are important in the corporate world.



Overview of the course



Course level

Undergraduate

Duration

3 years

Examination Type

Semester

Eligibility Criteria

10+2 (Science and Commerce Preferred) 

Admission Process

Merit-Based or Entrance Test

Course Fee

INR 90,000 to INR 5,00,000 

Average Salary

INR 3,00,000 to INR 8,00,000 

Top Recruiting Companies

Genpact, Tata Consultancy Services Limited, WNS Global Services, Wipro Technologies Ltd., Tata Motors, Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL), National Small Industries Corporation Limited (NSIC), Heavy Engineering Corporation Limited (HEC Ltd), etc.

Top Recruiting Areas

Banks, Insurance Industry, Charity Associations, Business Schools, Savings and Loan Associations, Credit Unions, Mortgage Companies, Finance Companies, Consultancies, etc





Streams of this course

Following are the streams of this course

 Budgetary Control

Working Capital Management

Fund Flow Statement

Standard Costing

Variance Analysis

Cash Flow Statement

Marginal Costing

 

Why should you study BBA accounting?





The BBA Accounting degree in Accounting aids in the development of entrepreneurial and business abilities in the field of accounting.

Case studies, workshops, internships, industrial visits, and contact with industry experts are all part of most institutions' BBA programmes, which give preparation and practical experience in the form of case studies, workshops, internships, and contact with industry experts.

It improves the student's practical, organisational, and communication abilities, as well as their capacity to make business judgments.

The correspondence course can be taken full-time or part-time.

The degree qualifies one to work in accounting and financial divisions of various corporations and firms, banks, government departments and agencies, educational institutions, and a variety of other organisations.

Following the BBA, the individual may pursue a Master of Business Administration programme to get more education and managerial experience.

              	 

Career opportunities after graduating BBA accounting :-

 

Job Profile

Job Description

Accounting Associate

Accountants and auditors might use their services for accounting, administrative work, and account management. They contact departments, vendors, and clients to handle overdue payments. Payments, invoices, and other transactions are processed, registered, and paid.

Accountant

They plan assets, liabilities, and capital account entries by collecting and evaluating account facts. They register financial activities and prescribe financial behaviour by analysing accounting possibilities by entering account data.

Accounting Clerk

They help supervisors by performing accounting and clerical responsibilities in the general ledger system, such as recording company transactions and keying daily spreadsheets. Vouchers, invoices, cheques, account statements, reports, and other documents use adding machines, calculators, databases, and bank accounts to enter in data.

Accounts Receivable Clerk

They post customer payments by monitoring cash, checks, and credit card transactions; they post revenue by reviewing and entering transactions from the lockbox and local deposits; and they manage receivables by adding up delinquent bills.

Accounting Firm Partner

They are highly skilled individuals who utilise their accounting and auditing expertise to examine the financial health of a company. Effective auditing duties assist to reflect the openness of accounting transactions and, as a result, serve to improve the reputation of the business.

Audit Manager

The Audit Manager is in charge of arranging and managing internal audits in accordance with the yearly audit schedule, as well as directing a department of auditors.

Forensic Accountant

Forensic accountants have particular accounting knowledge. They study financial accounts of a business while detecting fraud using investigative, auditing, and accounting skills.



 

What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course:-

Following ar

title: BBA ENTREPRENEURSHIP
description: BBA Entrepreneurship is a three-year bachelor's degree programme for undergraduate students.
details:
BBA ENTREPRENEURSHIP





What is this course?



BBA Entrepreneurship is a three-year bachelor's degree programme for undergraduate students.

Most institutions only offer it to students who studied Commerce or Mathematics in high school, however some colleges offer it to students from all subject streams.

The course is designed to help students develop a foundation of independent entrepreneurial thinking by teaching them about the current market environment and all of the skills they'll need to start a firm. 

Students with a family business history can benefit from this course, as it will help them comprehend business operations and growth potential.



Highlights of the course-



Course Level

Graduation

Duration

3 Years

Eligibility

10+2 with a minimum combined score of 50%

Admission Process

Merit based or Entrance based



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Financial planning.

Legal knowledge.

Marketing strategy.

Operating a business.

Sources of capital.

Succession.

Valuation.



Why should you study this Course?



BBA Entrepreneurship opens doors to positions as Business Consultants, Delivery Managers, and New Venture Developers in a variety of industries, including banking, retail, car, advertising, real estate, and many more.

This training equips women and minority entrepreneurs with the skills and knowledge they need to establish a business.

The majority of BBA Entrepreneurship graduates work in the banking industry, real estate, and advertising firms, among other fields, while others decide to start their own business, either manufacturing items or providing a variety of services.

An average compensation for a business analyst or delivery manager ranges from INR 3,00,000 to INR 20,00,00 per year, depending on experience and expertise.

For example, the e-commerce behemoth Amazon pays their business consultants around INR 10 LPA. Thus, a BBA Entrepreneurship is the appropriate career decision for you if you have a creative mind and a desire for leading a team efficiently in times of tight deadlines.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



Graduates of the BBA Entrepreneurship programme can pursue higher education, such as an MBA in Finance or an MBA in Entrepreneurship, at a national or foreign university of their choice. 

Gathering industrial experience and then completing more education for a higher career position and compensation benefits is usually useful.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Position

Job Description

Business Consultant

These are professionals who work closely with customers to build the business model, methodology, strategic decision-making, marketing plans, and problem-solving strategies, allowing the customer to develop business abilities and expertise.

Sales Manager

These are persons that are in charge of leading and guiding a team of salespeople, which involves hiring, assigning sales training, mentoring, creating targets, and constructing business designs.

Commercial Banker

Individuals who work for banks or financial institutions are responsible for obtaining new clients, keeping excellent connections with existing clients, as well as originating, negotiating, and completing commercial loans.

Finance Controller

These are the people who keep track of accounting data, assess and manage risk, publish financial statements, keep track of spending, and supervise accounting activities.

Small Business Owners

Their main responsibilities include managing the day-to-day operations of the business, enrolling people to help with financing, and publicising their brand.

course graduates?

BUMAT : The Bharati Vidyapeeth Deemed University's Bharati Vidyapeeth Undergraduate Management Aptitude Test (BUMAT) is a national-level entrance exam. The exam is for candidates seeking admission to the university's and its affiliated colleges' BBA and BCA programmes.

These are professionals who work closely with customers to build the business model, methodology, strategic decision-making, marketing plans, and problem-solving strategies, allowing the customer to develop business abilities and expertise.

Sales Manager

These are persons that are in charge of leading and guiding a team of salespeople, which involves hiring, assigning sales training, mentoring, creating targets, and constructing business designs.

Commercial Banker

Individuals who work for banks or financial institutions are responsible for obtaining new clients, keeping excellent connections with existing clients, as well as originating, negotiating, and completing commercial loans.

Finance Controller

These are the people who keep track of accounting data, assess and manage risk, publish financial statements, keep track of spending, and supervise accounting activities.

Small Business Owners

Their main responsibilities include managing the day-to-day operations of the business, enrolling people to help with financing, and publicising their brand.

title: BBA FINANCE
description: The goal of the BBA Finance course is to provide students with practical and theoretical knowledge of various financial components such as investing, insurance, and so on.
details:
BBA FINANCE





What is this course?



The goal of the BBA Finance course is to provide students with practical and theoretical knowledge of various financial components such as investing, insurance, and so on.

Students in the BBA Finance programme are expected to make well-informed predictions and projections in the areas of investment and insurance.

The program's goal is to help pupils have a better understanding of money and acquire skills to deal with money challenges.



Highlights of the course-



Duration of the course

3 years

Eligibility

10+2 with minimum 50% marks

Stream

Management

Average course fee

INR 2 to 5 Lakhs 



Fields/ Streams of the course-

Finance Manager.

Financial Analyst.

Investment Banker.

Banker.

Loan Officer.

Credit Analyst.

Financial Planner.

Financial Consultant





Why should you study this Course?



During company tours, summer entry-level positions, introductions, and case studies, you will have numerous opportunities to apply what you have learned. Management, privately-held company, accounting, global business, human resources, marketing, and business are examples of specialties.

Finance disciplines in BBA are particularly market-oriented, therefore students who graduate with a Bachelor of Business Administration receive good BBA jobs with an average pay of INR 2,50,000 to 10,00,000.



Career opportunities after this Course-



Risk management, retail, private company, insurance management, finance, treasury, sales and trade, and the stock market are all options for financial management graduates.



The following are some of the major job opportunities that BBA Finance can lead to:

Financial Manager/Analyst

Business Analyst

Credit Analyst 

Operation Manager

Accounting Manager 

Risk and Insurance Manager

Treasurer

Finance Manager 

Cash Manager

Finance Director

Financial Advisors

Finance Associates 



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



You have the option of continuing your education by obtaining an MBA or even a PhD. 

Other specialization programmes are also available. 

Students can also seek careers in CA or train for government positions such as civil service.

title: BBA GLOBAL BUSINESS
description: The following is information and details regarding the BBA Global Business programme:
details:
BBA GLOBAL BUSINESS





What is this course?



The following is information and details regarding the BBA Global Business programme:



All commercial actions that promote the flow of commodities, services, resources, ideas, and technologies across national geographical boundaries are referred to as global business.

Students will study about several areas of international business while pursuing this degree, such as Global Business Administration, International Business Programs, Buying and Selling Concepts, and so on.

Theoretical and practical understanding of trading and business will be provided to students.

International Marketing, Management of Cross Cultural Issues, Global Supply Chain Management, and other topics are covered in depth in the BBA Global Business programme.



Highlights of the course-



Course type

Graduation

Duration

3years

Examination Type

Semester / year wise

Eligibility

10+2 in Commerce Stream with a minimum of 50% marks

Admission Process

Entrance/ Merit-Based



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Marketing Executive.

Sales Executive.

International Brand Manager Business Development Manager.

Human Resources Officer.

Data Analyst.

International Logistics Manager.

Event Manager.

International Export Manager.





Why should you study this Course?

The objectives and goals of each individual will determine whether or not they pursue a BBA International Business degree. The following are some of the reasons why you should take this course:



High pay: For both commercial and public enterprises, average packages for BBA International Business graduates are on the upper side.

Opportunities for employment include: A person with an International Business degree can pursue a wide range of professional options. There is no limit to the number of instructional programmes available. The sky's the limit when it comes to PhDs, management positions, and competitive examinations for government jobs.

Students who complete this programme can expect to earn salaries ranging from INR 3,00,000 to INR 10,00,000, depending on their level of skill in the subject.

International Human Resources Managers, International Training Managers, International Operations Managers, Global Distribution Managers, and other positions in banks, MNCs, and other organizations will be available to BBA International Business degree graduates.

Candidates can continue their education by pursuing research degrees such as a PhD or a Doctor of Science.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



After completing this programme, BBA International Business degree holders might choose to work or continue their education. The following are the most popular educational options after earning a BBA in International Business:



MBA: The majority of BBA degree holders prefer to continue their education and enroll in a PGDM or MBA programme. A national entrance exam is used to determine admissions. A BBA IB degree combined with an MBA in a chosen concentration is highly advantageous, and many companies actively seek such personnel.

Competitive examinations: Graduates also have the option of preparing for competitive examinations. The most popular exams are those for job openings in government-sponsored organisations.Jobs are safe, with guaranteed high salary and regular raises.

Certificate Courses: After completing this course, students can enroll in Certificate Courses in Management.



Career opportunities after this Course-





Job Profile

Job Role

Export Manager

The duty of an Export Manager is to oversee the export of goods. He's also in charge of finances and business transactions.

International Brand Manager

To optimize sales in the worldwide market, he organizes, develops, and implements product marketing.

Global Business Manager

Develop and implement new marketing, budget and spending management, and marketing plans for increased earnings and sales.

International Logistics Manager

In order to achieve service level standards, the International Logistics Manager communicates with overseas vendors to handle import activities.

International Finance Manager

An international finance manager's job entails coordinating financial management functions such as accounting, grant administration, budgeting, and financial forecasting.

title: BBA HOSPITALITY
description: The Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in Hospitality and Tourism is a three-year programme.It's a full-time dual-specialization programme for people who want to work in both hospitality.
eligibility: Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?
details:
BBA HOSPITALITY







What is this course?



The Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in Hospitality and Tourism is a three-year programme.It's a full-time dual-specialization programme for people who want to work in both hospitality.

BBA Hospitality has a wide range of professional opportunities and job opportunities. Graduates of this programme have a wide range of work opportunities in the public and commercial sectors, including international business, market research, retail, hospitality, healthcare, aviation, travel and tour company, event management, and many other fields.



Highlights of the course-



Course Level

Undergraduate

Full form

Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in Hospitality is a bachelor's degree in business administration that focuses on the hospitality industry.

Duration

3 Years

Examination type

Semester-Basis



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Hotel Manager

Banquet Manager

Chef

Steward



Why should you study this Course?



Hospitality is a booming industry that is one of the fastest expanding in the world today. It is currently the world's largest contributor to the economy. Because of the large market potential, hospitality has become one of the most popular career choices among Indian students.

Successful graduates will have a wealth of work options in a variety of areas, including Tourism Departments, Airlines, Hotels, marketing management, retail, tourism, hospitality, healthcare, Tour operators, Meeting & Events Personnel, and so on.

Reasonable Starting Pay: BBA in Hospitality graduates are typically awarded a decent placement or wage package that ranges between INR 2-6 LPA, which is a good starting salary for new graduates.

Higher Education Options: Graduates might pursue higher education to advance their careers, increase the number of professional options available to them, Master of Business Administration in Hospitality Management, and other courses are available to them.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-



Courses such as the Master of Business Administration in Hospitality Management are available to them. The following are brief descriptions of the courses:

Master of Business Administration in Hospitality Management: This programme is for students who want to continue their study in the same sector. It's a two-year programme that teaches you how to apply what you've learned in the classroom to the field of management and hospitality, increasing your chances of getting a better job.

Competitive Exams: If you want to alter your subject of study, you can take a variety of competitive exams for government jobs and civil service positions.



Career opportunities after this Course-









Job Position

Job Description

Guest Service Executive

When visitors arrive, they are greeted, rooms and suites are assigned, keys are issued, and payment information is collected. To address the needs of guests, Guest Service Executives coordinate housekeeping, porters, transportation providers, and kitchen personnel.

Front Desk Officer

The Front Desk Officer is responsible for all receptionist and secretarial activities at our main entrance desk, including answering queries and addressing complaints, monitoring office supplies and placing orders as needed, and so on.

Housekeeping Manager

Housekeeping Managers operate in hotels, resorts, lodges, and inns to ensure that guests are comfortable. They are in charge of cleaning rooms, laundry bed linens, maintaining furnishings, and ensuring that guest rooms and public areas are clean and well-presented.

Food and Beverages Service Manager

In various hotels, resorts, lodges, and inns, the role entails planning, predicting, and executing food and beverage orders, patiently addressing client complaints, and alternative recipes for customers with special dietary demands.



What is the entry level salary packages & average salary packages for this

course graduates?



Job Position

Average Annual Salary

Guest Service Executive

INR 2,50,000

Front Desk Officer

INR 1,90,000

Housekeeping Manager

INR 7, 30,000

Food and Beverages Service Manager

INR 5,39,000



Want to take admission in this course of the best college but how?



For entrance to the course, the university administers the LPUNEST. It's a 2.5-hour online exam. The event is usually held in February or March.



LPUNEST: Lovely Professional University National Entrance and Scholarship Test is abbreviated as LPUNEST. Every year, it is held in an online format for admission to more than 150 programmes. The test serves as both an entrance and a scholarship test.

Singhania University, Rajast

title: BBA HR MANAGEMENT
description: BBA Human Resources Management is a three-year undergraduate programme that covers the fundamentals of managing human resources institutions and organizations. The course equips students with sufficient knowledge and imparts key skills that will assist them in working professionally.
details:
BBA HR MANAGEMENT





What is this course?



BBA Human Resources Management is a three-year undergraduate programme that covers the fundamentals of managing human resources institutions and organizations. The course equips students with sufficient knowledge and imparts key skills that will assist them in working professionally.

The BBA Human Resources Management curriculum is broken down into six semesters. This course is for candidates who desire to enhance their careers in this field or create their own Human Resources businesses. The BBA HRM course programme covers business fundamentals, management, and economic planning and policies.



Highlights of the course-



Course Level

Bachelors

Examination Type

Semester-wise/ Year-wise

Eligibility for the program

Exams from a recognised board for 10+2

Duration of the program

3 years



Fields/ Streams of the course-



Compensation and Benefits Manager 

Recruitment and Placement Manager

HR Consultant

Executive Recruiter

International HR Associate

Employee Relations Specialist 

HR Director

Vice President of Human Resources



Why should you study this Course?



Students will benefit from doing a BBA Human Resources Management programme because it is one of the most demanding courses in the near future. The following are some of the advantages:



Candidates will learn a variety of business methods, as well as business and financial management ideas, that will assist them in starting their own company or working in high-profile positions.

One can find rich and high-profile job opportunities that not only allow them to earn a high wage but also allow them to travel for a variety of reasons.

With a few years of experience, one can make INR 4-7 LPA , with the potential to develop to INR 30 - 45 LPA.







Career opportunities after this Course-



Job Profiles

Job Descriptions

Employee Relations Manager

They are in charge of bridging the gap between the employer and the employee and serving as a liaison between the two. Employee complaints and grievances are heard and addressed.

HR Manager

They are in charge of supervising and managing the procedures of a firm or organization's recruitment, hiring, interviewing, and developing strategic personnel resource plans.

HR Executive

Human resources executives are in charge of monitoring the recruiting and referral processes. They're also in charge of keeping HR rules up to date, as well as managing and monitoring benefit programmes.

Personnel Manager

They oversee and conduct basic managerial tasks such as organizing, directing, managing, and planning a company's procedures. They also offer guidance on a variety of topics for the company's advantage and profit.

Quality Assurance/ Quality Control Manager

They are in charge of managing the quality inspection at a specified level of quality. They also establish plans to control quality for the company's advantage.



What we can do in further/Higher after studying this course-

The BBA in Human Resources Management opens the door to a variety of career-building and money-making opportunities. After completing the course, students have the option of working or continuing their studies in the same sector. Following are some common courses for higher study following BBA Human Resources Management:



Human Resource Management (MBA)



MBA Human Resources Management is a two-year postgraduate programme that focuses on Marketing Management, Quantitative Techniques, and other Human Resources Management-related topics.

To be eligible for an MBA in Human Resources Management, students must have completed their undergraduate studies with a minimum of 50% in any subject from a recognised university.

Admissions to MBA Human Resources Management programmes are based on the results of entrance exams such as the CAT, XAT, and others, followed by a GD/PI round.

course graduates?
